recognize_series.description:
When true, the pipeline attempts to group related incoming files into a
series. Default false.

recognize_version.description:
When true, the pipeline attempts to detect that an incoming file is a
new version of an existing asset. Default false.

update_search_field.description:
Optional. Full API name of the field used to look up an existing object
when `update_mode` is `update` or `upsert` (ignored for `insert`). Must
be a lookup-supported field or the literal `_global_object_id`; fields
inside a reverse-nested structure are rejected.

update_mode.description:
Whether each processed file CREATES a new object or UPDATES an existing
one. Empty/absent is normalized to `insert`; any value outside the enum
fails with "Unknown update mode <mode>".
  - `insert`: always create a brand-new object; no existing-object
    lookup. `update_search_field` is ignored and `update_policy` is
    forced to empty.
  - `update`: locate an existing object via `update_search_field` and
    update it; does not create when none is found.
  - `upsert`: locate an existing object and update it, otherwise insert
    a new one.
For `update`/`upsert`, `update_policy` resolves an asset collision on
`eas_field`.

update_policy.description:
How to resolve the uploaded asset against an EXISTING asset in
`eas_field` when `update_mode` is `update` or `upsert`. An unknown value
fails with "Unknown update policy <policy>".
  - `create_version`: add the new file as a new version of the existing
    asset.
  - `replace`: overwrite the existing asset with the new file.
  - `create_version_preferred`: add as a new version and mark it the
    preferred one.
  - `refuse`: reject the collision; do not write.
Mode-dependent normalization:
  - `update_mode=insert`: this field is ignored and forced to empty;
    only the four values above (or empty) are accepted as input.
  - `update`/`upsert` with `eas_field` inside a NESTED field: forced to
    `create_version` (only create_version is meaningful for nested file
    fields).
  - `update`/`upsert`, non-nested: one of the four enum values is
    required (empty is NOT accepted).

tags.description:
Tags to set for the created main object. The object also receives all
default tags with display type "hidden". Created linked objects get the
default tags only, not these configured ones.

plugin.description:
Optional. Plugin callback that post-processes each uploaded file, in the
strict format `<pluginName>:<callbackName>` (the colon and callback are
mandatory). `callbackName` must start with a lowercase letter and use
only `[a-z_0-9]`. The plugin must exist and declare a `collection_upload`
callback of that name in its manifest; otherwise the request fails
(wrong format / unknown plugin / plugin not found / unknown
collection_upload). Example: `fylr_example:filename_copy`.

plugin_config.description:
Optional, plugin-specific configuration passed through to the named
`plugin` callback. Opaque to the collection pipeline: it is typed only as
a nested map `{ <configName>: { <paramName>: <value> } }` and is NOT
interpreted by core. Keys and value schemas are defined by the chosen
plugin's `collection_upload` config section in its manifest; unknown
config names or parameters are warn-logged and IGNORED (not rejected),
and parameter values are validated only by the plugin's own param types.
It is free-form here because the valid keys depend entirely on the
plugin manifest — consult that plugin's documentation. Only meaningful
when `plugin` is set.

_generated_rights.description: Object which lists the compiled permissions for the current user. Not all permissions are present in all contexts.

_has_acl.description: Flag that indicates that this collection has an active `_acl` (even if inherited).

_has_children.description:
Flag that inidicates if this collection has children. This flag is indpendent of the actual permissions of
the user for that collection. So, if the user cannot access the only sub collection of this collection, the
`_has_children` flag will still be set.

_has_remote_objects.description: Flag that inidicates if this collection contains at least one foreign collection object.

_level.description:
Starting with 1, `_level` states the depth of the collection in the collection tree. The top level from the user's
perspective is 3. The 1st level is the root collection, the 2nd level the user's top level collection which is a system
collection and automatically created to hold all the user's collections underneath.

items.description: List of permissions.

_id.description:
Auto assigned `_id` to indicate the permission. Permissions can be
updated by re-using the `_id`.

_owner.description: Available only in some contexts, this flag indicated _the owner_ of an object.

_pool_contact.description: Available only in some contexts, this flag indicates _the contact of a pool_ of an object.

_version.description: The version of the user record.

_generated_displayname.description:
System generated displayname. This is the user's `displayname`,
`last_name` & `first_name`, `login` or `email`. If none of these is
set, the `_id` is used.

login.description: Set for users of type `collection` or `email`.

frontend_language.description: The preferred frontend language of the user (BCP 47)

_generated_displayname.description:
System generated displayname. This is the groups's `displayname`,
`name` or `reference`. Rendered in the frontend language of the
current user. If none of these is set, the `_id` is used.

sticky.description:
Flag to indicate that this permission persist even if subordinate
permissions use the `_private_acl` flag to clear the list of permissions
inherited from their parent. This is useful to add permissions which
cannot be taken away from sub collections.

active.description:
Flag to indicate if this permission is active. It can be used to
temporarily disable permissions without losing details of the
permission.

_right_preset.description:
The preset used for this permission. If set, no permissions need to be
set in this entry, permissions are taken from the given preset.

from.description:
If set, the permission is only valid from this date on. It can be
given in ISO 8601 format, so e.g. "2025" sets the beginning of the
year 2025. Dates are parsed as UTC if not specified with time zone.

rights.description: Object which carries the permissions granted by this item.

additionalProperties.description: Depending on the context, only some properties can be used to define a right. The key is the name of permission.

_grantable.description: Flag to indicate that this permission can be shared in a collection ACL.

allow_update_and_remove.description: Flag to allow to update or remove a file id from a column.

classes.description: List of classes this permission if given for. An empty list matches all.

extensions.description: List of extensions this permission if given for. An empty list matches all.

versions.description: List of version names (renditions) this permission if given for. An empty list matches all.

limit.description: Byte size as string. For formats refer to (go-datasize](https://github.com/programmfabrik/go-datasize).

additionalProperties.description: The key is the object type ID.

oneOf.description: Set to `standard` for the standard mask.
The mask id given the permission for.

column_ids.description: List of column api ids to apply this permission to.

objecttype_ids.description: List of object type ids to apply this permission to.

pool_ids.description: List of pool IDs to apply this permission to.

tagfilter.description:
The permission can be filtered by tags. Depending on the context, the
tags of an object influence the permission. `any`, `all` and `not` are
matched in an `AND` fashion.

any.description: List of tag ids. Any one of the tags must set to match this condition.

all.description: List of tag ids. All of the tags must be set to match this condition.

not.description: List of tag ids. None of the tags must be set to match this condition.

send_email_notification.description:
If set and newly created, this write-only property is used to send an
email with sharing information to all users of this ACL item.

text.description: The text included in the email.

_private_acl.description:
Flag which indicates a private ACL. With this setting ACL from the parent are ignored (except for the items
which set `sticky`.

_path.description:
Array of parent collections. A minimal record is available for all parent collections. This includes collections
the user has not direct permission to access. Frontends can use the path to build a tree view of all collections.

_invalid_acl.description:
Collection can share permissions to other users. The owner of the
collection must carry the grantable permission for each object in the
collection in order to share the collection. **fylr** determines for the
whole collection if sharing is allowed or not. If it is not allowed, the
collection will be put in `_invalid_acl: true` state which disables the
ACL attached to the collection.

_owner.description:
The owner of the collection. This is set to the user of the parent collection.
Mismatching owners will result in an API error. Only users are allowed, no groups.
Available only in some contexts, this flag indicated _the owner_ of an object.

_pool_contact.description: Available only in some contexts, this flag indicates _the contact of a pool_ of an object.

_version.description: The version of the user record.

_generated_displayname.description:
System generated displayname. This is the user's `displayname`,
`last_name` & `first_name`, `login` or `email`. If none of these is
set, the `_id` is used.

login.description: Set for users of type `collection` or `email`.

frontend_language.description: The preferred frontend language of the user (BCP 47)

_generated_displayname.description:
System generated displayname. This is the groups's `displayname`,
`name` or `reference`. Rendered in the frontend language of the
current user. If none of these is set, the `_id` is used.

objects.description: Objects linked in this collection. This is only supported as writable property for PUT /collection.

_global_object_id.description:
The object id identifying the object. This is considered a local object if has
the format `<system object id>@local` or `<system object id>@<db uuid>`. All other ids
are considered foreign and accepted without further checks. Local ids are checked
for existance.

webfrontend_props.description:
An object to store custom data for collections. Frontends are advised
to use their own top level property here and put all custom data
underneath. Other top level data should be stored back as is. The
server applies no processing to that data.

lookup:_global_object_id.description:
Object lookups can use `_system_object_id` or `_uuid` to find existing objects. If objects are promised to
be injected into **fylr**, `_allow_defer` can be set. Custom columns can also be used to find an object.

_hotfolder_upload_urls.description: A list of URLs to upload files into this collection.

type.description:
The type `webdav` indicates the enabled read & write WebDAV access. The type `webdav-hotfolder``
is a writable WebDAV access which allows the user to drop files into the collection.

_has_pin.description:
This flag indicates that the collection has a pin code. The actual pin code is only visible
to `system.root` users and users with the `BAG_ACL`.

pin_ok.description:
Flag to indicate that the pin was entered correctly for the current user. `system.root` and
all users with the `BAG_ACL` permission will always see `pin_ok`.

_created_at.description: Timestamp when this collection was created.

_updated_at.description: Timestamp when this collection was updated.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks a required `BAG_*` permission on the
collection (or a required system right). See `code` for the
specific reason.

404.description: The collection addressed by the URL does not exist.

post.summary: Update collection.
post.description:
* The user needs `BAG_WRITE` to update a collection.
* Moving a collection to a new parent requires `BAG_CREATE` in the new parent and `BAG_ACL` in the current collection.
  When moving a collection, the owner is automatically set to the owner of the parent. This includes all child collections.
* New ACL items with `send_email_notification` will sent out and email.
* To set `_private_acl` or `_acl`the user needs `BAG_ACL`.
* Adding or editing a shared right is validated against the owner's grantable rights; a share the owner cannot grant returns `202` (see below) unless `allow_invalid_acl=true`.
* `COLLECTION_UPDATE` event is written.

parameters.name: x-fylr-authorization
parameters.description:
The access token in form of `Bearer <token>`. You can also use `authorization`
which does the same. `authorization` is looked at first.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: The access token in form of `Bearer <token>`.

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: The collection id of the collection to access.

parameters.name: allow_invalid_acl
parameters.description:
Allow storing the collection even if the owner doesn't have the necessary
permissions for all objects to share the rights defined in the `_acl` property
of the collection. The `_invalid_acl` flag will be set, if the user doesn't
have sufficient permissions to share all objects of the collection. The user
needs the permission `BAG_ACL` for the collection in order to use this
parameter.

Differs from easydb 5: this confirmation is driven by the boolean
`allow_invalid_acl` parameter. easydb 5 instead resolves the `202`
confirmation with a `collection_rights_policy` query parameter; there is no
`allow_invalid_acl` parameter in easydb 5.

parameters.name: background_invalid_acl
parameters.description:
If set, the invalid-ACL validity check (whether the collection owner may
grant the rights of all linked objects) is not run inline. Instead a queued
`ACTION_COLLECTION_ACL_CHECK` job is scheduled and the request returns
immediately, before the check has resolved.

_id.description: The `_id` is issued from the fylr "sequence" table when a collection is created.

_id_parent.description:
The `_id_parent` can be set to link a collection as sub collection to
another. The `_owner` if a child collection will always be set to the
owner of the parent collection.

_version.description:
The `_version` of the collection created or updated. New collections
must be inserted using version _1_. Version updates must provide the
current version + 1. The server rejects updates / insert if the
version doesn't match the expected version.

children_allowed.description:
Flag to indicate if this collection can have children or not. This
is to keep compability with easydb 5 and has no effect in **fylr**.

objects_allowed.description:
Flag to indicate if this collection can have link objects or not.
This is to keep compability with easydb 5 and has no effect in
**fylr**.

is_system_collection.description:
Flag to inidicate that this collection is a system collection. A
system collection cannot be deleted.

webfrontend_props.description:
An object to store custom data for collections. Frontends are advised
to use their own top level property here and put all custom data
underneath. Other top level data should be stored back as is. The
server applies no processing to that data.

displayname.description: The displayname of the collection is used as primary label for the user when this collection is displayed.

description.description: The description of the collection is used as a more detail information block when presenting the collection.

create_object.description:
If the collection is used to create objects, like with `/api/db?collection=ID` or via WebDAV, the objects need some
basic information so the records can be created. This object defines these basics. The `_compiled_created_object`
can be used to access the information from the first parent which has this property set. There is no possibility to
remove this setting on a lower level once the parent collection has set it.

objecttype.description:
Technical name of the target objecttype the pipeline writes into.
Must reference an existing objecttype (resolved via
`datamodel.TableByName`); an unknown name fails with
"Objecttype not found". De-facto required: an empty value disables the
entire create-object rule (ApiToObject returns nil, nil).

mask_id.description:
API id of the mask, scoped to `objecttype`, through which the
new/updated object is written. Must be an existing mask api-id on that
objecttype, otherwise: "Mask <id> not found in objecttype <name>".

pool_id.description:
Optional. Pool the main object is filed into. Required only when the
resolved `eas_field` path crosses a pool-managed objecttype; supplying
it when not needed fails with "Pool not needed for <field>", omitting it
when needed fails with "Pool required for <field>".

linked_pool_id.description:
Optional. Pool for an asset that lives inside a linked object. Allowed
only when `eas_field` is inside a link mask and that linked objecttype
is pool-managed; otherwise rejected.

pool.description:
Full pool descriptor — only used by the backup / restore pipeline so it
can recreate a referenced pool that doesn't exist on the target
instance. Regular callers should set `pool_id` and leave this empty.

linked_pool.description:
Same as `pool` but for the linked-object pool — only used by
backup / restore.

eas_field.description:
Full API name (relative to `objecttype`; the `<objecttype>.` prefix is
added internally) of the field that receives the uploaded asset. The
referenced column MUST be of type `files`, otherwise:
'eas_field <name> needs to be type "file" but is <type>'. An unknown
field fails with 'eas_field <name> not found in objecttype <name>'.
Linked-object fields are allowed. Optional for rules that attach no
file; when set it also drives pool-requirement detection and the
`update_policy` nesting check.

linked_object_pools.description:
Per-pool overrides for linked-object pools, keyed by the linked
object's field API name (without the objecttype prefix, added
internally). Each key must resolve to a link column whose target table
is pool-managed; otherwise "linked_object_field <name> not found" or
"has no pool management". Each value is the abbreviated pool descriptor
(id, name, path).

hierarchy_linked_object.description: Optional. Fills a linked-object field with hierarchy data. May be null.

field.description:
Full API name of the linked-object field to fill. Constraints
(enforced in Init): the field must exist with a column, the column
must be a text type, the target objecttype must be hierarchical, the
field must not be inside a reverse-nested structure, and its link
parent path may be at most depth 2 (no deep nesting).

mapping.description:
Selects the field mapping used to interpret incoming file metadata.
Accepted: the string `standard` (built-in standard mapping), the string
`none` (no mapping), an integer mapping id, or null/absent (no mapping).
Any other value: 'Mapping "<v>" not accepted. Allowed: "standard",
"none", <mapping-id>'.

mapping_recipe_configs.description:
Per-recipe configuration overrides. Allowed ONLY when `mapping` is a
specific integer mapping id; setting it otherwise fails with
"mapping recipe configs can only be set for a specific mapping".
(Go json key is `mapping_recipe_configs`, plural.)

allOf.description:
Per-recipe configuration. This object is a map whose keys are **recipe
names** and whose values configure that recipe for the current operation
(e.g. producing a version or running a metadata mapping).

Recipe name format:
  * Built-in / config cookbooks: `COOKBOOK-NAME:RECIPE-NAME`
    (for example `imageconverter:browserthumbs`).
  * Plugin-provided recipes: `PLUGIN-NAME:COOKBOOK-NAME:RECIPE-NAME`.

The `params` of each recipe are **recipe-specific**: the set of valid
parameter keys, their value types, defaults, ranges and option lists are
defined by each recipe individually, not by this API schema. That is why
`params` is documented as an open object (`additionalProperties: true`)
rather than a fixed schema — a closed schema cannot describe every recipe,
and the available recipes additionally depend on which plugins are
installed.

To discover the parameters a recipe accepts on a running instance, use the
recipe listing endpoint:

    GET /inspect/apidocs/recipes/

It lists every cookbook and recipe currently loaded (including
plugin-provided ones) together with each parameter's key, description and
type. Each value you send is validated against that recipe's parameter
definition: unknown keys are rejected; a `string` param may be regex-
constrained; an `int` param is bounded by its `min`/`max`; a `select`
param must be one of the recipe's options; a `bool` param accepts a
boolean or a string (`true`/`1`/`yes` mean true). Omitting a key (or
sending `null`) applies the recipe's
default; a parameter marked mandatory by the recipe must be supplied if it
has no default.

additionalProperties.description:
Configuration for a single recipe, keyed in the parent map by the recipe
name (see the parent description for the name format).

params.description:
Recipe-specific parameters as a map of parameter name to value.

The accepted keys and value types are defined by the recipe itself
and are listed, per recipe, at `GET /inspect/apidocs/recipes/`.
Validation rules applied per parameter, depending on the recipe's
declared parameter type:
  * string  — value must be a string; may be constrained by a regex.
  * int     — value must be an integer (a numeric string is also
              accepted); bounded by the parameter's `min`/`max`.
  * select  — value must be a string equal to one of the parameter's
              allowed options.
  * bool    — value must be a boolean (a string is also accepted:
              `true`/`1`/`yes` mean true, anything else false).
An absent key, or a `null` value, applies the recipe's default. A
parameter the recipe marks as mandatory must be provided unless it has
a default. Keys not defined by the recipe are rejected.

recognize_series.description:
When true, the pipeline attempts to group related incoming files into a
series. Default false.

recognize_version.description:
When true, the pipeline attempts to detect that an incoming file is a
new version of an existing asset. Default false.

update_search_field.description:
Optional. Full API name of the field used to look up an existing object
when `update_mode` is `update` or `upsert` (ignored for `insert`). Must
be a lookup-supported field or the literal `_global_object_id`; fields
inside a reverse-nested structure are rejected.

update_mode.description:
Whether each processed file CREATES a new object or UPDATES an existing
one. Empty/absent is normalized to `insert`; any value outside the enum
fails with "Unknown update mode <mode>".
  - `insert`: always create a brand-new object; no existing-object
    lookup. `update_search_field` is ignored and `update_policy` is
    forced to empty.
  - `update`: locate an existing object via `update_search_field` and
    update it; does not create when none is found.
  - `upsert`: locate an existing object and update it, otherwise insert
    a new one.
For `update`/`upsert`, `update_policy` resolves an asset collision on
`eas_field`.

update_policy.description:
How to resolve the uploaded asset against an EXISTING asset in
`eas_field` when `update_mode` is `update` or `upsert`. An unknown value
fails with "Unknown update policy <policy>".
  - `create_version`: add the new file as a new version of the existing
    asset.
  - `replace`: overwrite the existing asset with the new file.
  - `create_version_preferred`: add as a new version and mark it the
    preferred one.
  - `refuse`: reject the collision; do not write.
Mode-dependent normalization:
  - `update_mode=insert`: this field is ignored and forced to empty;
    only the four values above (or empty) are accepted as input.
  - `update`/`upsert` with `eas_field` inside a NESTED field: forced to
    `create_version` (only create_version is meaningful for nested file
    fields).
  - `update`/`upsert`, non-nested: one of the four enum values is
    required (empty is NOT accepted).

tags.description:
Tags to set for the created main object. The object also receives all
default tags with display type "hidden". Created linked objects get the
default tags only, not these configured ones.

plugin.description:
Optional. Plugin callback that post-processes each uploaded file, in the
strict format `<pluginName>:<callbackName>` (the colon and callback are
mandatory). `callbackName` must start with a lowercase letter and use
only `[a-z_0-9]`. The plugin must exist and declare a `collection_upload`
callback of that name in its manifest; otherwise the request fails
(wrong format / unknown plugin / plugin not found / unknown
collection_upload). Example: `fylr_example:filename_copy`.

plugin_config.description:
Optional, plugin-specific configuration passed through to the named
`plugin` callback. Opaque to the collection pipeline: it is typed only as
a nested map `{ <configName>: { <paramName>: <value> } }` and is NOT
interpreted by core. Keys and value schemas are defined by the chosen
plugin's `collection_upload` config section in its manifest; unknown
config names or parameters are warn-logged and IGNORED (not rejected),
and parameter values are validated only by the plugin's own param types.
It is free-form here because the valid keys depend entirely on the
plugin manifest — consult that plugin's documentation. Only meaningful
when `plugin` is set.

reference.description:
A unique string to set a collection reference. This can be used in collection lookups and in the search. The
server will create a root rollection with the reference `system:root`. Underneath the root collection, each user
gets a top level collection for his collection. This will have a reference starting with `user:ref:<login|reference>`.

shortname.description: A unique string to set a collection shortname. This can be used for search.

type.description:
Usually set to `workfolder` or `search`, this property has currently no effect in **fylr**. Only for the backup,
`search` is used to replace ids in saved searches.

pin_code.description:
For sharing, a collection can be protected with a pin code. This pin code must be entered by the user and saved
via `/api/user` in order to access the objects in the collection.

uuid.description:
A unique ID for the collection. This can be set for new collections or will be assigned by the server.
The UUID of the collection to access it via WebDAV.

lookup:_id.description:
The lookup can be used to find collections by reference. When this is used the `_version` is automatically
set to the current version + 1.

reference.description: The reference of the collection to lookup.

lookup:_id_parent.description: The lookup to find the parent collection.

reference.description: The reference of the collection to lookup.

_count.description: The number of objects in this collection.

_count_recursive.description: The number of objects in this collection and and all descendant collections (recursive).

_create_object_compiled.description: The `collection.create_object` setting for this collection (if set) or the first parent collection which has it.`

objecttype.description:
Technical name of the target objecttype the pipeline writes into.
Must reference an existing objecttype (resolved via
`datamodel.TableByName`); an unknown name fails with
"Objecttype not found". De-facto required: an empty value disables the
entire create-object rule (ApiToObject returns nil, nil).

mask_id.description:
API id of the mask, scoped to `objecttype`, through which the
new/updated object is written. Must be an existing mask api-id on that
objecttype, otherwise: "Mask <id> not found in objecttype <name>".

pool_id.description:
Optional. Pool the main object is filed into. Required only when the
resolved `eas_field` path crosses a pool-managed objecttype; supplying
it when not needed fails with "Pool not needed for <field>", omitting it
when needed fails with "Pool required for <field>".

linked_pool_id.description:
Optional. Pool for an asset that lives inside a linked object. Allowed
only when `eas_field` is inside a link mask and that linked objecttype
is pool-managed; otherwise rejected.

pool.description:
Full pool descriptor — only used by the backup / restore pipeline so it
can recreate a referenced pool that doesn't exist on the target
instance. Regular callers should set `pool_id` and leave this empty.

linked_pool.description:
Same as `pool` but for the linked-object pool — only used by
backup / restore.

eas_field.description:
Full API name (relative to `objecttype`; the `<objecttype>.` prefix is
added internally) of the field that receives the uploaded asset. The
referenced column MUST be of type `files`, otherwise:
'eas_field <name> needs to be type "file" but is <type>'. An unknown
field fails with 'eas_field <name> not found in objecttype <name>'.
Linked-object fields are allowed. Optional for rules that attach no
file; when set it also drives pool-requirement detection and the
`update_policy` nesting check.

linked_object_pools.description:
Per-pool overrides for linked-object pools, keyed by the linked
object's field API name (without the objecttype prefix, added
internally). Each key must resolve to a link column whose target table
is pool-managed; otherwise "linked_object_field <name> not found" or
"has no pool management". Each value is the abbreviated pool descriptor
(id, name, path).

hierarchy_linked_object.description: Optional. Fills a linked-object field with hierarchy data. May be null.

field.description:
Full API name of the linked-object field to fill. Constraints
(enforced in Init): the field must exist with a column, the column
must be a text type, the target objecttype must be hierarchical, the
field must not be inside a reverse-nested structure, and its link
parent path may be at most depth 2 (no deep nesting).

mapping.description:
Selects the field mapping used to interpret incoming file metadata.
Accepted: the string `standard` (built-in standard mapping), the string
`none` (no mapping), an integer mapping id, or null/absent (no mapping).
Any other value: 'Mapping "<v>" not accepted. Allowed: "standard",
"none", <mapping-id>'.

mapping_recipe_configs.description:
Per-recipe configuration overrides. Allowed ONLY when `mapping` is a
specific integer mapping id; setting it otherwise fails with
"mapping recipe configs can only be set for a specific mapping".
(Go json key is `mapping_recipe_configs`, plural.)

allOf.description:
Per-recipe configuration. This object is a map whose keys are **recipe
names** and whose values configure that recipe for the current operation
(e.g. producing a version or running a metadata mapping).

Recipe name format:
  * Built-in / config cookbooks: `COOKBOOK-NAME:RECIPE-NAME`
    (for example `imageconverter:browserthumbs`).
  * Plugin-provided recipes: `PLUGIN-NAME:COOKBOOK-NAME:RECIPE-NAME`.

The `params` of each recipe are **recipe-specific**: the set of valid
parameter keys, their value types, defaults, ranges and option lists are
defined by each recipe individually, not by this API schema. That is why
`params` is documented as an open object (`additionalProperties: true`)
rather than a fixed schema — a closed schema cannot describe every recipe,
and the available recipes additionally depend on which plugins are
installed.

To discover the parameters a recipe accepts on a running instance, use the
recipe listing endpoint:

    GET /inspect/apidocs/recipes/

It lists every cookbook and recipe currently loaded (including
plugin-provided ones) together with each parameter's key, description and
type. Each value you send is validated against that recipe's parameter
definition: unknown keys are rejected; a `string` param may be regex-
constrained; an `int` param is bounded by its `min`/`max`; a `select`
param must be one of the recipe's options; a `bool` param accepts a
boolean or a string (`true`/`1`/`yes` mean true). Omitting a key (or
sending `null`) applies the recipe's
default; a parameter marked mandatory by the recipe must be supplied if it
has no default.

additionalProperties.description:
Configuration for a single recipe, keyed in the parent map by the recipe
name (see the parent description for the name format).

params.description:
Recipe-specific parameters as a map of parameter name to value.

The accepted keys and value types are defined by the recipe itself
and are listed, per recipe, at `GET /inspect/apidocs/recipes/`.
Validation rules applied per parameter, depending on the recipe's
declared parameter type:
  * string  — value must be a string; may be constrained by a regex.
  * int     — value must be an integer (a numeric string is also
              accepted); bounded by the parameter's `min`/`max`.
  * select  — value must be a string equal to one of the parameter's
              allowed options.
  * bool    — value must be a boolean (a string is also accepted:
              `true`/`1`/`yes` mean true, anything else false).
An absent key, or a `null` value, applies the recipe's default. A
parameter the recipe marks as mandatory must be provided unless it has
a default. Keys not defined by the recipe are rejected.

recognize_series.description:
When true, the pipeline attempts to group related incoming files into a
series. Default false.

recognize_version.description:
When true, the pipeline attempts to detect that an incoming file is a
new version of an existing asset. Default false.

update_search_field.description:
Optional. Full API name of the field used to look up an existing object
when `update_mode` is `update` or `upsert` (ignored for `insert`). Must
be a lookup-supported field or the literal `_global_object_id`; fields
inside a reverse-nested structure are rejected.

update_mode.description:
Whether each processed file CREATES a new object or UPDATES an existing
one. Empty/absent is normalized to `insert`; any value outside the enum
fails with "Unknown update mode <mode>".
  - `insert`: always create a brand-new object; no existing-object
    lookup. `update_search_field` is ignored and `update_policy` is
    forced to empty.
  - `update`: locate an existing object via `update_search_field` and
    update it; does not create when none is found.
  - `upsert`: locate an existing object and update it, otherwise insert
    a new one.
For `update`/`upsert`, `update_policy` resolves an asset collision on
`eas_field`.

update_policy.description:
How to resolve the uploaded asset against an EXISTING asset in
`eas_field` when `update_mode` is `update` or `upsert`. An unknown value
fails with "Unknown update policy <policy>".
  - `create_version`: add the new file as a new version of the existing
    asset.
  - `replace`: overwrite the existing asset with the new file.
  - `create_version_preferred`: add as a new version and mark it the
    preferred one.
  - `refuse`: reject the collision; do not write.
Mode-dependent normalization:
  - `update_mode=insert`: this field is ignored and forced to empty;
    only the four values above (or empty) are accepted as input.
  - `update`/`upsert` with `eas_field` inside a NESTED field: forced to
    `create_version` (only create_version is meaningful for nested file
    fields).
  - `update`/`upsert`, non-nested: one of the four enum values is
    required (empty is NOT accepted).

tags.description:
Tags to set for the created main object. The object also receives all
default tags with display type "hidden". Created linked objects get the
default tags only, not these configured ones.

plugin.description:
Optional. Plugin callback that post-processes each uploaded file, in the
strict format `<pluginName>:<callbackName>` (the colon and callback are
mandatory). `callbackName` must start with a lowercase letter and use
only `[a-z_0-9]`. The plugin must exist and declare a `collection_upload`
callback of that name in its manifest; otherwise the request fails
(wrong format / unknown plugin / plugin not found / unknown
collection_upload). Example: `fylr_example:filename_copy`.

plugin_config.description:
Optional, plugin-specific configuration passed through to the named
`plugin` callback. Opaque to the collection pipeline: it is typed only as
a nested map `{ <configName>: { <paramName>: <value> } }` and is NOT
interpreted by core. Keys and value schemas are defined by the chosen
plugin's `collection_upload` config section in its manifest; unknown
config names or parameters are warn-logged and IGNORED (not rejected),
and parameter values are validated only by the plugin's own param types.
It is free-form here because the valid keys depend entirely on the
plugin manifest — consult that plugin's documentation. Only meaningful
when `plugin` is set.

_generated_rights.description: Object which lists the compiled permissions for the current user. Not all permissions are present in all contexts.

_has_acl.description: Flag that indicates that this collection has an active `_acl` (even if inherited).

_has_children.description:
Flag that inidicates if this collection has children. This flag is indpendent of the actual permissions of
the user for that collection. So, if the user cannot access the only sub collection of this collection, the
`_has_children` flag will still be set.

_has_remote_objects.description: Flag that inidicates if this collection contains at least one foreign collection object.

_level.description:
Starting with 1, `_level` states the depth of the collection in the collection tree. The top level from the user's
perspective is 3. The 1st level is the root collection, the 2nd level the user's top level collection which is a system
collection and automatically created to hold all the user's collections underneath.

items.description: List of permissions.

_id.description:
Auto assigned `_id` to indicate the permission. Permissions can be
updated by re-using the `_id`.

_owner.description: Available only in some contexts, this flag indicated _the owner_ of an object.

_pool_contact.description: Available only in some contexts, this flag indicates _the contact of a pool_ of an object.

_version.description: The version of the user record.

_generated_displayname.description:
System generated displayname. This is the user's `displayname`,
`last_name` & `first_name`, `login` or `email`. If none of these is
set, the `_id` is used.

login.description: Set for users of type `collection` or `email`.

frontend_language.description: The preferred frontend language of the user (BCP 47)

_generated_displayname.description:
System generated displayname. This is the groups's `displayname`,
`name` or `reference`. Rendered in the frontend language of the
current user. If none of these is set, the `_id` is used.

sticky.description:
Flag to indicate that this permission persist even if subordinate
permissions use the `_private_acl` flag to clear the list of permissions
inherited from their parent. This is useful to add permissions which
cannot be taken away from sub collections.

active.description:
Flag to indicate if this permission is active. It can be used to
temporarily disable permissions without losing details of the
permission.

_right_preset.description:
The preset used for this permission. If set, no permissions need to be
set in this entry, permissions are taken from the given preset.

from.description:
If set, the permission is only valid from this date on. It can be
given in ISO 8601 format, so e.g. "2025" sets the beginning of the
year 2025. Dates are parsed as UTC if not specified with time zone.

rights.description: Object which carries the permissions granted by this item.

additionalProperties.description: Depending on the context, only some properties can be used to define a right. The key is the name of permission.

_grantable.description: Flag to indicate that this permission can be shared in a collection ACL.

allow_update_and_remove.description: Flag to allow to update or remove a file id from a column.

classes.description: List of classes this permission if given for. An empty list matches all.

extensions.description: List of extensions this permission if given for. An empty list matches all.

versions.description: List of version names (renditions) this permission if given for. An empty list matches all.

limit.description: Byte size as string. For formats refer to (go-datasize](https://github.com/programmfabrik/go-datasize).

additionalProperties.description: The key is the object type ID.

oneOf.description: Set to `standard` for the standard mask.
The mask id given the permission for.

column_ids.description: List of column api ids to apply this permission to.

objecttype_ids.description: List of object type ids to apply this permission to.

pool_ids.description: List of pool IDs to apply this permission to.

tagfilter.description:
The permission can be filtered by tags. Depending on the context, the
tags of an object influence the permission. `any`, `all` and `not` are
matched in an `AND` fashion.

any.description: List of tag ids. Any one of the tags must set to match this condition.

all.description: List of tag ids. All of the tags must be set to match this condition.

not.description: List of tag ids. None of the tags must be set to match this condition.

send_email_notification.description:
If set and newly created, this write-only property is used to send an
email with sharing information to all users of this ACL item.

text.description: The text included in the email.

_private_acl.description:
Flag which indicates a private ACL. With this setting ACL from the parent are ignored (except for the items
which set `sticky`.

_path.description:
Array of parent collections. A minimal record is available for all parent collections. This includes collections
the user has not direct permission to access. Frontends can use the path to build a tree view of all collections.

_invalid_acl.description:
Collection can share permissions to other users. The owner of the
collection must carry the grantable permission for each object in the
collection in order to share the collection. **fylr** determines for the
whole collection if sharing is allowed or not. If it is not allowed, the
collection will be put in `_invalid_acl: true` state which disables the
ACL attached to the collection.

_owner.description:
The owner of the collection. This is set to the user of the parent collection.
Mismatching owners will result in an API error. Only users are allowed, no groups.
Available only in some contexts, this flag indicated _the owner_ of an object.

_pool_contact.description: Available only in some contexts, this flag indicates _the contact of a pool_ of an object.

_version.description: The version of the user record.

_generated_displayname.description:
System generated displayname. This is the user's `displayname`,
`last_name` & `first_name`, `login` or `email`. If none of these is
set, the `_id` is used.

login.description: Set for users of type `collection` or `email`.

frontend_language.description: The preferred frontend language of the user (BCP 47)

_generated_displayname.description:
System generated displayname. This is the groups's `displayname`,
`name` or `reference`. Rendered in the frontend language of the
current user. If none of these is set, the `_id` is used.

objects.description: Objects linked in this collection. This is only supported as writable property for PUT /collection.

_global_object_id.description:
The object id identifying the object. This is considered a local object if has
the format `<system object id>@local` or `<system object id>@<db uuid>`. All other ids
are considered foreign and accepted without further checks. Local ids are checked
for existance.

webfrontend_props.description:
An object to store custom data for collections. Frontends are advised
to use their own top level property here and put all custom data
underneath. Other top level data should be stored back as is. The
server applies no processing to that data.

lookup:_global_object_id.description:
Object lookups can use `_system_object_id` or `_uuid` to find existing objects. If objects are promised to
be injected into **fylr**, `_allow_defer` can be set. Custom columns can also be used to find an object.

_hotfolder_upload_urls.description: A list of URLs to upload files into this collection.

type.description:
The type `webdav` indicates the enabled read & write WebDAV access. The type `webdav-hotfolder``
is a writable WebDAV access which allows the user to drop files into the collection.

_has_pin.description:
This flag indicates that the collection has a pin code. The actual pin code is only visible
to `system.root` users and users with the `BAG_ACL`.

pin_ok.description:
Flag to indicate that the pin was entered correctly for the current user. `system.root` and
all users with the `BAG_ACL` permission will always see `pin_ok`.

_created_at.description: Timestamp when this collection was created.

_updated_at.description: Timestamp when this collection was updated.

200.description: The response contains the collection created, including the issued `_id`.

_id.description: The `_id` is issued from the fylr "sequence" table when a collection is created.

_id_parent.description:
The `_id_parent` can be set to link a collection as sub collection to
another. The `_owner` if a child collection will always be set to the
owner of the parent collection.

_version.description:
The `_version` of the collection created or updated. New collections
must be inserted using version _1_. Version updates must provide the
current version + 1. The server rejects updates / insert if the
version doesn't match the expected version.

children_allowed.description:
Flag to indicate if this collection can have children or not. This
is to keep compability with easydb 5 and has no effect in **fylr**.

objects_allowed.description:
Flag to indicate if this collection can have link objects or not.
This is to keep compability with easydb 5 and has no effect in
**fylr**.

is_system_collection.description:
Flag to inidicate that this collection is a system collection. A
system collection cannot be deleted.

webfrontend_props.description:
An object to store custom data for collections. Frontends are advised
to use their own top level property here and put all custom data
underneath. Other top level data should be stored back as is. The
server applies no processing to that data.

displayname.description: The displayname of the collection is used as primary label for the user when this collection is displayed.

description.description: The description of the collection is used as a more detail information block when presenting the collection.

create_object.description:
If the collection is used to create objects, like with `/api/db?collection=ID` or via WebDAV, the objects need some
basic information so the records can be created. This object defines these basics. The `_compiled_created_object`
can be used to access the information from the first parent which has this property set. There is no possibility to
remove this setting on a lower level once the parent collection has set it.

objecttype.description:
Technical name of the target objecttype the pipeline writes into.
Must reference an existing objecttype (resolved via
`datamodel.TableByName`); an unknown name fails with
"Objecttype not found". De-facto required: an empty value disables the
entire create-object rule (ApiToObject returns nil, nil).

mask_id.description:
API id of the mask, scoped to `objecttype`, through which the
new/updated object is written. Must be an existing mask api-id on that
objecttype, otherwise: "Mask <id> not found in objecttype <name>".

pool_id.description:
Optional. Pool the main object is filed into. Required only when the
resolved `eas_field` path crosses a pool-managed objecttype; supplying
it when not needed fails with "Pool not needed for <field>", omitting it
when needed fails with "Pool required for <field>".

linked_pool_id.description:
Optional. Pool for an asset that lives inside a linked object. Allowed
only when `eas_field` is inside a link mask and that linked objecttype
is pool-managed; otherwise rejected.

pool.description:
Full pool descriptor — only used by the backup / restore pipeline so it
can recreate a referenced pool that doesn't exist on the target
instance. Regular callers should set `pool_id` and leave this empty.

linked_pool.description:
Same as `pool` but for the linked-object pool — only used by
backup / restore.

eas_field.description:
Full API name (relative to `objecttype`; the `<objecttype>.` prefix is
added internally) of the field that receives the uploaded asset. The
referenced column MUST be of type `files`, otherwise:
'eas_field <name> needs to be type "file" but is <type>'. An unknown
field fails with 'eas_field <name> not found in objecttype <name>'.
Linked-object fields are allowed. Optional for rules that attach no
file; when set it also drives pool-requirement detection and the
`update_policy` nesting check.

linked_object_pools.description:
Per-pool overrides for linked-object pools, keyed by the linked
object's field API name (without the objecttype prefix, added
internally). Each key must resolve to a link column whose target table
is pool-managed; otherwise "linked_object_field <name> not found" or
"has no pool management". Each value is the abbreviated pool descriptor
(id, name, path).

hierarchy_linked_object.description: Optional. Fills a linked-object field with hierarchy data. May be null.

field.description:
Full API name of the linked-object field to fill. Constraints
(enforced in Init): the field must exist with a column, the column
must be a text type, the target objecttype must be hierarchical, the
field must not be inside a reverse-nested structure, and its link
parent path may be at most depth 2 (no deep nesting).

mapping.description:
Selects the field mapping used to interpret incoming file metadata.
Accepted: the string `standard` (built-in standard mapping), the string
`none` (no mapping), an integer mapping id, or null/absent (no mapping).
Any other value: 'Mapping "<v>" not accepted. Allowed: "standard",
"none", <mapping-id>'.

mapping_recipe_configs.description:
Per-recipe configuration overrides. Allowed ONLY when `mapping` is a
specific integer mapping id; setting it otherwise fails with
"mapping recipe configs can only be set for a specific mapping".
(Go json key is `mapping_recipe_configs`, plural.)

allOf.description:
Per-recipe configuration. This object is a map whose keys are **recipe
names** and whose values configure that recipe for the current operation
(e.g. producing a version or running a metadata mapping).

Recipe name format:
  * Built-in / config cookbooks: `COOKBOOK-NAME:RECIPE-NAME`
    (for example `imageconverter:browserthumbs`).
  * Plugin-provided recipes: `PLUGIN-NAME:COOKBOOK-NAME:RECIPE-NAME`.

The `params` of each recipe are **recipe-specific**: the set of valid
parameter keys, their value types, defaults, ranges and option lists are
defined by each recipe individually, not by this API schema. That is why
`params` is documented as an open object (`additionalProperties: true`)
rather than a fixed schema — a closed schema cannot describe every recipe,
and the available recipes additionally depend on which plugins are
installed.

To discover the parameters a recipe accepts on a running instance, use the
recipe listing endpoint:

    GET /inspect/apidocs/recipes/

It lists every cookbook and recipe currently loaded (including
plugin-provided ones) together with each parameter's key, description and
type. Each value you send is validated against that recipe's parameter
definition: unknown keys are rejected; a `string` param may be regex-
constrained; an `int` param is bounded by its `min`/`max`; a `select`
param must be one of the recipe's options; a `bool` param accepts a
boolean or a string (`true`/`1`/`yes` mean true). Omitting a key (or
sending `null`) applies the recipe's
default; a parameter marked mandatory by the recipe must be supplied if it
has no default.

additionalProperties.description:
Configuration for a single recipe, keyed in the parent map by the recipe
name (see the parent description for the name format).

params.description:
Recipe-specific parameters as a map of parameter name to value.

The accepted keys and value types are defined by the recipe itself
and are listed, per recipe, at `GET /inspect/apidocs/recipes/`.
Validation rules applied per parameter, depending on the recipe's
declared parameter type:
  * string  — value must be a string; may be constrained by a regex.
  * int     — value must be an integer (a numeric string is also
              accepted); bounded by the parameter's `min`/`max`.
  * select  — value must be a string equal to one of the parameter's
              allowed options.
  * bool    — value must be a boolean (a string is also accepted:
              `true`/`1`/`yes` mean true, anything else false).
An absent key, or a `null` value, applies the recipe's default. A
parameter the recipe marks as mandatory must be provided unless it has
a default. Keys not defined by the recipe are rejected.

recognize_series.description:
When true, the pipeline attempts to group related incoming files into a
series. Default false.

recognize_version.description:
When true, the pipeline attempts to detect that an incoming file is a
new version of an existing asset. Default false.

update_search_field.description:
Optional. Full API name of the field used to look up an existing object
when `update_mode` is `update` or `upsert` (ignored for `insert`). Must
be a lookup-supported field or the literal `_global_object_id`; fields
inside a reverse-nested structure are rejected.

update_mode.description:
Whether each processed file CREATES a new object or UPDATES an existing
one. Empty/absent is normalized to `insert`; any value outside the enum
fails with "Unknown update mode <mode>".
  - `insert`: always create a brand-new object; no existing-object
    lookup. `update_search_field` is ignored and `update_policy` is
    forced to empty.
  - `update`: locate an existing object via `update_search_field` and
    update it; does not create when none is found.
  - `upsert`: locate an existing object and update it, otherwise insert
    a new one.
For `update`/`upsert`, `update_policy` resolves an asset collision on
`eas_field`.

update_policy.description:
How to resolve the uploaded asset against an EXISTING asset in
`eas_field` when `update_mode` is `update` or `upsert`. An unknown value
fails with "Unknown update policy <policy>".
  - `create_version`: add the new file as a new version of the existing
    asset.
  - `replace`: overwrite the existing asset with the new file.
  - `create_version_preferred`: add as a new version and mark it the
    preferred one.
  - `refuse`: reject the collision; do not write.
Mode-dependent normalization:
  - `update_mode=insert`: this field is ignored and forced to empty;
    only the four values above (or empty) are accepted as input.
  - `update`/`upsert` with `eas_field` inside a NESTED field: forced to
    `create_version` (only create_version is meaningful for nested file
    fields).
  - `update`/`upsert`, non-nested: one of the four enum values is
    required (empty is NOT accepted).

tags.description:
Tags to set for the created main object. The object also receives all
default tags with display type "hidden". Created linked objects get the
default tags only, not these configured ones.

plugin.description:
Optional. Plugin callback that post-processes each uploaded file, in the
strict format `<pluginName>:<callbackName>` (the colon and callback are
mandatory). `callbackName` must start with a lowercase letter and use
only `[a-z_0-9]`. The plugin must exist and declare a `collection_upload`

Optional, plugin-specific configuration passed through to the named

and parameter values are validated only by the plugin's own param types.
It is free-form here because the valid keys depend entirely on the

reference.description:
A unique string to set a collection reference. This can be used in collection lookups and in the search. The
server will create a root rollection with the reference `system:root`. Underneath the root collection, each user
gets a top level collection for his collection. This will have a reference starting with `user:ref:<login|reference>`.

shortname.description: A unique string to set a collection shortname. This can be used for search.

type.description:
Usually set to `workfolder` or `search`, this property has currently no effect in **fylr**. Only for the backup,
`search` is used to replace ids in saved searches.

pin_code.description:
For sharing, a collection can be protected with a pin code. This pin code must be entered by the user and saved
via `/api/user` in order to access the objects in the collection.

uuid.description:
A unique ID for the collection. This can be set for new collections or will be assigned by the server.
The UUID of the collection to access it via WebDAV.

lookup:_id.description:
The lookup can be used to find collections by reference. When this is used the `_version` is automatically
set to the current version + 1.

reference.description: The reference of the collection to lookup.

lookup:_id_parent.description: The lookup to find the parent collection.

reference.description: The reference of the collection to lookup.

_count.description: The number of objects in this collection.

_count_recursive.description: The number of objects in this collection and and all descendant collections (recursive).

_create_object_compiled.description: The `collection.create_object` setting for this collection (if set) or the first parent collection which has it.`

objecttype.description:
Technical name of the target objecttype the pipeline writes into.
Must reference an existing objecttype (resolved via
`datamodel.TableByName`); an unknown name fails with
"Objecttype not found". De-facto required: an empty value disables the
entire create-object rule (ApiToObject returns nil, nil).

mask_id.description:
API id of the mask, scoped to `objecttype`, through which the
new/updated object is written. Must be an existing mask api-id on that
objecttype, otherwise: "Mask <id> not found in objecttype <name>".

pool_id.description:

resolved `eas_field` path crosses a pool-managed objecttype; supplying
it when not needed fails with "Pool not needed for <field>", omitting it
when needed fails with "Pool required for <field>".

linked_pool_id.description:
Optional. Pool for an asset that lives inside a linked object. Allowed
only when `eas_field` is inside a link mask and that linked objecttype
is pool-managed; otherwise rejected.

pool.description:
Full pool descriptor — only used by the backup / restore pipeline so it
can recreate a referenced pool that doesn't exist on the target
instance. Regular callers should set `pool_id` and leave this empty.

linked_pool.description:
Same as `pool` but for the linked-object pool — only used by
backup / restore.

eas_field.description:
Full API name (relative to `objecttype`; the `<objecttype>.` prefix is
added internally) of the field that receives the uploaded asset. The
referenced column MUST be of type `files`, otherwise:
'eas_field <name> needs to be type "file" but is <type>'. An unknown

Linked-object fields are allowed. Optional for rules that attach no
file; when set it also drives pool-requirement detection and the
