returns without waiting for it.

parameters.name: background_invalid_acl_timelimit
parameters.description:
Go duration string (e.g. `30s`) bounding the inline
collection-ACL re-check. Defaults to `30s`. If the check
exceeds it, the response is a 202 offering to continue the
check in the background (`background_invalid_acl=true`).

200.description: The pools were saved.

202.description:
A confirmation is required before the save can complete. Two
cases: (a) a watermark or caption change needs originals to be
re-rendered — resend with `confirm=resync` or `confirm=skip`;
(b) the post-save collection-ACL re-check exceeded its time
limit — resend with `background_invalid_acl=true` to finish it
in the background.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Triggers include an
invalid payload, the instance being in read-only mode (code
`ReadOnlyMode`), a save of a non-root pool without a parent
(code `PoolNeedsParent`), and — on `DELETE` — a pool that
still holds objects (code `PoolNotEmpty`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The request is forbidden. For POST/PUT, DELETE and `/stats`
the user must hold the `system.poolmanager` system right (or
`system.root`); otherwise the response is code
`SystemRightRequired` with `parameters.rights` naming the
required right(s). For reads (`GET /pool/{poolID}`, `/stats`)
and the per-pool BAG checks on save and delete, the relevant
pool `_acl` grant is missing (code `RightRequired`, with
`parameters.right` naming the missing right, e.g. `bag_read`,
`bag_create`, `bag_write`, `bag_acl`, `bag_delete`).

put.summary: Create or update one or more pools (alias for POST).

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description:
Answer to the file-resync 202 prompt. When a save changes a
pool's watermark or caption, the originals in that pool must
be re-rendered. Resend with `confirm=resync` to schedule the
re-sync, or `confirm=skip` to save without re-rendering.
Omitting it on the first request triggers the 202 prompt.

parameters.name: allow_invalid_acl
parameters.description:
After the pool change, **fylr** re-checks the ACL validity of
collections affected by the pool's ACL. With
`allow_invalid_acl=true` the save is accepted even if some
collections end up with an invalid ACL; with the default
`false` such a case is returned as an error.

parameters.name: background_invalid_acl
parameters.description:
If `true`, the post-save collection-ACL re-check is queued as
a background task instead of running inline, and the request
returns without waiting for it.

parameters.name: background_invalid_acl_timelimit
parameters.description:
Go duration string (e.g. `30s`) bounding the inline
collection-ACL re-check. Defaults to `30s`. If the check
exceeds it, the response is a 202 offering to continue the
check in the background (`background_invalid_acl=true`).

200.description: The pools were saved.

202.description:
A confirmation is required before the save can complete. Two
cases: (a) a watermark or caption change needs originals to be
re-rendered — resend with `confirm=resync` or `confirm=skip`;
(b) the post-save collection-ACL re-check exceeded its time
limit — resend with `background_invalid_acl=true` to finish it
in the background.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Triggers include an
invalid payload, the instance being in read-only mode (code
`ReadOnlyMode`), a save of a non-root pool without a parent
(code `PoolNeedsParent`), and — on `DELETE` — a pool that
still holds objects (code `PoolNotEmpty`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The request is forbidden. For POST/PUT, DELETE and `/stats`
the user must hold the `system.poolmanager` system right (or
`system.root`); otherwise the response is code
`SystemRightRequired` with `parameters.rights` naming the
required right(s). For reads (`GET /pool/{poolID}`, `/stats`)
and the per-pool BAG checks on save and delete, the relevant
pool `_acl` grant is missing (code `RightRequired`, with
`parameters.right` naming the missing right, e.g. `bag_read`,
`bag_create`, `bag_write`, `bag_acl`, `bag_delete`).

get.summary: List all pools visible to the authenticated user.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of pools to return. Defaults to `1000`. The
server pages internally and returns at most `limit` pools the
session is allowed to read.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
Number of pools to skip before collecting results. Defaults to
`0`.

200.description: Array of all pools the current session can see.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

/pool/{poolID}.description:
Read or delete a single **pool** identified by `poolID`. `GET`
returns the pool, wrapped in a single-element array (requires the
`bag_read` right on it); `DELETE` removes it (requires the
`system.poolmanager` system right and `bag_delete`, and only
succeeds when the pool and its subpools hold no objects).

Differs from easydb 5: on `GET`, the ACL block and the other
administrative fields are included only when the session holds the
`system.poolmanager` system right (or `system.root`), not based on
the per-pool `bag_write` right. (easydb 5 includes the ACL when the
user has `bag_write` and omits it when the user has only `bag_read`.)

get.summary: Retrieve a single pool by id.

parameters.name: poolID
parameters.description: The pool's `_id`.

200.description:
A single-element array containing the requested pool. The
handler always serialises the pool list as a JSON array, even
for one pool.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The request is forbidden. For POST/PUT, DELETE and `/stats`
the user must hold the `system.poolmanager` system right (or
`system.root`); otherwise the response is code
`SystemRightRequired` with `parameters.rights` naming the
required right(s). For reads (`GET /pool/{poolID}`, `/stats`)
and the per-pool BAG checks on save and delete, the relevant
pool `_acl` grant is missing (code `RightRequired`, with
`parameters.right` naming the missing right, e.g. `bag_read`,
`bag_create`, `bag_write`, `bag_acl`, `bag_delete`).

delete.description:
Deletes the pool. The session must hold the `system.poolmanager`
system right and the `bag_delete` right on the pool. Pools that
still contain objects (directly or via subpools) cannot be deleted
— the response surfaces this as a 400 with code `PoolNotEmpty`.

Differs from easydb 5: an unknown pool id is reported as `404`
with code `ResourceNotFound`. (easydb 5 returns a `400` "pool id
not found" error.)

parameters.name: poolID
parameters.description: The pool's `_id`.

200.description: The pool was deleted.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Triggers include an
invalid payload, the instance being in read-only mode (code
`ReadOnlyMode`), a save of a non-root pool without a parent
(code `PoolNeedsParent`), and — on `DELETE` — a pool that
still holds objects (code `PoolNotEmpty`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The request is forbidden. For POST/PUT, DELETE and `/stats`
the user must hold the `system.poolmanager` system right (or
`system.root`); otherwise the response is code
`SystemRightRequired` with `parameters.rights` naming the
required right(s). For reads (`GET /pool/{poolID}`, `/stats`)
and the per-pool BAG checks on save and delete, the relevant
pool `_acl` grant is missing (code `RightRequired`, with
`parameters.right` naming the missing right, e.g. `bag_read`,
`bag_create`, `bag_write`, `bag_acl`, `bag_delete`).

404.description:
The pool id in the URL does not match any stored pool. The
response carries `code: ResourceNotFound` with
`parameters.resource` = `Pool <id>`.

/pool/{poolID}/stats.description:
Asset statistics for the **pool** identified by `poolID` — file
counts and storage sizes of originals (latest, history and deleted),
returned as a `PoolStats` object. Requires the `system.poolmanager`
system right and `bag_read` on the pool; `include_subpools=true`
rolls the figures up over the pool's sub-tree.

get.description:
Returns file/asset statistics (counts and storage sizes of
originals — latest, history and deleted) for the pool. Requires
the `system.poolmanager` system right and `bag_read` on the pool.
With `include_subpools=true` the figures roll up the whole
sub-tree below the pool; otherwise only this pool.

parameters.name: poolID
parameters.description: The pool's `_id`.

parameters.name: include_subpools
parameters.description:
If `true`, the counts roll up the whole sub-tree below the
requested pool. Defaults to `false`.

200.description:
Asset statistics for the pool — file counts and storage
usage scoped to this pool (and, with `include_subpools=true`,
its transitive sub-tree).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The request is forbidden. For POST/PUT, DELETE and `/stats`
the user must hold the `system.poolmanager` system right (or
`system.root`); otherwise the response is code
`SystemRightRequired` with `parameters.rights` naming the
required right(s). For reads (`GET /pool/{poolID}`, `/stats`)
and the per-pool BAG checks on save and delete, the relevant
pool `_acl` grant is missing (code `RightRequired`, with
`parameters.right` naming the missing right, e.g. `bag_read`,
`bag_create`, `bag_write`, `bag_acl`, `bag_delete`).

/objecttype.description:
Read and write **objecttypes** — the per-record-type configuration
that fylr layers on the datamodel definition (default masks, exposure
flags, ACL, tags, transitions, mask / column filters, asset-filename
policy, watermark and janitor policy). The objecttype itself (its name, fields and
hierarchy) is part of the datamodel and is created through the schema
endpoints; this endpoint only configures objecttypes that already
exist in the current (committed) datamodel. A write updates the
objecttype configuration directly and takes effect immediately when
the request transaction commits — there is no working copy and no
separate `schema/commit` step.

`GET` is open to any authenticated user; `POST` requires
`system.objecttypemanager`. Authentication is by access token (a
`Bearer` header or the `access_token` query parameter).

Differs from easydb 5: writes that touch collection ACLs are
confirmed with the `confirm` / `allow_invalid_acl` /
`background_invalid_acl` query parameters instead of easydb 5's
single `collection_rights_policy` parameter.

get.summary: List all objecttypes in the current datamodel.
get.description:
Returns every objecttype in the current datamodel as an
array of descriptors. Any authenticated user may read; the
auxiliary fields that need `system.objecttypemanager` are included
only when that right is held (or are dropped entirely with
`format=short`).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description:
Set `format=short` to omit the auxiliary fields that require
the `system.objecttypemanager` right (`_acl`, tags,
transitions, mask / column filters, etc.). Any other value,
or omitting the parameter, returns the full descriptor. Only
`short` is special-cased — there is no separate `long` /
`standard` behaviour.

200.description: Array of objecttype descriptors.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

post.summary: Create or update the settings of one or more objecttypes.
post.description:
Updates the configuration of an array of objecttypes directly. The
objecttypes must already exist in the committed datamodel; this
endpoint writes their settings, not their structure. The change
takes effect immediately when the request transaction commits —
there is no `schema/commit` step. The user needs
`system.objecttypemanager` (or `system.root`). When the instance is
in read-only mode the request is rejected before processing (see
the 400 response).

The objecttype itself (its name, fields, hierarchy) is defined by
the datamodel, not created here — every element must carry an
existing `objecttype._id`, and `objecttype._version` must be
`current + 1` (optimistic locking; a mismatch is a 400
`VersionMismatch`). Only the configuration fields are mutated;
fields the requester is not allowed to change are ignored. Fields
omitted from the body keep their stored value (the server reloads
the current objecttype and merges). Notable parse rules:

  * `mapping_*` fields take a mapping id or the string `"none"` to
    clear; `mapping_image_import_recipe_configs` may only be set
    when `mapping_image_import` names a real mapping.
  * `_standard_masks`: `null` clears the preference; a non-empty
    array of mask ids sets it; an empty array `[]` is rejected
    (`ObjecttypeNeedsStandardMask`).
  * `watermark.gravity`, if present, must be one of `nw n ne w c e
    sw s se` or empty.
  * `caption` keys must each name an existing files field that is
    not inside a reverse-nested and whose objecttype's pool
    management matches the endpoint (objecttype, not pool).
  * `janitor_policy` bounds must be `null` or `>= 0`. `janitor_policy`
    is only meaningful for non-pool objecttypes; for a pool
    objecttype it is neither stored nor returned. The key is
    merged from the stored value only when omitted — sending
    `null` explicitly clears it.
  * `standard_numbering` is accepted for compatibility but ignored
    (never stored, always returned empty).

The save can pause and return a 202 to ask for confirmation (see
the 202 response). The query parameters below are how the client
answers those confirmations: it re-sends the **same** request body
with the matching parameter appended.

Differs from easydb 5: the 202 confirmation contract uses
`confirm` / `allow_invalid_acl` / `background_invalid_acl` rather
than easydb 5's `collection_rights_policy`.

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description:
Answer to the renditions-resync 202 that is raised when a
watermark or caption change requires recomputing the
renditions of already-stored files. `resync` recomputes them
now; `skip` saves without recomputing. Any other value is
rejected with a generic 400 (`confirm "<value>" not
supported`).

parameters.name: allow_invalid_acl
parameters.description:
Answer to the invalid-collection-ACL 202. When `true`, a save
whose ACL change would invalidate a collection the owner can
no longer fully grant is allowed to proceed; the affected
collections are flagged `invalid_acl` and sharing for them is
disabled. If the user lacks `bag-acl` on such a collection the
request instead fails with `UrlParameterNotSupported` for
`allow_invalid_acl`.

parameters.name: background_invalid_acl
parameters.description:
Answer to the ACL-revalidation timeout 202. When `true`, the
collection-ACL revalidation runs as an asynchronous background
task and the request returns immediately instead of completing
the check synchronously.

parameters.name: background_invalid_acl_timelimit
parameters.description:
Overrides the synchronous time budget before the timeout 202
is offered. A Go duration string (e.g. `5s`); an invalid value
surfaces as a generic 400.

200.description:
The objecttypes were saved. The body echoes the saved
descriptors (with `_acl`, tags, transitions and mask filters
included). The `X-Fylr-Cache-ID` response header carries the
new datamodel cache id so clients can invalidate caches.

X-Fylr-Cache-ID.description:
Updated cache id (`<startup-unix>-<cache-id>`), bumped on
every successful write.

202.description:
The save needs confirmation before it can complete. Resubmit
the identical request with the query parameter named by the
task's form option or button appended — for example
`allow_invalid_acl=true`, `background_invalid_acl=true`, or
`confirm=resync` / `confirm=skip`.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. All errors below are
`realm: api`, `statuscode: 400`. The objecttype-specific codes
(`package: objecttype`) are `UnknownField` /
`UnsupportedFieldType`, both raised while resolving an
`_export_asset_filenames` template against the datamodel.

The body parse and the `ApiToObject` validation add several
`package: ferrors` codes:

  * `ReadOnlyMode` — the instance is in read-only mode; the
    request is rejected before any processing (message
    `Read-only mode is enabled.`).
  * `VersionMismatch` — a submitted objecttype's `_version` is
    not `current + 1`. `parameters.got` / `parameters.expected`
    carry the values.
  * `ObjecttypeNeedsStandardMask` — `_standard_masks` was sent
    as an empty array (`[]`); send `null` to clear the
    preference instead. `parameters.name` carries the
    objecttype name.
  * `UrlParameterNotSupported` — `allow_invalid_acl=true` was
    sent but the user lacks `bag-acl` on an affected
    collection. `parameters.param` is `allow_invalid_acl`.

Generic 400s (`code: ServerGeneric` or a bare message) also
surface here for: an unsupported `confirm` value (`confirm
"<value>" not supported`); an unknown `_standard_masks` /
`_maskfilters` mask id (`Objecttype: Mask <id> not found in
Datamodel`); a `watermark.gravity` outside the allowed set
(`watermark.gravity "<value>" not supported`); an out-of-range
`janitor_policy` bound (a negative value;
`janitor_policy.file_link_delete.keep_days_history_versions
must be null or > 0` and the analogous `keep_count` /
`min_object_deleted_days` messages); a bad `caption`
(`caption "<field>": field not found`, `... must not be inside
a reverse nested`, `... is not of type "file"`, or a
pool-management mismatch); recipe configs set while
`mapping_image_import` is `"none"` (`mapping recipe configs can
only be set for a specific mapping`); own transitions / tags on
an objecttype whose pool does not allow them (`Objecttype
"<name>" cannot have own transitions` / `... own tags`); a bad
`background_invalid_acl_timelimit` duration. Datamodel-validation,
save-pipeline and DB-driver errors (e.g.
`DatabaseUniqueKeyViolation`) can appear too. The `code` enum
on the schema lists only the two objecttype codes; it is not
exhaustive.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the system right required for
the operation (`system.objecttypemanager`, or `system.root`).

423.description:
The write could not acquire a database lock because a
concurrent write is touching the same rows (PostgreSQL
`55P03`). The transaction is rolled back and the error is
surfaced as `code: DatabaseLockError`, `package: ferrors`,
`statuscode: 423`. Retry the identical request once the
conflicting write has finished.

/objecttype/{id}.description:
Read a single **objecttype** by its numeric `id`. Returns a
one-element array holding the descriptor; pass `format=short` to drop
the auxiliary fields that need the `system.objecttypemanager` right.
Any authenticated user may read.

Differs from easydb 5: an unknown `id` returns a generic 400
(`code: ServerGeneric`), not a 404.

get.description:
Loads one objecttype by numeric `id` and returns it wrapped in a
one-element array (the descriptor is the only array entry — the
response is not a bare object).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The numeric id of the objecttype. The path segment must be a
positive integer (route pattern `[1-9][0-9]*`); name-based
lookup is not supported on this endpoint.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description:
Set `format=short` to omit the auxiliary fields that require
the `system.objecttypemanager` right (`_acl`, tags,
transitions, mask / column filters, etc.). Any other value,
or omitting the parameter, returns the full descriptor. Only
`short` is special-cased — there is no separate `long` /
`standard` behaviour.

200.description: A one-element array containing the objecttype descriptor.

400.description:
The numeric `id` in the URL does not match any objecttype in
the current datamodel. The load fails and is reported as a
generic server error (`code: ServerGeneric`, `package:
ferrors`, `statuscode: 400`) with a message of the form
`Server error occurred: objecttype.LoadById: Error loading:
ObjecttypeList.Load: Not all objecttypes found in DB` — there
is no structured 404 for an unknown id on this endpoint.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

/objecttype/{id}/stats.description:
Read **asset statistics** for one objecttype, identified by its
numeric `id` — file counts and storage usage rolled up across every
object of the type. Requires the `system.objecttypemanager` right.

fylr-only endpoint — easydb 5 has no `/objecttype/{id}/stats`
equivalent.

get.description:
Aggregates, in SQL, the originals and their renditions across all
latest / history / deleted objects of the type, bucketing by file
class and extension. Requires the `system.objecttypemanager` right
(or `system.root`).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The numeric id of the objecttype (positive integer; route
pattern `[1-9][0-9]*`). Name-based lookup is not supported.

200.description:
Asset statistics for the objecttype — file counts and
storage usage rolled up across every object of the type.

400.description:
The numeric `id` in the URL does not match any objecttype in
the current datamodel. The load fails and is reported as a
generic server error (`code: ServerGeneric`, `package:
ferrors`, `statuscode: 400`) with a message of the form
`Server error occurred: objecttype.LoadById: Error loading:
ObjecttypeList.Load: Not all objecttypes found in DB` — there
is no structured 404 for an unknown id on this endpoint.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the system right required for
the operation (`system.objecttypemanager`, or `system.root`).

/mask/{version}.description:
Access the **mask** definitions of a datamodel version. Masks
project objecttypes to a subset of fields and are the unit through
which the frontend renders forms and detail views. `version` can
be:

  * `HEAD` — the working copy (uncommitted edits). Readable by any
    authenticated user (`GET` requires only an authenticated user,
    not `system.datamodel`).
  * `CURRENT` — the most recently committed version. Resolved
    server-side; equivalent to the highest committed number.
  * a positive integer pointing at a specific committed version.

get.summary: Retrieve all masks for a datamodel version.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description:
Datamodel version. `HEAD` returns the working copy (readable
by any authenticated user); `CURRENT` resolves to the most
recently committed version; a numeric id resolves to a
specific committed version.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description:
Output detail level. `standard` (default): field descriptors
only. `long`: additionally embeds each non-splitter field's
underlying `column` definition and `full_name`. Unrecognised
values behave like `standard` (not rejected).

Differs from easydb 5: `format` selects the level of detail
and the response is always JSON. easydb 5's `format` chose the
serialization (`xml` — its default — or `json`); fylr has no
XML output.

200.description:
The mask-set for the requested datamodel version: a
`MaskSchemaApi` wrapper (`type`, `version`,
`based_on_schema_version`, `max_mask_id`) whose `masks` array
holds one descriptor per mask.

400.description:
The `version` segment is not a known committed version (or
`HEAD` / `CURRENT`). Returns code `DatamodelVersionNotFound`,
package `datamodel`.

401.description:
No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

Differs from easydb 5: a missing/invalid session is reported
as `401` (`UserRequired`); easydb 5 documents a single `403`
for both "not authenticated" and "lacks required rights".

/mask/{version}/{mask}.description:
Read a single **mask** by name from a datamodel version. Returns the
requested mask wrapped in a `MaskSchemaApi` envelope. The reserved
name `_all_fields` yields the objecttype's complete field set instead
of a named mask. `version` accepts `HEAD`, `CURRENT`, or a committed
version number.

get.summary: Retrieve a single mask of a datamodel version.

parameters.name: mask
parameters.description:
Mask name. The special name `_all_fields` returns the
objecttype's complete field set (all columns), wrapped in the
same mask-set envelope. Available to any authenticated user.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description:
Output detail level. `standard` (default): field descriptors
only. `long`: additionally embeds each non-splitter field's
underlying `column` definition and `full_name`. Unrecognised
values behave like `standard` (not rejected).

Differs from easydb 5: `format` selects the level of detail
and the response is always JSON. easydb 5's `format` chose the
serialization (`xml` — its default — or `json`); fylr has no
XML output.

200.description:
A `MaskSchemaApi` wrapper whose `masks` array contains the
single requested mask (for `_all_fields`, the synthesised
all-fields mask per objecttype).

400.description:
The `version` segment is not a known committed version (or
`HEAD` / `CURRENT`). Returns code `DatamodelVersionNotFound`,
package `datamodel`.

401.description:
No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

Differs from easydb 5: a missing/invalid session is reported
as `401` (`UserRequired`); easydb 5 documents a single `403`
for both "not authenticated" and "lacks required rights".

404.description:
The mask NAME in the URL does not exist for this datamodel
version (code `ResourceNotFound`, package `api`). An unknown
`version` is reported as a 400 `DatamodelVersionNotFound`
(package `datamodel`), not a 404.

/mask/HEAD.description:
Write the **mask** definitions into the working datamodel (`HEAD`).
The submitted `MaskSchemaApi` is stored as the uncommitted working
copy; the change becomes visible to other readers only after a
separate `POST /api/v1/schema/commit`. Requires
`system.datamodel[level=development]` (or `[level=commit]`) and is
rejected in read-only mode.

post.summary: Write masks into the working datamodel (`HEAD`).
post.description:
Saves the supplied mask set into the working copy. The change is
only visible to other readers after a separate commit via
`POST /api/v1/schema/commit`. Requires
`system.datamodel[level=development]` (or `[level=commit]`).
Rejected in read-only mode.

200.description:
The HEAD masks were saved. Returns the complete stored HEAD
mask-set (`MaskSchemaApi`, all masks — not just the submitted
subset) with any server-applied normalisation and the current
`max_mask_id`. The response also carries an updated fylr
cache-id header.

400.description:
The submitted document is malformed or inconsistent with the
current objecttypes / fields (a datamodel-validator code such
as `SchemaDuplicateMaskName`), OR the instance is in read-only
mode (code `ReadOnlyMode`, package `ferrors`). Read-only
rejections carry package `ferrors`, not `datamodel`.

401.description:
No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

Differs from easydb 5: a missing/invalid session is reported
as `401` (`UserRequired`); easydb 5 documents a single `403`
for both "not authenticated" and "lacks required rights".

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks `system.datamodel[level=development]`
(or `[level=commit]`), the right required to write the HEAD
mask-set. (`system.root` also satisfies the check.)

/schema/user/{version}.description:
The **schema** endpoint exposes the JSON-Schema view of a datamodel
version — the structural description that `/api/db` and `/api/search`
validate against. The same datamodel can also be rendered as a
diagram via `?format=svg` / `?format=png`.

`version` can be:

  * `HEAD` — the working copy (uncommitted edits).
  * `CURRENT` — the most recently committed version. Resolved
    server-side; equivalent to the highest committed number.
  * a positive integer pointing at a specific committed version.

Reading requires only an authenticated user; no `system.datamodel`
right is needed.

get.description:
Returns the datamodel either as a `SchemaApi` JSON object
(default) or as an image of its objecttype graph.

parameters.name: version
format
parameters.description:
When set, returns a rendered graph of the schema instead of
the JSON representation. The response is
`image/svg+xml` / `image/png` with
`Content-Disposition: inline; filename="fylr-schema-<instance>-<version>-v<n>.<ext>"`.
Any other value (or no value) returns the JSON form.

Differs from easydb 5: the default (no `format`) response is the
JSON `SchemaApi`, and there is no XML form. easydb 5 documented
`format=xml` as the default representation.

200.description:
The datamodel snapshot. By default a JSON `SchemaApi`
document; when `format=svg` / `format=png`, the rendered
graph image.

schema.description:
The objecttype graph rendered as SVG, returned when
`format=svg`.

The objecttype graph rendered as PNG, returned when
`format=png`.

400.description:
The `version` in the URL does not resolve to an existing
datamodel — a numeric version that matches no datamodel row
(the lookup is by version number, independent of whether that
version was committed), or a non-positive value. The body
carries code `DatamodelVersionNotFound`. Note this is a `400`,
not a `404`.

Differs from easydb 5: an unknown schema version is reported as
`400` `DatamodelVersionNotFound`, not the `404` easydb 5
documented for a "Requested schema version not found".

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

/schema/user/HEAD.description:
Writes the working datamodel (`HEAD`). A `POST` stores a
`SchemaApi` document into the working copy and returns it back
(normalised, with the bumped `max_table_id` / `max_column_id`).
The change stays invisible to plain readers until it is committed
via `POST /schema/commit`. Requires
`system.datamodel[level=development]` or `[level=commit]`.

post.summary: Update the working datamodel (`HEAD`).
post.description:
Writes the supplied `SchemaApi` document into the working copy.
The change is only visible to other readers after a commit via
`POST /schema/commit`. Requires
`system.datamodel[level=development]` (or `[level=commit]`).

200.description:
The HEAD datamodel was updated. The response returns the
stored `SchemaApi` (with any server-applied normalisations
and the bumped `max_table_id` / `max_column_id`).

400.description:
The submitted document is malformed or inconsistent with
the current objecttypes / masks: unknown column reference,
duplicate names, a `max_table_id` / `max_column_id` smaller
than the stored value, or a `version` / `based_on_version`
that is not the expected next version. A column type change
that cannot be migrated because the column already holds data
is rejected with code `TypeChangeUnsupported`. A deleted
objecttype that still holds objects is rejected with code
`ObjecttypeNotEmpty` (`parameters.stats` names them). May also
return code `ReadOnlyMode` when the instance is in read-only
mode.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user holds none of the system rights this
write needs: `system.datamodel[level=development]`,
`system.datamodel[level=commit]`, or `system.root`. The body
carries code `SystemRightRequired`; `parameters.rights` lists
the accepted rights.

/schema/commit.description:
Commits the working datamodel (`HEAD`), promoting it to a versioned
snapshot that readers without `system.datamodel` can see. A `POST`
freezes `HEAD`, sets its `committed_at`, and returns
`{ "status": "ok" }`; the `reindex` and `confirm` query parameters
govern reindexing and the `202` confirm step. Requires
`system.datamodel[level=commit]`.

post.summary: Commit the working datamodel (`HEAD`).
post.description:
Promotes the working copy to a committed datamodel — sets its
`committed_at` timestamp and makes its version visible to readers
that don't hold `system.datamodel`. HEAD already has a version
number before the commit (it's the version a future commit would
produce); the commit just freezes it.
Requires `system.datamodel[level=commit]` (or `system.root`).

Commit options are query parameters:

  * `reindex` — `skip` (no reindex), `reindex` (reindex in the
    background), or `reindex_block` (reindex in the background
    and temporarily lock the frontend). If a change needs a
    reindex and `reindex` is absent, the call returns `202`
    asking which to use.
  * `confirm=yes` — acknowledge the `202` confirm task (reindex
    and/or changed constraints) and proceed with the commit.

There is no destructive-change toggle: unsupported type changes
are rejected with code `TypeChangeUnsupported`, not opted into.

Differs from easydb 5: reindexing is controlled by `reindex`
(`skip` / `reindex` / `reindex_block`) plus a `202`/`confirm=yes`
handshake. easydb 5 had no such handshake and instead took a
`reindex_generate_events=1` flag to emit `OBJECT_INDEX` events
during reindex jobs; fylr has no equivalent parameter here.

parameters.name: reindex
confirm
parameters.description:
Set to `yes` to acknowledge the `202` confirm task (reindex
and/or changed constraints) and run the commit.

200.description:
The commit succeeded. The response is a fixed
`{ "status": "ok" }` object — the new version number is not
in the body (read it via `GET /schema/user/CURRENT` or the
`DATAMODEL_COMMIT` event).

202.description:
The commit needs confirmation before it runs. Returned when
the change requires a reindex and/or changes constraints and
the request did not already carry `confirm=yes`. Resend
`POST /schema/commit?confirm=yes` (optionally with
`reindex=skip` / `reindex` / `reindex_block`) to proceed.

400.description:
The HEAD datamodel is inconsistent and cannot be committed: a
column type change that cannot be migrated because the column
already holds data is rejected with code
`TypeChangeUnsupported`, and a deleted objecttype that still
holds objects is rejected with code `ObjecttypeNotEmpty`
(`parameters.stats` names them). May also return code
`ReadOnlyMode` when the instance is in read-only mode.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user holds neither
`system.datamodel[level=commit]` nor `system.root`. The body
carries code `SystemRightRequired`; `parameters.rights` lists
the accepted rights.

/message.description:
Admin-managed **fylr** messages shown in the web front end, optionally
requiring per-user confirmation (`confirm_every_version`, `show_always`)
and optionally scoped to a `start_time`/`end_time` window. A message is
addressed to user groups via `_groups`, not to individual users. All
operations require the `system.message` system right (or
`system.root`); a user who holds it sees and manages every message —
there is no per-user sender/recipient scoping.

Differs from easydb 5: the message kind is the `webfrontend_type`
field (free-form) and the body text is `message`; easydb 5 named these
`type` (with values such as `eula`) and `message_html`.

post.summary: Create or update one or more messages.
post.description:
Each array element is upserted by `message._version`: send
`_version: 1` to create a new message; send the stored version + 1
together with `message._id` to update an existing one (a mismatch
returns `VersionMismatch`, 400). A save replaces the message's
`_groups` set with the one supplied (an omitted/empty `_groups`
clears it). Allowed in read-only mode.

200.description: The messages were created or updated.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Possible causes: an unparseable
body or other generic failure (`code: ServerGeneric`); an update
whose `_version` is not the stored version + 1 (`code:
VersionMismatch`); a `start_time` after the `end_time` (`code:
StartTimeAfterEndTime`); or a `_groups` entry referencing a group
(by `reference`) that does not exist (`code: LookupNotFound`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the `system.message` system right
(and is not `system.root`). The response carries
`code: SystemRightRequired` (package `acl`).

put.summary: Create or update one or more messages (alias for POST).
put.description:
Identical to `POST /message`: the same handler decodes the body
and upserts each element by `message._version`.

200.description: The messages were created or updated.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Possible causes: an unparseable
body or other generic failure (`code: ServerGeneric`); an update
whose `_version` is not the stored version + 1 (`code:
VersionMismatch`); a `start_time` after the `end_time` (`code:
StartTimeAfterEndTime`); or a `_groups` entry referencing a group
(by `reference`) that does not exist (`code: LookupNotFound`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the `system.message` system right
(and is not `system.root`). The response carries
`code: SystemRightRequired` (package `acl`).

get.description:
Returns every stored message (no per-user filter), ordered by
`_id` ascending. Requires the `system.message` system right.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of messages to return. Results are ordered by
`_id` ascending.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of leading messages to skip (for paging).

200.description: Array of messages.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the `system.message` system right
(and is not `system.root`). The response carries
`code: SystemRightRequired` (package `acl`).

/message/{id}.description:
Read or delete a single **fylr** message identified by its `_id`
(a positive integer) in the URL. `GET` returns the message wrapped in a
one-element array; `DELETE` removes it and responds with
`status.acknowledged: ok`. An `_id` that matches no stored message
returns `MessageNotFound` (404). Both operations require the
`system.message` system right (or `system.root`); `DELETE` is allowed
in read-only mode.

get.summary: Retrieve a single message by id.
get.description:
Returns the matching message wrapped in a one-element array (the same
handler shape as the list endpoint), not a bare object.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The message's `_id`.

200.description: A one-element array containing the message.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the `system.message` system right
(and is not `system.root`). The response carries
`code: SystemRightRequired` (package `acl`).

404.description:
The message id in the URL does not match any stored message.
The response carries `code: MessageNotFound` (package
`message`).

200.description: The message was deleted.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the `system.message` system right
(and is not `system.root`). The response carries
`code: SystemRightRequired` (package `acl`).

404.description:
The message id in the URL does not match any stored message.
The response carries `code: MessageNotFound` (package
`message`).

/transitions.description:
Manage workflow **transitions** — rules that fire when objects are
inserted, updated or deleted, gated on the object's tags, and run
actions (set/unset tags, send email, call a webhook, change the
owner, or invoke a plugin callback). This endpoint is the admin
surface: list the defined transitions, replace them. The transitions
themselves are evaluated implicitly during the object save pipeline,
not through a separate endpoint.

Both operations require the `system.tagmanager` system right (a
user holding `system.root` is always granted it). The endpoint
takes no path, query, or header parameters; authentication uses
the standard access token (see the API overview).

Differs from easydb 5: easydb 5 gated only the `POST`, and on the
`system.rights_management` right; **fylr** gates both methods on
`system.tagmanager`. easydb 5 returned `400` when the session was
unauthenticated or lacked the right; **fylr** returns `401`
(`UserRequired`) and `403` (`SystemRightRequired`) respectively.

get.description:
Returns the global workflow transitions (those not bound to a
specific pool or objecttype), ordered by their `position`.
Pool- and objecttype-scoped transitions are excluded; read those
through the corresponding pool / objecttype endpoints.

Requires the `system.tagmanager` system right.

Differs from easydb 5: easydb 5's `GET` needed only an
authenticated session; **fylr** also requires
`system.tagmanager`.

200.description: Array of transition descriptors.

401.description:
No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access
token. Error code `UserRequired`.

403.description:
The user lacks `system.tagmanager` (and is not
`system.root`). Error code `SystemRightRequired`.

post.summary: Replace the full set of transitions.
post.description:
Accepts an array of transition descriptors and replaces the
**global** transition set with them (entries with `_id` are
updated; entries without `_id` are created; transitions that
exist on the server but not in the payload are deleted). A
partial array therefore removes every omitted global
transition. Transitions scoped to a specific pool or objecttype
are not managed here — they are sent through the corresponding
pool / objecttype endpoints.

Requires the `system.tagmanager` system right. Rejected if the
instance is in read-only mode (checked before the right, so a
read-only instance returns `400 ReadOnlyMode` even to a user
without the right).

The response echoes the persisted set, with server-issued `_id`s
filled in. On success a `X-Fylr-Cache-ID` response header carries
the new cache id (format `<startup-unix>-<cache-id>`), bumped after
the transaction commits.

Differs from easydb 5: **fylr** gates this on `system.tagmanager`
(easydb 5 used `system.rights_management`); validation failures
and the read-only rejection surface as `400` with explicit error
`code`s (`ServerGeneric`, `ReadOnlyMode`) rather than easydb 5's
bare `400`.

200.description:
The persisted transition set, in the same shape as `GET
/transitions`. Newly-created entries (sent without `_id`)
come back with their server-issued `_id`. The order matches
the order sent in the request body.

400.description:
Read-only mode is enabled (code `ReadOnlyMode`, checked
before the right and before parsing), or the payload is
invalid. Invalid workflow actions carry their own codes
(package `transition`): a `webhook` action with no webhook
selected (`WebhookNotSelected`), with a `name` not declared
in the base config (`WebhookUnknown`, parameter `name`), or
with an unknown `callback` (not `pre_save` /
`after_commit_async`; `WebhookCallbackUnknown`, parameter
`callback`); a `change_owner` action without an owner
(`ChangeOwnerNotSelected`). Everything else is code
`ServerGeneric`: unparseable JSON; an entry whose `_id`
matches no existing transition in the database; an unknown
transition `type` (not one of `resolve`, `reject`,
`exit_resolve`, `exit_reject`, `process`); an unknown
`operations` value (not `INSERT` / `UPDATE` / `DELETE`); a
`tagfilter:before` / `tagfilter:after` that lists the same
tag id more than once; a `who` entry whose `_basetype` is
neither `user` nor `group`, or that mixes the wrong key for
its basetype; or an action `type` that matches no built-in
(`email`, `set_tags`, `webhook`, `change_owner`) and resolves
to no registered plugin callback.

401.description:
No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access
token. Error code `UserRequired`.

403.description:
The user lacks `system.tagmanager` (and is not
`system.root`). Error code `SystemRightRequired`.

/event.description:
Read, write and subscribe to **events**. fylr writes events for many
server-side mutations (object insert/update, collection changes,
file deletes, email sends, ...). This endpoint exposes the event log.

post.summary: Write a single user event.
post.description:
Allows clients to log user events into the event stream. Any
authenticated user may write event types in the **FRONTEND** and
**PLUGIN** groups (e.g. `SEARCH`, `DETAIL_VIEW`, `FRONTEND_ERROR`).
Writing event types in the system groups (`ADMIN`, `DOWNLOAD`,
`EXPORT`, `INDEX`, `LOGIN_LOGOUT`, `SYSTEM`, `UPLOAD`) requires
`system.root` together with `skip_constraints=true` (see the
`skip_constraints` parameter); any other type — including the
`TASK` group — is always rejected with `code: EventTypeNotAllowed`.
Unlike the other event write operations, `POST /event` is permitted
while the instance is in read-only mode.

Differs from easydb 5: easydb 5 lets a client insert only `SEARCH` and
`DETAIL_VIEW`, and only when the matching base config (`system.log.search`
/ `system.log.detail_view`) is enabled. fylr allows the whole FRONTEND
group (`SEARCH`, `DETAIL_VIEW`, `FRONTEND_ERROR`,
`ASSET_DOWNLOAD_CONFIRMATION_MESSAGE`) and the PLUGIN group with no
base-config gate, plus the system groups via `system.root` +
`skip_constraints`.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description:
If `true`, the event is saved asynchronously: the request returns
`{"background": "ok"}` immediately and per-event validation errors
(e.g. a disallowed `type`) are **not** reported in the response.

parameters.name: skip_constraints
parameters.description:
If `true`, the system event groups (`ADMIN`, `DOWNLOAD`, `EXPORT`,
`INDEX`, `LOGIN_LOGOUT`, `SYSTEM`, `UPLOAD`) may also be written —
not just the FRONTEND/PLUGIN user types — and a top-level `user`
object is accepted on the event. Requires `system.root`; a non-root
caller that sets it receives a `400 ServerGeneric` error.

200.description:
For a synchronous write the stored event is echoed as an
`EventApi`. With `background=true` the body is `{"background":"ok"}`.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Possible `code` values:
`EventTypeNotAllowed` (the payload's `type` is not a FRONTEND/PLUGIN
user type and was not unlocked with root + `skip_constraints`),
`ServerGeneric` (invalid JSON body, an unparseable query filter
such as `id`/`sort`, a non-root caller passing `skip_constraints`,
or an event carrying a `user`/`user_id` without `skip_constraints`),
or `ReadOnlyMode` (the instance is in read-only mode; affects the
write/delete operations only, not `POST /event`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

/event/poll/{fromEventId}.description:
Fetch pollable events with an `_id` greater than `fromEventId`, ordered by
`_id` ascending and capped at `limit`. The request returns immediately —
it does **not** block — and the array is empty when no such event exists.
Requires an authenticated user.

Differs from easydb 5: in easydb 5 the id segment is optional
(`/event/poll[/<last_max_id>]`) and defaults to the session's
`current_max_event_id`. In fylr `fromEventId` is a required path segment;
poll from the start with `0`.

get.summary: Fetch pollable events newer than an event id.
get.description:
Runs a single query for pollable events with `_id` strictly greater
than `fromEventId` (ordered by `_id` ascending, up to `limit` rows) and
returns immediately; the array may be empty. This is a one-shot poll,
not a blocking long-poll — clients repeat the call with the highest
`_id` they have seen. For a continuous push use `GET /event/stream`.

parameters.name: fromEventId
parameters.description:
The last event id the caller has seen. Use `0` to poll from
the very beginning of the pollable event log.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of events to return in this response. Default `25`;
values `<= 0` or `> 1000` are clamped to `1000`.

200.description: Array of new events.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

/event/stream.description:
Subscribe to the event log over a **WebSocket**. The connection is
upgraded and new pollable events are pushed as they happen, one `EventApi`
per JSON text frame, until the socket is closed. Inbound client messages
are discarded. Requires an authenticated user.

get.description:
Upgrades the connection to a **WebSocket** (the handler calls the
gorilla/websocket upgrader) and pushes new pollable events as they
happen. Each event is sent as one JSON text frame carrying the
`EventApi` object. The client should connect with the `ws://`/`wss://`
scheme; any inbound message from the client is discarded.

101.description:
The connection was upgraded to a WebSocket. New events follow as
JSON text frames (one `EventApi` per frame) until the socket is
closed.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

/event/{eventId}.description:
Read or delete a single event identified by its `_id`. `GET` returns the
event as an `EventApi` and requires `system.api.event[get]`; `DELETE`
removes it and requires `system.api.event[delete]`. An unknown id yields
`code: EventNotFound`.

get.summary: Retrieve a single event by id.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The event's `_id`.

200.description: The event.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the system right required for this
operation: `system.api.event[get]` for reads
(`GET /event/{eventId}`, `GET /event/list`) or
`system.api.event[delete]` for deletes
(`DELETE /event/{eventId}`, `DELETE /event/list`). `system.root`
always suffices. There is no per-object ACL on events.

404.description:
No stored event has the id given in the URL. The response carries
`code: EventNotFound`.

delete.summary: Delete a single event by id.
delete.description:
Requires the `system.api.event[delete]` system right (or `system.root`).
Rejected with `400 ReadOnlyMode` while the instance is in read-only mode.

200.description: The event was deleted.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Possible `code` values:
`EventTypeNotAllowed` (the payload's `type` is not a FRONTEND/PLUGIN
user type and was not unlocked with root + `skip_constraints`),
`ServerGeneric` (invalid JSON body, an unparseable query filter
such as `id`/`sort`, a non-root caller passing `skip_constraints`,
or an event carrying a `user`/`user_id` without `skip_constraints`),
or `ReadOnlyMode` (the instance is in read-only mode; affects the
write/delete operations only, not `POST /event`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the system right required for this
operation: `system.api.event[get]` for reads
(`GET /event/{eventId}`, `GET /event/list`) or
`system.api.event[delete]` for deletes
(`DELETE /event/{eventId}`, `DELETE /event/list`). `system.root`
always suffices. There is no per-object ACL on events.

404.description:
No stored event has the id given in the URL. The response carries
`code: EventNotFound`.

/event/list.description:
Query and bulk-manage events. `GET` lists events filtered by query
parameters (`type`, `objecttype`, `user_id`, `date_from`/`date_to`, ...),
paginated and sorted, as `json` or `csv`. `POST` bulk-writes an array of
events; `DELETE` bulk-removes every event matching the same query filters.
Reads need `system.api.event[get]`, deletes need `system.api.event[delete]`.

get.description:
Returns events ordered by `_id` descending by default. The JSON
response is an object `{count, limit, offset, objects[]}`.

Filters and pagination are query parameters. Pagination: `offset`,
`limit`. Output: `format` (`json` (default) or `csv`); `pretty`
(JSON only, default `true`). Filters are matched as **IN** lists
(comma-separated): `id` (id list/ranges, e.g. `1,2-30,4`), `pollable`
(bool), `type`, `basetype`, `objecttype`, `user_id`, `group_id`,
`user_type`, `object_id`, `system_object_id`. Time window:
`date_from`, `date_to` (ISO timestamps, range on `event.timestamp`).

`sort` is a comma-separated list of `field.DIR` (DIR = `ASC`|`DESC`)
over the fields `_id`, `type`, `schema`, `object_id`,
`system_object_id`, `object_version`, `timestamp`,
`user_generated_displayname`.

With `format=csv` the response is `text/csv` and these output options
apply: `csv_delimiter`, `csv_quote`, `csv_escape`, `csv_explode`,
`csv_explode_array_concat`, `csv_max_length`, `cvs_use_bom`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of events to skip.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Response format. `csv` returns `text/csv` instead of JSON.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
Event id filter as a comma-separated list of ids and ranges,
e.g. `1,2-30,4`.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of event `type` values to match.

parameters.name: objecttype
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of objecttype names to match.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of user ids to match.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Lower bound (inclusive) on `event.timestamp`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Upper bound on `event.timestamp`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Comma-separated `field.DIR` ordering (DIR = `ASC`|`DESC`).

200.description:
Object envelope of matching events (JSON format). With
`format=csv` the response is `text/csv` instead.

count.description:
Total number of events matching the filter (before
`limit`/`offset`).

limit.description: Echo of the effective limit (`0` = unlimited).

schema.description:
CSV rendering of the matching events (one row per event;
`info.*` columns when `csv_explode=true`).

400.description:
A query filter could not be parsed (e.g. a malformed `id` range,
an unknown `sort` field/direction, or an unknown `format`). The
response carries `code: ServerGeneric`.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the system right required for this
operation: `system.api.event[get]` for reads
(`GET /event/{eventId}`, `GET /event/list`) or
`system.api.event[delete]` for deletes
(`DELETE /event/{eventId}`, `DELETE /event/list`). `system.root`
always suffices. There is no per-object ACL on events.

post.summary: Bulk-write events.
post.description:
Writes the supplied array of events. Useful for batch tooling. As with
`POST /event`, any authenticated user may write FRONTEND/PLUGIN event
types; the system event groups require `system.root` with
`skip_constraints=true`, and any other type (including `TASK`) is
rejected with `code: EventTypeNotAllowed`. Unlike `POST /event`, this
bulk operation is rejected with `400 ReadOnlyMode` while the instance
is in read-only mode.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description:
If `true`, the events are saved asynchronously: the request returns
`{"background": "ok"}` immediately and per-event validation errors
(e.g. a disallowed `type`) are **not** reported in the response.

parameters.name: skip_constraints
parameters.description:
If `true`, the system event groups (`ADMIN`, `DOWNLOAD`, `EXPORT`,
`INDEX`, `LOGIN_LOGOUT`, `SYSTEM`, `UPLOAD`) may also be written —
not just the FRONTEND/PLUGIN user types — and a top-level `user`
object is accepted on each event. Requires `system.root`; a non-root
caller that sets it receives a `400 ServerGeneric` error.

200.description:
For a synchronous write the stored events are echoed as an array of
`EventApi`. With `background=true` the body is `{"background":"ok"}`.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Possible `code` values:
`EventTypeNotAllowed` (the payload's `type` is not a FRONTEND/PLUGIN
user type and was not unlocked with root + `skip_constraints`),
`ServerGeneric` (invalid JSON body, an unparseable query filter
such as `id`/`sort`, a non-root caller passing `skip_constraints`,
or an event carrying a `user`/`user_id` without `skip_constraints`),
or `ReadOnlyMode` (the instance is in read-only mode; affects the
write/delete operations only, not `POST /event`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

delete.description:
Deletes every event matching the supplied filters. The filters are
read from the **query string** — the same `EventListQuery` as
`GET /event/list` (commonly `id=...`, plus `type`, `date_from`,
`date_to`, `user_id`, ...); there is **no request body**. With no
filters the match set is the entire event log, so deletion is
unbounded. Requires the `system.api.event[delete]` system right
(or `system.root`), and is rejected with `400 ReadOnlyMode` while the
instance is in read-only mode.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
Event id filter as a comma-separated list of ids and ranges,
e.g. `1,2-30,4`. The matched events are deleted.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of matched events to delete.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of event `type` values to match.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Lower bound (inclusive) on `event.timestamp`.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Upper bound on `event.timestamp`.

200.description: The matched events were deleted.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Possible `code` values:
`EventTypeNotAllowed` (the payload's `type` is not a FRONTEND/PLUGIN
user type and was not unlocked with root + `skip_constraints`),
`ServerGeneric` (invalid JSON body, an unparseable query filter
such as `id`/`sort`, a non-root caller passing `skip_constraints`,
or an event carrying a `user`/`user_id` without `skip_constraints`),
or `ReadOnlyMode` (the instance is in read-only mode; affects the
write/delete operations only, not `POST /event`).

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the system right required for this
operation: `system.api.event[get]` for reads
(`GET /event/{eventId}`, `GET /event/list`) or
`system.api.event[delete]` for deletes
(`DELETE /event/{eventId}`, `DELETE /event/list`). `system.root`
always suffices. There is no per-object ACL on events.

/export.description:
Manage **exports** — long-running jobs that bundle objects and their
files into a downloadable archive (TAR.GZ or ZIP). All `/export`
endpoints require an authenticated user (a valid access token, sent as
`Authorization: Bearer`, `X-Fylr-Authorization: Bearer`, or the
`access_token` query parameter); there is no dedicated system right for
the feature. Exports are user-scoped: each export records the `user_id`
that saved it, and the handlers permit access only to that owner or to a
`system.root` user.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return. Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Compact filter expression. Comma-separates ORs, plus-separates
ANDs within an OR, colon separates field from value, pipe
separates allowed values. Example:
`type:download+status:failed|done_with_warnings,type:export|export_incremental`.
Only the fields `type` and `status` are filterable; any other
field name returns a 400 `Unknown column`. `type` accepts
`export` / `export_incremental` / `download`; `status` accepts
the export states (`new`, `pending`, `processing`, `done`,
`done_with_warnings`, `failed`).

Differs from easydb 5: fylr adds the run state
`done_with_warnings`; easydb 5 documents only `new`, `pending`,
`processing`, `failed`, and `done`.

200.description: A page of the user's exports.

400.description:
The `filter` references a field other than `type` or `status`
(message `Unknown column "<field>"`), or no user is authenticated.
Both surface as code `ServerGeneric` (package `ferrors`). This
route never returns `403`: it lists only the caller's own exports
(`user_id = <caller>`).

post.summary: Create a new export.
post.description:
Saves a new export owned by the authenticated user (the stored
`user_id` is always set to the caller, regardless of any value in the
body). There is no owner check on this route.

200.description: The created export.

400.description:
The export could not be saved — e.g. a missing `search`, an
unknown `type` or `mapping`, a non-`.xsl`/`.xslt` attached file,
or, on update, a stale `version`. Most of these surface as code
`ServerGeneric` (package `ferrors`); a version conflict surfaces
as `VersionMismatch` (package `ferrors`).

409.description:
Another export owned by the same user already uses the
requested `name` (code `ExportDuplicateName`, package
`export`).

put.summary: Create a new export (alias for POST).

200.description: The created export.

400.description:
The export could not be saved — e.g. a missing `search`, an
unknown `type` or `mapping`, a non-`.xsl`/`.xslt` attached file,
or, on update, a stale `version`. Most of these surface as code
`ServerGeneric` (package `ferrors`); a version conflict surfaces
as `VersionMismatch` (package `ferrors`).

409.description:
Another export owned by the same user already uses the
requested `name` (code `ExportDuplicateName`, package
`export`).

/export/{exportId}.description:
Read, update, or delete a single export identified by `exportId`.
`GET` returns the export (rendering detail controlled by the `format`
query), `POST` updates its configuration, and `DELETE` removes it. `GET`
and `DELETE` enforce the owner / `system.root` check; `POST` does not —
it re-saves the export with `user_id` set to the caller. `DELETE` is
refused in read-only mode; `POST` is allowed in read-only mode.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description:
Rendering detail level. Defaults to `long` when omitted. An
unrecognised value yields a 400 error.

200.description: The export.

400.description:
The `exportId` in the URL matches no stored export. `LoadById`
fails with message `export <id> not found`, returned as code
`ServerGeneric` (package `ferrors`).

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

post.summary: Update an existing export.
post.description:
Re-saves the export. The request body must carry the export's `id`
(matching the URL) and the next `version` (the stored version plus
one); a stale `version` is rejected with a 400 `VersionMismatch`. The
saved `user_id` is set to the caller, and there is no separate owner
check on this route. Returns 409 if the new `name` collides with
another export owned by the same user.

200.description: The updated export.

400.description:
The export could not be saved — e.g. a missing `search`, an
unknown `type` or `mapping`, a non-`.xsl`/`.xslt` attached file,
or, on update, a stale `version`. Most of these surface as code
`ServerGeneric` (package `ferrors`); a version conflict surfaces
as `VersionMismatch` (package `ferrors`).

409.description:
Another export owned by the same user already uses the
requested `name` (code `ExportDuplicateName`, package
`export`).

200.description: The export was deleted.

400.description:
Either the server is in read-only mode, in which case exports
cannot be deleted (code `ReadOnlyMode`, package `ferrors`), or
the export id in the URL matches no stored export (code
`ServerGeneric`, package `ferrors`).

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

/export/{exportId}/start.description:
Schedule a production run for the export identified by `exportId` and
return the export. Permitted even when the server is in read-only
mode so existing archives stay downloadable.

post.summary: Start (or restart) the export's generation.
post.description:
Schedules a production run for the export. Permitted even when the
server is in read-only mode, so downloads keep working.

200.description: The export is scheduled.

400.description:
The `exportId` in the URL matches no stored export. `LoadById`
fails with message `export <id> not found`, returned as code
`ServerGeneric` (package `ferrors`).

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

/export/{exportId}/stop.description:
Signal the running export identified by `exportId` to stop. The
request waits up to 4 seconds for the export to reach state `failed`,
then returns the export.

post.summary: Stop a running export.
post.description:
Signals the export to stop and waits up to 4 seconds for it to
reach state `failed`, then returns the export (via the same
rendering as `GET /export/{exportId}`). The export must be in state
`processing`: stopping one in any other state fails with a 400
(`unable to stop export in status ...`, code `ServerGeneric`). If the
export does not reach `failed` within 4 seconds, the request fails
with a 400 (`failed to stop export after 4 seconds`, code
`ServerGeneric`). This route is permitted even in read-only mode.

200.description:
The export reached state `failed` after the stop signal; the
export is returned.

400.description:
The `exportId` in the URL matches no stored export. `LoadById`
fails with message `export <id> not found`, returned as code
`ServerGeneric` (package `ferrors`).

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

/export/{exportId}/tar_gz/.description:
Download the TAR.GZ archive produced for the export identified by
`exportId`. As a `PathPrefix` mount, a path appended after `tar_gz/`
serves an individual file extracted from the archive.

Differs from easydb 5: fylr's TAR route is `tar_gz/` and streams a
gzip-compressed TAR. easydb 5's anonymous archive download serves a
plain `.tar`, and its `packer` config offers `zip`, `zip_10mb`,
`zip_2gb`, and `tar_bz2` (BZip2) — there is no gzip-TAR option.

get.summary: Download the generated TAR.GZ archive (or a path inside it).
get.description:
Returns the bytes of the export's TAR.GZ archive. The handler also
accepts deeper paths (PathPrefix mount) — those serve individual
files extracted from the archive.

200.description: The archive (or selected entry) bytes.

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

400.description:
The `exportId` matches no stored export, so streaming never
starts (code `ServerGeneric`, package `ferrors`). Note that once
the archive stream has begun (HTTP `200` already sent), per-file
production failures are NOT reported as a JSON error: the ZIP
handler embeds the error as an entry inside the archive, and the
TAR.GZ handler aborts the stream with an inline `500`.

/export/{exportId}/zip/.description:
Download the ZIP archive produced for the export identified by
`exportId`. As a `PathPrefix` mount, a path appended after `zip/`
serves an individual file extracted from the archive.

get.summary: Download the generated ZIP archive (or a path inside it).
get.description:
Returns the bytes of the export's ZIP archive. The handler also
accepts deeper paths (PathPrefix mount) — those serve individual
files extracted from the archive.

200.description: The archive (or selected entry) bytes.

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

400.description:
The `exportId` matches no stored export, so streaming never
starts (code `ServerGeneric`, package `ferrors`). Note that once
the archive stream has begun (HTTP `200` already sent), per-file
production failures are NOT reported as a JSON error: the ZIP
handler embeds the error as an entry inside the archive, and the
TAR.GZ handler aborts the stream with an inline `500`.

/export/{exportId}/uuid/{uuid}/tar_gz/.description:
Download the TAR.GZ archive produced for a specific run `uuid` of the
export identified by `exportId`, rather than the most recent run. As a
`PathPrefix` mount, a path appended after `tar_gz/` serves an
individual file extracted from the archive.

get.summary: Download the TAR.GZ produced for a specific export run UUID.
get.description:
Same as `/export/{exportId}/tar_gz/` but addresses a particular
production run identified by `uuid`. Useful for clients that need
to download a specific historical artifact, not just the most
recent one.

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: The export run UUID.

200.description: The archive (or selected entry) bytes.

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

400.description:
The `exportId` matches no stored export, so streaming never
starts (code `ServerGeneric`, package `ferrors`). Note that once
the archive stream has begun (HTTP `200` already sent), per-file
production failures are NOT reported as a JSON error: the ZIP
handler embeds the error as an entry inside the archive, and the
TAR.GZ handler aborts the stream with an inline `500`.

/export/{exportId}/uuid/{uuid}/zip/.description:
Download the ZIP archive produced for a specific run `uuid` of the
export identified by `exportId`, rather than the most recent run. As a
`PathPrefix` mount, a path appended after `zip/` serves an individual
file extracted from the archive.

get.summary: Download the ZIP produced for a specific export run UUID.

200.description: The archive (or selected entry) bytes.

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

400.description:
The `exportId` matches no stored export, so streaming never
starts (code `ServerGeneric`, package `ferrors`). Note that once
the archive stream has begun (HTTP `200` already sent), per-file
production failures are NOT reported as a JSON error: the ZIP
handler embeds the error as an entry inside the archive, and the
TAR.GZ handler aborts the stream with an inline `500`.

/export/{exportId}/file/.description:
Access the individual produced files of the export identified by
`exportId`. As a `PathPrefix` mount, an appended file path returns
that file's bytes, while an empty path or one ending in `/` renders an
HTML directory index of the export's files. `GET` and `HEAD` are
supported.

get.summary: Download a single file from the export, or list the export's files.
get.description:
PathPrefix mount. With a file path appended, returns that file's
bytes. With an empty path or a path ending in `/`, renders an HTML
directory index of the export's produced files (the `exportIndex`
page). `HEAD` is also accepted and returns the headers only, no
body.

200.description:
The file bytes (when a file path is appended) or the HTML file
index (when the path is empty or ends in `/`).

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

400.description:
The requested file could not be produced: e.g. the referenced
file is not yet in status `done` (`FileNotDone`), a file version
or group is unavailable (`FileVersionNotAvailable`,
`FileGroupNotAvailable`), an api-object path is invalid
(`ApiObjectPath`), or deep-link access is disabled
(`DeepLinkAccessByIdDisabled`, `DeepLinkAccessByColumnDisabled`).
All carry package `export`.

404.description:
The requested file does not exist in the export (code
`FileNotFound`, package `export`). `parameters.file` carries
the requested path.

head.summary: Headers for a single export file (or the file index).
head.description: Same routing as the `GET` form but returns headers only, no body.

200.description: Headers for the file or the file index; no body.

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

/export/{exportId}/uuid/{uuid}/file/.description:
Access the individual produced files of a specific run `uuid` of the
export identified by `exportId`. When `uuid` matches one of the
export's transport UUIDs, access is granted via that transport
(deep-link style) instead of the owner check. Behaves like
`/export/{exportId}/file/` otherwise; `GET` and `HEAD` are supported.

Differs from easydb 5: fylr grants unauthenticated download access
through `/export/{exportId}/uuid/{uuid}/...`, where `uuid` is a
transport UUID. easydb 5 instead exposes
`/export/<export-id>/download/<secret-passkey>/<name>.zip` (and
`.tar`); fylr has no `/download/<passkey>/` route.

get.summary: Download a single file (or list files) for a specific export run UUID.
get.description:
Same as `/export/{exportId}/file/` but addresses a particular
production run identified by `uuid`. When `uuid` matches one of the
export's transport UUIDs, access is granted via that transport
(deep-link style) instead of the owner check. `HEAD` is also
accepted.

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: The export run / transport UUID.

200.description:
The file bytes (when a file path is appended) or the HTML file
index (when the path is empty or ends in `/`).

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

400.description:
The requested file could not be produced: e.g. the referenced
file is not yet in status `done` (`FileNotDone`), a file version
or group is unavailable (`FileVersionNotAvailable`,
`FileGroupNotAvailable`), an api-object path is invalid
(`ApiObjectPath`), or deep-link access is disabled
(`DeepLinkAccessByIdDisabled`, `DeepLinkAccessByColumnDisabled`).
All carry package `export`.

404.description:
The requested file does not exist in the export (code
`FileNotFound`, package `export`). `parameters.file` carries
the requested path.

head.summary: Headers for a single export file (or the file index) for a run UUID.
head.description: Same routing as the `GET` form but returns headers only, no body.

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: The export run / transport UUID.

200.description: Headers for the file or the file index; no body.

403.description:
The authenticated user is neither the export's owner (matched by
`user_id`) nor a `system.root` user. The response carries code
`InsufficientRights` (package `api`); `parameters.resource` is the
literal `Export` here (the `DELETE` handler uses `export-{id}`).
A request with no authenticated user also lands here, except for
create / update / list, which surface a generic `400` instead.

/publish.description:
Record **publishings** of fylr objects in external systems.
**fylr has no publishing layer of its own** — the publication
itself (for example a DataCite DOI, an external gallery page or a
share link) is produced and hosted elsewhere. A publish entry is
fylr's record of such a publication: it ties an object's
`system_object_id` to the **collector** it was published through
(a target configured in the base config, e.g. `datacite`) and to
the external `publish_uri` where the publication lives, plus a
deep-link back into fylr (`easydb_uri`). These endpoints create,
list and delete those records — they do not generate or serve the
published artifacts.

post.summary: Record one or more publishings.
post.description:
The payload is an array of publish descriptors. Requires the
`system.api.publish[post]` system right and a non-read-only
instance.

A descriptor that carries an existing `_id` **updates** that
publish row in place; a descriptor without `_id` (or with `_id`
`0`) inserts a new one. Both kinds may be mixed in one request.

Differs from easydb 5: easydb 5 documents this endpoint as
insert-only — "Only new publishing can be done using this API.
There is no possibility to update an existing objects." (DELETE +
re-POST is the documented way to change a publication there). fylr
additionally updates the existing row when a descriptor's `_id` is
set.

200.description: The created publish entries.

400.description:
The publication could not be created. Returned as a
`PublishError` (`package: object`, `statuscode: 400`) when a
descriptor is invalid: `PublishUnknownCollector`,
`PublishInvalidData`, `PublishDeletedObject`. Returned as the
standard `Error` envelope (`package: ferrors`) when the
instance is in **read-only** mode (`ReadOnlyMode`, message
`Read-only mode is enabled.` — this check runs before the
