/task/modules.description:
List the available task modules. Each entry reports a module `name` (the
value a task references to select its runner), a localised `displayname`
and `description`, a `debug` flag, and the `params` definition describing
the parameters that module accepts.

The modules are built into **fylr**, they cannot be added by plugins. The
`task_params` of a task must match the `params` definition of its module:

### `consolidate_objects`

Re-links all references to the source objects to one target object, then
deletes the sources. Use this to merge duplicate linked objects (e.g.
keywords, actors, places). Bidirectional links are honored: re-pointing
one updates the mirrored entry on the target and removes the stale
mirror from a source that is kept.

* `source_system_object_ids` (**int-list**, required): system object ids
  of the objects to consolidate. All link references to them (in any link
  field, including nested) are re-pointed to the target. A nested row
  whose link would duplicate a sibling row already linking the target is
  dropped — unless the row carries other data, then it is kept and
  re-pointed. The target must not be part of the sources, duplicate ids
  are rejected.
* `target_system_object_id` (**int**, required, min. `1`): system object
  id of the object the sources are consolidated into. It must have the
  same objecttype as the sources; sources of another objecttype are
  skipped with a warning. The target must not itself link any of the
  sources (the consolidation would link it to itself): such a task is
  rejected at save time with `ConsolidateTargetLinksSources`.
* `dry_run` (**bool**, default `true`): only log what the task would do,
  change nothing.
* `delete_sources` (**bool**, default `true`): delete each source after
  re-linking (delete policy `setnull`, the sources can be restored from
  the trash).

### `delete_objects`

Deletes (or restores) the objects found by the task search.

* `search` (**json**, required): a **fylr** search request which finds the
  objects to process. Set `incremental` to `true` to only process objects
  modified since the last task run.
* `delete_policy` (**select**, default `setnull`): one of `setnull`
  (unlink the deleted objects in other objects), `remove` (also delete
  subordinate or reverse-linked objects), `purge` (purge objects from the
  trash), `undelete` (restore objects from the trash).

### `metadata`

Applies mapped file metadata to the objects found by the task search.

* `search` (**json**, required): a **fylr** search request which finds the
  objects to process.
* `create_object` (**json**): objecttype/mask configuration for objects
  created from metadata.
* `set_unset_tags` (**json**): tags to set or remove on the processed
  objects, format: `{ "tags": [ { "_id": <tag id>, "set": <bool> } ] }`.
* `overwrite_values` (**bool**): overwrite existing values with mapped
  metadata.

### `search`

Counts the objects found by the task search.

* `search` (**json**, required): a **fylr** search request.

### `set_unset_tags`

Sets and removes tags on the objects found by the task search.

* `search` (**json**, required): a **fylr** search request which finds the
  objects to process. Set `incremental` to `true` to only process objects
  modified since the last task run.
* `set_unset_tags` (**json**, required): the tags to set or remove,
  format: `{ "tags": [ { "_id": <tag id>, "set": <bool> } ] }`.

### `sleep`

Does nothing but sleep, for development and testing (`debug` is set).

* `runtime` (**int**): seconds to sleep.
* `secret` (**secret**): unused, for testing.
* `input` (**text**, default `default test string`): unused, for testing.

200.description: Retrieve a list of available task modules.

name.description: Name of the module.

debug.description:
If set, this module is meant for developement, testing,
debug purposes only. Frontends are advised to not expose
this module publicly.

items.description: Each item describes a config setting.

name.description: The unique name of this config

group.description: The **group** sets the tab this config variable is in.

require_system_right.description: System right which this config requires, e.g. "system.root" if it requires root rights.

unauthenticated_visible.description: If true, this config is visible to unauthenticated sessions (access token with no user).

unavailable.description:
If true, the feature this section configures is not available on
this instance (e.g. not included in the license). Frontends should
render the section greyed out but visible. Computed per request;
omitted when false.

401.description: No authenticated user. The request must carry a valid access token.

403.description:
The authenticated user lacks the `system.task` system right (and is
not `system.root`). Raised with code `SystemRightRequired`
(`parameters.rights` lists the rights that would have satisfied the
check).

/oauth2/auth.description:
OAuth2 / OpenID Connect **authorization endpoint** (RFC 6749 §3.1).
This is where the browser web-app logs a user in. It authenticates
the end user — by password (`auth_method=easydb`, the default; also
`ldap`, `email`, `collection`), anonymously (`anonymous`, when guest
login is enabled), via SAML, or with an action code — and returns an
authorization response (an authorization `code`, `token` or
`id_token`) by redirecting back to the client's `redirect_uri`.

Served at `/api/oauth2/auth` — **not** under `/api/v1`. No prior
access token is required.

**Stay logged in:** opt-in per base config *Login* →
`keep_login` (`off` by default, or a session lifetime of 7 or 30
days). When enabled, the login form offers a "Stay logged in"
checkbox (`keep_login=1`); a successful login — including a passed
two-factor challenge — then additionally sets the persistent,
HttpOnly `fylr-keep-login` browser cookie. A later request to this
endpoint without credentials silently re-authorizes the cookie's
user (no login form, a fresh authorization response;
`authenticated.method` of such a session reports `keep_login`). The
lifetime slides on each use; `/logout` revokes the server-side
session and expires the cookie. When the option is `off`,
`keep_login=1` is ignored and existing cookies are dropped.

**Session binding (anti-hijacking):** an access/refresh token issued
to the browser here is bound to the browser's long-lived, HttpOnly
`fylr-browser-id` cookie, and the keep-login cookie above is bound the
same way. That cookie must then accompany the `Authorization: Bearer`
token on every API request; a token — or a keep-login cookie —
presented from a different browser, i.e. without the matching cookie,
is rejected as invalid (`InvalidToken`), so a stolen bearer token on
its own cannot be replayed. Binding is silent and always on. A login
whose token is consumed on a **different origin** than this **fylr** —
a third-party OAuth client (a cross-origin `redirect_uri`) or a
cross-server *webOnly* frontend (a cross-origin `state` redirect) — is
issued **unbound** instead, since that origin's requests would not
carry the cookie.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: The OAuth2 client identifier.

parameters.name: response_type
parameters.description:
The OAuth2/OIDC response type. Combined types such as
`code id_token` are also supported.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description:
Where to redirect with the result. Must match the client
registration.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description:
Space-separated scopes, within the client's allowed set (a
client that configures none may use all of `read`, `write`,
`openid`, `offline`). Their effect in fylr:

  * `openid` — also issue an OpenID Connect `id_token` (an
    RS256 JWT) alongside the access token. (The
    `/api/oauth2/userinfo` endpoint itself accepts any valid
    access token and is not gated on this scope.)
  * `offline` — request a refresh token. A refresh token is
    issued (for the authorization-code and password grants)
    only when this scope is granted, and granting it also
    applies the configured refresh-token expiration override.
    fylr accepts both `offline` and the OIDC-standard
    `offline_access` for this.
  * `read`, `write` — capability labels only; they are **not**
    consulted to authorize API calls. Actual API authorization
    uses fylr's per-user/per-pool ACL rights, which are
    independent of these OAuth2 scopes despite the matching
    names.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque value echoed back to the client.

parameters.name: code_challenge
parameters.description: PKCE code challenge.

parameters.name: auth_method
parameters.description:
fylr login method (default `easydb`). The endpoint accepts a
comma-separated list and tries the methods in order; the
web-app sends a single value. Each method authenticates a
specific user type:

  * `easydb` — `login` + `password` against the fylr user
    database (user types `easydb`, `system`,
    `easydb_self_register`). Also the fallback when
    `auth_method` is set to an unrecognised value. (An empty
    `auth_method` parses to `auto`, not `easydb`.)
  * `ldap` — `login` + `password` bound against a configured
    LDAP directory; provisions/updates an `ldap` user. Only
    available when an LDAP connection is configured and the
    license permits external auth.
  * `email` — passwordless login for invited `email` users:
    `login` plus the account UUID (sent in the `password`
    field) — the UUID is the secret.
  * `collection` — `login` + `password` for `collection` user
    accounts.
  * `saml` — SAML 2.0 SSO; no credentials in the form. The
    first call redirects to the IdP, then the returned
    assertion provisions/updates an `sso` user. Only available
    when SAML is configured and the license permits external
    auth.
  * `action_code` — a one-time `secret` + `action_code` pair
    used by internal redirect flows (forced password change,
    system messages); logs in whichever user the code was
    issued for. Single-use.
  * `anonymous` — guest login, no credentials; binds to a
    per-browser anonymous user. Only when guest login is
    enabled (base config `guest`).
  * `auto` — server-side selector (the web-app default):
    resolves to `anonymous` when guests are allowed and no
    `login` is supplied, otherwise to password login
    (`easydb`, then `ldap` when configured).

parameters.name: login
parameters.description: User login (for password methods).

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password (for password methods).

302.description:
Redirect to the client `redirect_uri` carrying the
authorization `code`, `token` or `id_token` (per
`response_type`). May instead redirect (`303`) to an
interstitial page: two-factor authentication for enforced
users — `/api/page/twoFactor` (enter the email / authenticator
code), `/api/page/twoFactorChoose` (method picker),
`/api/page/twoFactorSetup` or `/api/page/twoFactorSetupPasskey`
(first-time enrollment) — or `/api/page/setPassword` (forced
password change) or `/api/page/showMessage` (pending tasks).
After a passed challenge the page redirects back here with
`auth_method=action_code` to complete the original request
without re-entering the password.

200.description:
The HTML login form, rendered when credentials are absent or
invalid (with a generic `server.oauth2.LoginFailed` message).

post.summary: Submit the login form / authorization request.
post.description:
Same as `GET`, but the parameters are sent as an
`application/x-www-form-urlencoded` body — used when the login
form is submitted.

keep_login.description:
The "Stay logged in" checkbox. `1` mints the persistent
`fylr-keep-login` cookie after a fully completed login
(see the endpoint description). Ignored unless the base
config enables the feature (*Login* → `keep_login`).

302.description: Redirect to `redirect_uri` with the authorization result.

200.description: HTML login form (on missing/invalid credentials).

/oauth2/token.description:
OAuth2 **token endpoint** (RFC 6749 §3.2). Exchanges an
authorization code, resource-owner password credentials, client
credentials, or a refresh token for an access token (and, for the
`openid` scope, an `id_token`). Access tokens are bearer tokens; the
lifetime is 24 hours by default and can be overridden by the base
config (`oauth2` access-token expiration).

A token minted here is bound to the `fylr-browser-id` cookie when the
request carries one (see the *Session binding* note on the
authorization endpoint); the password and client-credentials grants
normally send no cookie and so receive **unbound** tokens that work
without it.

Served at `/api/oauth2/token` — **not** under `/api/v1`.

post.summary: Issue an access token.
post.description:
`application/x-www-form-urlencoded` request. Confidential clients
authenticate with `client_id`/`client_secret` (form fields or HTTP
Basic); public clients send only `client_id`. For the resource-owner
password grant fylr validates `username`/`password` against the
easydb, collection, email, then LDAP login methods in turn.

**Two-factor authentication:** the password grant cannot present a
second factor, so for users who are required to use 2FA it is
refused with `invalid_grant` (indistinguishable from a wrong
password) — unless the base config deliberately allows it
(*Two-factor authentication* → *Allow password grant*).
`system:root` is always exempt.

code.description: Authorization code (`grant_type=authorization_code`).

200.description: The issued token.

expires_in.description:
Seconds until the access token expires (86400 by
default; configurable in the base config).

id_token.description: JWT id_token (RS256), present for the `openid` scope.

400.description:
RFC 6749 error response (`invalid_request`, `invalid_grant`,
`unsupported_grant_type`, `invalid_client`, ...). fylr's
internal error is appended to `error_description`.

/oauth2/revoke.description:
OAuth2 **token revocation** endpoint (RFC 7009). Revokes an access
or refresh token; the web-app uses it to log out, and a
`USER_LOGOUT` audit event is written for the token's user. Per
RFC 7009 it returns `200` even for an unknown token.

Served at `/api/oauth2/revoke` — **not** under `/api/v1`.

post.summary: Revoke a token (logout).

token.description: The access or refresh token to revoke.

200.description: The token was revoked (also returned for an unknown token).

/oauth2/introspect.description:
OAuth2 **token introspection** endpoint (RFC 7662). Returns the
active state and metadata of a token. The caller must be an
authorized client.

Served at `/api/oauth2/introspect` — **not** under `/api/v1`.

post.summary: Introspect a token.

200.description:
Introspection result. `active` is `false` for an unknown,
expired or revoked token; the other fields are present only
when the token is active.

/oauth2/userinfo.description:
OpenID Connect **UserInfo** endpoint. Returns claims about the user
identified by the presented bearer access token, filtered by the
instance's `userinfo` allowlist. `sub` is always returned; every
other claim appears only when its name is in the allowlist.

The allowlist is configured in the base config under
`openid.userinfo` — a list of claim names (for example `email`,
`displayname`, `first_name`), bound to the internal `OpenIdUserInfo`
setting. The same list drives `claims_supported` in the discovery
document.

Served at `/api/oauth2/userinfo` — **not** under `/api/v1`. Like all
`/api/oauth2` and `/api/v1` routes, the request runs through the
`setSessionFromToken` middleware, which accepts the access token via
the `Authorization: Bearer` header, the `X-Fylr-Authorization` header
(a Safari workaround) or the `access_token` query parameter.
`userinfo` is the only OAuth2 endpoint that consumes the resulting
session user; the other endpoints parse the token themselves from the
form body for their RFC operations.

get.summary: Get claims for the access-token user.

200.description: The user's OpenID claims.

400.description:
No valid user could be resolved from the token. Code
`InvalidToken` (`realm: api`).

post.summary: Get claims for the access-token user (form POST).

200.description: The user's OpenID claims.

/.well-known/openid-configuration.description:
OpenID Connect **discovery document** (provider metadata). A public,
unauthenticated document advertising fylr's OAuth2 / OpenID Connect
issuer and endpoints so that OIDC clients can configure themselves.

Served at the server root (`/.well-known/openid-configuration`) —
**not** under `/api/v1`. The endpoint URLs it returns are absolute,
built from the instance's configured external URL
(`fylr.externalURL`), and point at `/api/oauth2/*`.

The route is not method-restricted: any HTTP method returns the same
metadata document with status `200`.

get.summary: Fetch the OpenID Connect provider metadata.

200.description: The discovery document.

claims_supported.description:
The claim names `userinfo` may return — always `sub`,
plus every claim enabled in the base config's
`openid.userinfo` list (`email_verified` is included
only when `email` is enabled).

/inspect/.description:
**Instance overview** — the /inspect home dashboard. Summarises the running
instance: database, indexer, settings, index names, connected storage
backends and license.

> **Access.** `/inspect` is fylr's root-only operations console. On the
> public **webapp port** it is reverse-proxied behind a session check and
> requires the **`system.root`** right; on the **backend port** it is mounted
> with no authentication and must therefore stay on a private network. It is
> **not** part of the token-authenticated `/api/v1` surface, so the security
> schemes of this document do not apply.

BearerAuth.description:
Access token in the `Authorization` header:
`Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

AccessTokenQuery.name: access_token
AccessTokenQuery.description:
Access token as the `access_token` query parameter — handy for
links and downloads; prefer the `Authorization` header otherwise.

FylrAuthHeader.name: X-Fylr-Authorization
FylrAuthHeader.description:
`Bearer <token>` in the `X-Fylr-Authorization` header — a
fallback for Safari, which overwrites `Authorization`.

UserInfo.description:
OpenID Connect claims for the authenticated user, returned by
`GET`/`POST` `/api/oauth2/userinfo`. `sub` is always present; every
other claim is returned only when its name is listed in the instance's
`userinfo` allowlist (base-config `OpenIdUserInfo`).

sub.description:
Subject identifier — the user's login, or email, or the numeric
user id if neither is set. Always present, not gated by the
allowlist.

email.description:
Email address. An empty string when the user has none (but the
`email` claim is enabled).

email_verified.description:
Whether the email address has been confirmed. Returned alongside
`email`.

ExportVersion.description:
One rendition-selection entry. Used both as an element of
`export.classes.<class>[]` and of `export.assets.<file-id>.versions`.
Each entry produces one file in the export for every matching source
file. Validated by `exportVersions.ApiToObject`
(internal/api/export/export_api.go:153-207).

type.description:
Which rendition of the file to export.
`original` — the root/original file (resolves to the original
even when the current value is a produced version).
`current` — the file value as currently set.
`version` — a named produced version; requires `version`.
`custom` — an on-the-fly rendition described by `custom` or, by
name, by `custom_version_preset` (image class only).

metadata.description:
Metadata-embedding rule for the exported file. One of the
strings `standard` (apply the object's standard image-export
metadata mapping), `keep` (leave embedded metadata untouched),
`remove` (strip IPTC / XMP / Photoshop tags via exiftool), or an
integer mapping-profile id (a custom metadata mapping).
`null` / omitted is treated as `standard`. Any other value is
rejected.

version.description:
Name of the produced version to export. Required when
`type` = `version`; matched against the file's available child
versions by name. Ignored for other `type` values.

custom_version_preset.description:
Name of a custom version preset configured in the base config
block `custom_version_presets`. An alternative to an inline
`custom` object when `type` = `custom`: the server resolves the
preset to its options. Set either `custom` or
`custom_version_preset`, not both; an unknown name is rejected.
The produced file is named after the preset instead of `custom`.

ExportCustomVersion.description:
Parameters for an on-the-fly custom rendition (only meaningful when
the enclosing version's `type` = `custom`, and only for files of
class `image`). Drives the internal `_produce:produce` recipe at
delivery time.

watermark.description: Whether to apply a watermark to the rendition.

quality.description:
Output quality. Either a keyword or a numeric string `1`-`100`.
Keywords map to: `low`/`small` = 50, `medium` = 75, `high` = 85,
`maximum` = 100. A numeric value must be an integer in the range
1..100 (`>0` and `<=100`); `0`, negative, `>100` or non-numeric
values are rejected. An empty string is allowed and skipped.
Validated via `baseconfig.QualityAsInt`.

format.description:
Output image format / file extension. `keep` or empty keeps the
source extension; any other value becomes the produced file's
extension and target format. NOT validated server-side — passed
through to the produce recipe. Common values listed in `enum`.

colorspace.description:
Target colorspace passed to the produce recipe (ImageMagick
colorspace name, e.g. `sRGB`, `RGB`, `Gray`, `CMYK`). `keep`
(or empty) leaves the colorspace unchanged. NOT enum-validated
server-side.

colorprofile.description:
Name of an ICC color profile to assign. Validated at save time
against the available color profiles (the built-in `.icc`
profiles shipped with fylr plus any customer-configured /
uploaded profiles); an unknown name is rejected. `""` or `"-"`
means no profile. Because customer profiles are dynamic this is
not a fixed enum; the built-in profiles are listed below.

size.description:
Resize strategy. Only `custom` activates custom resizing (then
`custom_size` and the `custom_*_pixel` fields apply). `keep`
leaves the size unchanged.

custom_size.description:
Custom-size mode (applies when `size` = `custom`). Validated
against the known dimensions; an unsupported value is rejected.
`dimension_max` / `dimension_min` / `width` / `height` use
`custom_size_pixel` as the single bound (longest side, shortest
side, width, or height). `cover` / `contain` use
`custom_width_pixel` + `custom_height_pixel` as a target box.

dpi.description: Output DPI passed to the produce recipe.

custom_size_pixel.description:
Single pixel bound used when `custom_size` is `dimension_max`,
`dimension_min`, `width`, or `height`.

custom_width_pixel.description:
Target width in pixels used when `custom_size` is `cover` or
`contain`. Swapped with `custom_height_pixel` when
`transform.rotate-z` is 90 or 270.

custom_height_pixel.description:
Target height in pixels used when `custom_size` is `cover` or
`contain`. Swapped with `custom_width_pixel` when
`transform.rotate-z` is 90 or 270.

ExportTransform.description:
Geometric transform applied to a custom rendition. Passed to the
produce recipe.

rotate-x.description:
A non-zero value mirrors the image on the x axis. Mutually
exclusive with a non-zero `rotate-y`.

rotate-y.description:
A non-zero value mirrors the image on the y axis. Mutually
exclusive with a non-zero `rotate-x`.

rotate-z.description:
In-plane rotation in degrees. Values 90 and 270 swap width and
height for `cover` / `contain` custom sizes.

crop.description: Optional crop rectangle; `null` means no crop.

top.description: Crop offset from the top, in pixels.

left.description: Crop offset from the left, in pixels.

ExportEasField.description:
Per-asset-field export options. Keyed under `export.eas_fields` by a
datamodel field api-name (`objecttype.field`). The key must resolve
to a field whose column type is `files` (an asset field) and whose
required merge depth is `<= merge_max_depth`.

files.description:
Whether to ship the actual binary files for this asset field.
If false, only `data` descriptors (if any) are processed.

include_non_preferred.description:
If true, non-preferred files in a multi-file asset value are
also exported; if false, only the preferred file is exported
(unless overridden per file via `export.assets`).

data.description:
Extra metadata-data descriptors emitted alongside (or instead
of) the binary files.

ExportFieldData.description:
One metadata-data descriptor for an asset field. References a
specific rendition and a verbosity.

type.description: Which rendition the metadata describes.

format.description:
Verbosity of the embedded file metadata. `long` = full,
`short` = reduced.

ExportAsset.description:
Object form of an `export.assets.<file-id>` value, selecting an
explicit version list for one file. (The other accepted forms are
boolean `true` = use the class defaults, and `false` = skip the
file.)

versions.description:
Explicit list of rendition selections for this file, used
instead of the class defaults.

eas_parent_id.description: Optional parent asset/file id this file belongs to.

json.description: Per-asset flag for JSON output of this asset entry.

GeoPoint.description:
A geographic point in WGS84 decimal degrees. Both coordinates are
required. Range checking (`lat` -90..90, `lon` -180..180) is enforced
downstream by OpenSearch.

_id.description:
Server-issued numeric id of the tag. Returned on create and required
on every subsequent update so the server can detect concurrent edits.

_version.description:
The **_version** needs to be set to **1** for new objects and set to an
incremented number on each object update. This is used to ensure that two
clients do not overwrite data of the same object.

displayname.description:
Localized human-readable name of the tag. Shown wherever the front
end surfaces a tag — picker, assignment list, facet, detail view.

description.description:
Localized longer text explaining what the tag is for. Shown in admin
tooltips and in the detail view next to the tag chip.

frontend_prefs.description:
Frontend preferences can be any JSON Map with data used by applications. The
conventions here is that each application can use its on key on the top level
of this Map. Only this key may be modified by the application, foreign keys
must be kept unchanged.

enabled.description: Set to **true** if this tag is available?

is_default.description:
Set to **true** if the tag is set by default when creating new objects.
This is a frontend only flag.

sticky.description: If set this tag cannot be removed by Pools which maintain their own tag lists.

type.description:
Whether the tag applies to a single object version (`individual`) or
across all versions of an object (`all_versions`). Empty/omitted
defaults to `individual`.

displaytype.description:
How and where the tag is surfaced in the front end. One of `facet`,
`search`, `detail`, `editor`, `hidden`. Empty/omitted defaults to
`facet`.

displayname.description: Localized name of the tag group.

type.description:
A tag group of type **checkbox** can set multiple tags of that group. A tag group
of type **choice** can only set one tag of that group.

items.description: Each item describes a config setting.

name.description: The unique name of this config

group.description: The **group** sets the tab this config variable is in.

require_system_right.description: System right which this config requires, e.g. "system.root" if it requires root rights.

unauthenticated_visible.description: If true, this config is visible to unauthenticated sessions (access token with no user).

unavailable.description:
If true, the feature this section configures is not available on
this instance (e.g. not included in the license). Frontends should
render the section greyed out but visible. Computed per request;
omitted when false.

additionalProperties.description: _key_ is the name of the plugin

items.description: Each item describes a config setting.

name.description: The unique name of this config

group.description: The **group** sets the tab this config variable is in.

require_system_right.description: System right which this config requires, e.g. "system.root" if it requires root rights.

unauthenticated_visible.description: If true, this config is visible to unauthenticated sessions (access token with no user).

unavailable.description:
If true, the feature this section configures is not available on
this instance (e.g. not included in the license). Frontends should
render the section greyed out but visible. Computed per request;
omitted when false.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this license was created.

key.description: UUID of license file, e.g., for blacklisting.

issuer.description: Name of the issuer of the license.

holder.description: Name of the holder of the license.

type.description: Type of license, such as "subscription" or "buy".

edition.description: Edition of the license, such as "workgroup", "department", or "organization".

domains.description: Must match fylr.externalURL, allows simple wildcard "*".

from.description: Valid from this date, if empty already valid.

to.description: Valid to this date, if empty license does not expire.

additionalProperties.description: Set the capabilities for plugins.

created_at.description: UTC time of when the task was created.

updated_at.description: UTC time of when the task was updated.

_id.description: ID of the task. This is issued by the server.

_version.description:
Version of the task. The version must be incremented when saving a new
version of the task. While the task is running, the server may perform
updates on the task object without incrementing the version.

task_module.description: Name of the task module to run the task. Retrieve a list with `/api/v1/task/modules`.

task_params.description: The task properties. The schema for this is defined by the task module.

task_timeout_sec.description: When the task is run, this timeout is set. In case of a timeout the task will be in state `error`. Use `0` for no timeout.

description.description: A user provided description of the task.

next_run_at.description: The UTC time when the task will be started. It is possible that the task is run later that the given time (if other tasks are currently running), but not before. Set this to `null` to not execute this task. If a task is scheduled, the scheduler will update this property after the task has run.

frontend_prefs.description: Provided by API using Apps. The top level key of the map should reflect the name of your App. Apps are required to store unknown keys (and data) along with their own contribution.

email.description: The email address of the recipient. Mails are send using the `To:` email header.

problems_only.description: If set, emails are only sent if the task finished with a state other than `done`.

items.description: A schedule sets criterias to repeatedly start task or jobs. All defined criterias need to be met in order for a schedule to run.
The day of the month when this schedule should run.

weekdays.description: The weekday when this schedule should run.

items.description: The hour when this schedule should run. Range `0`-`23`.
The minute when this schedule should run. Range `0`-`59`.

timezone.description: The timezone this schedule should be run in. Valid names are in [IANA format](https://www.iana.org/time-zones).

state.description: Stores the state of a task. This can be used by the task module to continue interrupted work or otherwise. The data is provided over the API, so frontends may use it.

status.description: `status` is set by the **fylr** task runner.

status_percent.description: If supported by the task runner, a number from `0` to `100` (percent) indicating the progress of a task in state `running`. This can be `null` if the task module has not started updating this yet, or it is not supported by the task module. Frontends should only show and use this if it is not `null`.

status_msg.description: If the status is `error` or `canceled`, the status message gives more information.

run_started_at.description: UTC time when the last run of the task was started. It is set to `null` when the task re-starts.

run_canceled_at.description: UTC time when the last run of the task was canceled. It is set to `null` when the task re-starts.

run_finished_at.description: UTC time when the last run of the task has finished. It is set to `null` when the task re-starts.

_user.description:
The user that created the task. The task is run as this user. This is a
flat user object; only the creating user (or a `system.root` user) can
retrieve the task.

_id.description: The id of the user. It is automatically assigned.

_version.description: The version of the user record.

_generated_displayname.description: The generated displayname of the user. This uses `login` and `email`.

_id.description: The `_id` is **created** _when_ a file is uploaded into the fylr server.

eas_parent_id.description: The `eas_parent_id` is the id of the file this file is derived from. When using **/api/produce**, the newly produced files will get the parent id set to the file they are produced from.

reference.description: If set, this reference string is a unique string identifying the file. If the reference contains a `sha244:<hash>` or `sha256:<hash>`, the provided hash can be checked against the file's checksum using an action triggerable in **/inspect/files**. Other parts of the reference can be added but must be separated by `:`.

upload_user.description: The user who uploaded this file into the system. If the upload happens via Hotfolder, the uploader is the owner of the upload collection.

user.description: This will show a short representation of [user](https://docs.fylr.io/for-developers/system-data-types/user).

date_uploaded.description: The UTC timestamp when this file was uploaded.

best_date.description: Best date is a compiled UTC timestamp from the technical metadata. **fylr** uses the first set date from `technical_metadata.date_time_original`, `technical_metadata.create_date`, `date_uploaded`.

status.description: The status of the file. Known states are: `pending`, `processing`, `sync`, `done`, `failed`. Files and renditions can be accessed in status `sync` and `done`. Only files in state `done` can be exported.

last_status_at.description: The UTC timestamp when the last status change was last written to the database.

extension.description: The compiled extension of the file. This is matched by parsing available recipes at the time of the metadata generation. Extensions are matched by longer length first. So for a file `flower.webdvd.zip` the extension matched would be `.webdvd.zip` and not `.zip` if there is an available recipe.

class.description: The compiled class of the file. The classes are set by the recipe. Standard recipes of fylr include `image`, `audio`, `office`, `video`. The fylr frontend uses the class to pick the general player to view the file. A class `unknown` is assigned if no matching recipe was found but the upload accepts _unknown types_.

class_version_status.description: A compiled concatenation of `class`, `version` and `status`, joined by `.`. This is used for aggregations of file status.

class_extension.description: A compiled concatenation of `class`, `version`, joined by `.`. This is used for aggregations of files

is_original.description: Set to true if the file is an original. It is false if the file is a generated or uploaded rendition. Produced files are registered as originals underneath the original they are produced from.

hash.description: The `hash` is a non unique identifier of the file. For remote files, it is the [**md5sum**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Md5sum) of the URL, for local files it is the [**SHA-256**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SHA-2) of the binary file data. It is calculated together with the metadata for the file and a copy of `technical_metadata.sha256_hash`. The `hash` is used for duplicate detection.

technical_metadata.description:
Intrinsic technical properties of the file, serialized as a **flat**
key/value object (a single level — this is *not* the namespaced groups
map found in `metadata`). The well-known, fylr-computed keys are typed by
the `technical_metadata` component below (`width`, `height`, `dpi`,
`colorspace`, `format`, `dimensions`, `gps_location`, ...).

In addition, metadata recipes and plugins may inject **arbitrary extra
keys** (the value of each is recipe-defined: string, number, boolean,
nested object or array). Those extra keys are covered by
`additionalProperties` and are intentionally open because they originate
from external extractors (exiftool / plugins) and are not enumerable in
code.

See [Technical Metadata](https://docs.fylr.io/for-developers/system-data-types/file#technical_metadata)
for details.

compiled.description: A compiled version of the metadata for this file. For images this looks like this: `	JPG, 1920 x 1285 px, 569.5 kB`.

compiled_props.description: A fielded version of `compiled`. Frontends can use this to beatify the compiled output of the metadata.

extension.description: The upper cased version of the `extension`.

dimensions.description: The rendered dimensions for this file. This may include a unit. For page sizes European paper sizes like A4 will be recognised.

duration.description: Runtime of the audio or video file.

pages.description: The number of pages followed by a ` p.`. Frontends may localize this manually.

_mapped_metadata.description:
A rendered **object record** mapped from this file's metadata, used to
create an object from a pre-configured metadata mapping. It is *not*
free-form: it is a full fylr object (the same shape as a `/db` object
record) produced via the configured mapping for the upload collection,
object type, pool and request.

It is only present in the response when the `/api/eas` request asks for a
mapping (`mapping=standard` or a mapping id) **and** a mapping is
resolved; otherwise it is omitted. The nested object keyed by the object
type name holds the mapped content fields.

groupnames.description: Sorted list of metadata group names found in `groups`.

value.description: The value can be any type

name.description: The name of the attribute.

group.description: The group name of the attribute

original_filepath.description: Filename of the file as set during the upload. On Windows, this can contain drive letters and backslashes.

original_filename.description: The filename as set by `original_filepath` minus the path.

original_filename_basename.description: The filename as set by `original_filepath` minus the path and the detected `extension`.

versions.description:
Map of rendition name to file version. Depending on the user's asset
rights (`asset_show` / `asset_download`), not all versions are
accessible, so some may be absent. The key `original` refers to the
uploaded original; produced and custom renditions use their own version
names. When present but empty the value is rendered as `{}`.

_duplicates.description: If set, this attribute can hold an array of duplicates.

certainty.description: The certainty of the duplicate detection in percent. A value of 1.0 means 100%. Currently **fylr** can only detect exact same files (as per file hash).

_system_object_id.description: The `_system_object_id` of the object using the duplicate file.

_column_id.description: The column API id of the column holding the duplicate file.

_linked_objects.description:
When a file is uploaded and linked to a collection via
[`/api/eas`](https://docs.fylr.io/for-developers/api/endpoints/api-eas),
the attribute contains a list of objects created based on the upload
settings of that collection.

_version.description:
The `_version` of the object created or updated. An upload may
update an existing object if a version or series detection added a
file to an existing nested or an existing file array.

preferred.description:
This may be set if the file is in the context of an object. If set,
preferred indicates that this version is the preferred one in a list of
other versions.

name.description:
Custom name of the file. This is set in the relation to the object. When
retrieving the file via
[`/api/eas`](https://docs.fylr.io/for-developers/api/endpoints/api-eas).

frontend_prefs.description: Set only in relation to an object. This map can contain custom attributes and values.

reference.description: The `reference` of the file to look up.

duration.description: Media duration in seconds; may be fractional.

format.description: The format of the file: `landscape`, `portrait` or `square`.

samples_per_pixel.description:
Number of samples (channels) per pixel of a TIFF, e.g. `7` for RGB plus
4 extra channels (alpha, spot colors) as written by Photoshop.

response_202.description:
A confirm-and-resend payload (HTTP 202): the request was understood but needs
the client to confirm one or more decisions first. For each task the client
derives the query parameter(s) it implies and re-sends the **same** request
with them appended. Those parameters come from the task's `form` (the chosen
option's `value` under the field `name`) and from the button used
(`name`=`value`); `hidden` buttons carry parameters that must be sent even
though they are not shown. The `name`/`value` properties below are what a
frontend turns into the confirmation URL.

tasks.description:
The decisions the request raised. Every task must be resolved — its
parameter(s) added to the re-sent request — for the request to complete.

title.description: Short, localized heading for the confirmation.

message.description:
Localized explanation of what is being confirmed (for example that
the save removes the user's own access, or that the records being
deleted are still linked from other records).

form.description:
Optional input fields the user chooses from. The selected option's
`value` is sent as the query parameter named by the field's `name`
when the request is re-sent — this is how `delete_policy` and
`reverse_pool_changed_mode` are chosen. Absent for a plain
button-only confirmation such as revoke.

label.description: Localized label for the field. May be empty.

type.description:
Input-control type of the field — `options` for a single
choice out of `options`.

name.description:
**Query-parameter name** under which the chosen option's
`value` is sent when re-sending the request (for example
`delete_policy`, `reverse_pool_changed_mode`). One of the two
values a frontend reads to build the confirmation request.

options.description: The selectable choices for this field.

text.description: Localized label shown for this choice.

value.description:
**Value to send** for the field's `name` parameter when
this choice is selected (for example `remove`,
`setnull`, `create_collection`). The other half — with
the field `name` — of the confirmation parameter.

disabled.description:
When true the choice is shown but not selectable (for
example a delete policy that other records prevent).

horizontal.description: Display hint — lay the options out in a row.

buttons.description:
The actions offered for the task. A button whose `name` is set adds
`name`=`value` to the re-sent request. `revoke`, `confirmTransition`
and `revoke:confirmTransition` are the parameters the server acts on;
a generic submit button may instead carry `name: confirm`, which the
server ignores — there the operative parameter comes from the task's
`form`.

text.description: Localized button label.

hidden.description:
When true the button is not shown, but its `name`=`value` is a
parameter the client must still include when re-sending — used
to carry an already-chosen parameter across a second
confirmation (for example a `delete_policy` preserved while a
workflow transition is confirmed).

name.description:
**Query-parameter name** to add to the re-sent request when
this button is used (Go `ConfirmKey`). Operative names are
`revoke`, `confirmTransition` and `revoke:confirmTransition`; a
generic submit button uses `confirm`, which the server ignores.

value.description:
**Value** for the `name` parameter (Go `ConfirmValue`) — for
example `yes` for `revoke`, or the confirmation token for
`confirmTransition`.

allOf.description:
A confirm-and-resend payload (HTTP 202): the request was understood but needs
the client to confirm one or more decisions first. For each task the client
derives the query parameter(s) it implies and re-sends the **same** request

frontend turns into the confirmation URL.

DbPost202.description:
The `202` body of `POST /db/{objecttype}` — a `response_202` confirm-and-resend
task. The example shows the access-revocation case; transition and reverse-pool
cases use the same shape with `confirmTransition` / `reverse_pool_changed_mode`.

tasks.title: Save record
tasks.message: By saving this record, you will no longer be able to edit this record. Do you want to proceed?

DbDelete202.description:
The `202` body of `DELETE /db/{objecttype}` — a `response_202` confirm-and-resend
task. The example shows the delete-policy case (objects still linked from
others); the client re-sends with the chosen `delete_policy`.

tasks.title: Confirmation
tasks.message: The record you are trying to delete is referenced in other records. Please choose how you want to proceed.

options.text: Create collection with records to be deleted

ConfigPost202.description:
The `202` body of `POST /config` (and of a sub-path config patch) — a
`response_202` confirm-and-resend task. The example shows the savable-config-
error case (here an LDAP connection that does not dial): resend with
`save=ignore_errors` to persist despite the recoverable error. The
language-change case uses the same shape with a `form` field named `reindex`
(options `skip` / `reindex` / `reindex_block`) and a button `confirm`=`yes`.

tasks.title: Basis-Konfiguration
tasks.message:
In der Konfiguration gibt es folgende Probleme:

* Unable to connect: ...

Bestätigen Sie, um trotzdem zu speichern.

ExportForbiddenError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/export*` endpoints when the authenticated
user is not allowed to access the export. The export handlers permit
access only to the export's owner or a `system.root` user; any other
user gets this `403`.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `InsufficientRights` — the user is neither the export's owner
    nor `system.root`. `parameters.resource` identifies the
    export (for example `Export` or `export-42`).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `resource` identifies the export the
user was not allowed to access.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `403`.

example.error:
Insufficient rights to access resource export-42

Package: api
Code: InsufficientRights

BackupNotFoundError.description:
Error returned by the `/api/v1/system/backup/{id}` endpoints (GET,
DELETE and `/download`) when no backup with the given id exists.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Codes raised from the backup
package:

  * `BackupNotFound` — no backup with the addressed id is on file.
    `parameters.backupid` carries the id that was looked up.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `backupid` is the numeric id that was
looked up.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `404`.

example.error:
Backup #89 was not found

Package: backup
Code: BackupNotFound

ResourceNotFoundError.description:
Error returned by `GET /api/v1/system/location/{id}` when no storage
location with the given id is configured.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `ResourceNotFound` — the addressed resource does not exist.
    `parameters.resource` carries a `<kind>: <id>` token (for
    example `location: 5`).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `resource` is the `<kind>: <id>` token
identifying the missing resource.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `404`.

example.error:
Resource location: 5 not found

Package: api
Code: ResourceNotFound

InsufficientRightsError.description:
Error returned by `GET /api/v1/system/openapi/spec.json` when the
caller is not the `system.root` user and the base config flag
`system.config.openapi_spec_endpoint` is not enabled.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `InsufficientRights` — the caller may not access the addressed
    resource. `parameters.resource` names the resource that was
    requested.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable details. `resource` names the requested resource.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `403`.

example.error:
Insufficient rights to access resource openapi/spec.json

Package: api
Code: InsufficientRights

PurgeForbiddenError.description:
Error returned by `POST /api/v1/system/purgeall` when the caller is
not allowed to purge. `purgeall` is restricted to the actual
`system:root` user: a user without `system.root` is rejected with
`SystemRightRequired`, while a user who holds `system.root` but is not
the `system:root` user is rejected with `PurgeSystemRootOnly`.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `SystemRightRequired` — the user does not hold `system.root`.
    `parameters.rights` lists the rights that would have
    satisfied the check.
  * `PurgeSystemRootOnly` — the user holds `system.root` but is not
    the `system:root` user; only that user may purge.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. For `SystemRightRequired`, `rights`
lists the rights that would have satisfied the check.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `403`.

example.error:
Only the system user with reference 'system:root' is allowed to purge

Package: acl
Code: PurgeSystemRootOnly

PluginError.description:
Error returned by the plugin static-asset, extension-dispatch and
management routes when the addressed plugin is unknown, disabled, or
conflicts with an existing one. The HTTP status code depends on the
`code` (404 for `PluginNotFound`, 400 for the others).

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `PluginNotFound` — no plugin with the requested name or id
    exists (HTTP 404).
  * `PluginDisabled` — the plugin exists but is not enabled, so
    its static assets and extension routes are not served
    (HTTP 400).
  * `PluginDuplicateName` — a `PUT /plugin/manage` tried to create
    a plugin whose `name` is already taken (HTTP 400).

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline (request parsing,
`ReadOnlyMode` when the instance is read-only, ...) may also
surface on the management routes.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `plugin` carries the plugin name or id
from the request.

example.error:
Plugin fylr_example is disabled

Package: api
Code: PluginDisabled

PluginManageApi.description:
A single plugin as seen by the management API (`/plugin/manage`).
Carries the stored plugin row (id, name, enabled flag, source) plus
the parsed `manifest`. Used both as the request body of
`PUT`/`POST /plugin/manage` and as their response.

On create (`PUT`) the body must NOT carry an `id`; on update
(`POST /plugin/manage/{id}`) the body `id` must match the path `{id}`.

name.description:
Unique plugin name (matches the manifest `name:`). Read-only in
responses; derived from the uploaded plugin on create.

enabled.description:
Whether the plugin is enabled. A freshly uploaded plugin is
created disabled (`false`); enable it with an update.

auto_added.description:
`true` when the plugin was discovered and registered automatically
(e.g. from the plugins directory) rather than uploaded via the API.

path.description: Filesystem path the plugin is loaded from, if any.

url.description: Source URL the plugin is fetched from when `type` is `url`.

type.description:
Source type of the plugin. `zip` for a plugin uploaded as a ZIP
asset (`zip_file`), `url` for one fetched from a remote `url`.

zip_file.description:
The uploaded plugin ZIP, referenced by file id. On create from an
upload, send `{ "_id": <fileId> }` of a previously uploaded asset.

_id.description: File id of the uploaded plugin ZIP asset.

update_policy.description:
Update policy stored for the plugin (controls how/when **fylr**
refreshes the plugin from its source).

manifest.description:
The parsed plugin manifest plus the request-time asset URLs
(`base_url`, `plugin_url`).

last_checked_at.description: When **fylr** last checked the plugin source for updates.

PluginManageList.description:
Response of `GET /plugin/manage` — the full list of installed
plugins (enabled and disabled) as seen by the management API.

PluginDeleteAck.description:
Response of `DELETE /plugin/manage/{id}` — confirms the plugin was
removed.

acknowledged.description: Always `true` when the plugin was deleted.

allOf.description:
A confirm-and-resend payload (HTTP 202): the request was understood but needs
the client to confirm one or more decisions first. For each task the client
derives the query parameter(s) it implies and re-sends the **same** request
with them appended. Those parameters come from the task's `form` (the chosen
option's `value` under the field `name`) and from the button used
(`name`=`value`); `hidden` buttons carry parameters that must be sent even
though they are not shown. The `name`/`value` properties below are what a
frontend turns into the confirmation URL.

tasks.description:
The decisions the request raised. Every task must be resolved — its
parameter(s) added to the re-sent request — for the request to complete.

title.description: Short, localized heading for the confirmation.

message.description:
Localized explanation of what is being confirmed (for example that
the save removes the user's own access, or that the records being
deleted are still linked from other records).

form.description:
Optional input fields the user chooses from. The selected option's
`value` is sent as the query parameter named by the field's `name`
when the request is re-sent — this is how `delete_policy` and
`reverse_pool_changed_mode` are chosen. Absent for a plain
button-only confirmation such as revoke.

label.description: Localized label for the field. May be empty.

type.description:
Input-control type of the field — `options` for a single
choice out of `options`.

name.description:
**Query-parameter name** under which the chosen option's
`value` is sent when re-sending the request (for example
`delete_policy`, `reverse_pool_changed_mode`). One of the two
values a frontend reads to build the confirmation request.

options.description: The selectable choices for this field.

text.description: Localized label shown for this choice.

value.description:
**Value to send** for the field's `name` parameter when
this choice is selected (for example `remove`,
`setnull`, `create_collection`). The other half — with
the field `name` — of the confirmation parameter.

disabled.description:
When true the choice is shown but not selectable (for
example a delete policy that other records prevent).

horizontal.description: Display hint — lay the options out in a row.

buttons.description:
The actions offered for the task. A button whose `name` is set adds
`name`=`value` to the re-sent request. `revoke`, `confirmTransition`
and `revoke:confirmTransition` are the parameters the server acts on;
a generic submit button may instead carry `name: confirm`, which the
server ignores — there the operative parameter comes from the task's
`form`.

text.description: Localized button label.

hidden.description:
When true the button is not shown, but its `name`=`value` is a
parameter the client must still include when re-sending — used
to carry an already-chosen parameter across a second
confirmation (for example a `delete_policy` preserved while a
workflow transition is confirmed).

name.description:
**Query-parameter name** to add to the re-sent request when
this button is used (Go `ConfirmKey`). Operative names are
`revoke`, `confirmTransition` and `revoke:confirmTransition`; a
generic submit button uses `confirm`, which the server ignores.

value.description:
**Value** for the `name` parameter (Go `ConfirmValue`) — for
example `yes` for `revoke`, or the confirmation token for
`confirmTransition`.

UserPost202.description:
The `202` body of `POST` / `PUT /user` — a `response_202` confirm-and-resend
task raised when a user in the payload sets a password that does not satisfy
the configured password-complexity rules. The task has no `form`; its button
carries `confirm=ignore_password_requirements`. To save the user anyway, the
client re-sends the **same** request with the query parameter
`confirm=ignore_password_requirements` appended. (For this operation the
`confirm` parameter is operative — unlike the generic `confirm` button the
server otherwise ignores.) The `%(requirement)s` placeholder in the message
is filled with the configured password hint.

tasks.title: Password Change
tasks.message: One or more user passwords do not match the configured requirements: %(requirement)s Should the password be saved anyway?

UserDelete202.description:
The `202` body of `DELETE /user/{id}` — a `response_202` confirm-and-resend
task raised when no `delete_policy` was supplied (and the instance default is
`ask`). The task's `form` field `delete_policy` lists the allowed policies;
the client re-sends the request with the chosen `delete_policy` value. The
generic submit button carries `confirm=delete`, which the server ignores —
the operative parameter is `delete_policy`. The example shows a single
archived user: the offered options are `delete` and `unarchive`.

tasks.title: Confirmation
tasks.message: Users can be archived or deleted. When archiving, the information is retained in the change history, but not when deleting. If configured, users are pseudonymized during archiving. When archiving, the user's collections are deleted and cannot be restored.

Collection202.description:
Returned (HTTP 202) by a collection write that shares rights when the
collection's `_owner` lacks a grantable right for one of the linked objects
and the caller has `BAG_ACL`. The task offers to store the collection anyway
with sharing disabled: re-send the same request with `allow_invalid_acl=true`
(the button's `name`=`value`). When stored this way the collection is flagged
`_invalid_acl: true` and its ACL is not applied.

tasks.title: Collection
tasks.message:
The owner "alice" of the collection "Marketing" does not have the
permission "read" for record #4711 in order to share the collection.
You can save the collection anyways, but sharing for this collection
will be disabled completely.

ObjecttypePost202.description:
Confirm-and-resend payload (HTTP 202) returned by
`POST /api/v1/objecttype` when the save needs confirmation. The client
re-sends the **same** request body with the query parameter implied by
the task appended:

  * `allow_invalid_acl=true` — proceed with an ACL change that would
    invalidate a collection (button `allow_invalid_acl`).
  * `background_invalid_acl=true` — run the ACL revalidation as a
    background task after a synchronous timeout (button
    `background_invalid_acl`).
  * `confirm=resync` / `confirm=skip` — recompute or skip renditions
    when a watermark / caption change requires it (form field
    `confirm`).

See the `confirm`, `allow_invalid_acl`, `background_invalid_acl` and
`background_invalid_acl_timelimit` query parameters on the operation.

tasks.title: Collection
tasks.message:
The owner "..." of the collection "..." does not have the
permission "..." for record #... in order to share the
collection. You can save the collection anyways, but sharing
for this collection will be disabled completely.

PoolForbiddenError.description:
Error returned by the `/pool` endpoints when the user is not allowed
to perform the operation. Two distinct codes map to this `403`
depending on which gate rejected the request.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `SystemRightRequired` — POST/PUT, DELETE and `/stats` require
    the `system.poolmanager` system right; the session does not
    hold it. `parameters.rights` names the required system
    right(s).
  * `RightRequired` — a per-pool BAG right is missing. Read
    (`GET /pool/{poolID}`) needs `bag_read`; save needs
    `bag_create` / `bag_write` / `bag_acl` on the pool or its
    parent; delete needs `bag_delete`. `parameters.right` names
    the missing right and `parameters.rightdisplayname` its
    localized label.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `SystemRightRequired` carries `rights`;
`RightRequired` carries `right` and `rightdisplayname`.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `403`.

example.error:
Current user is missing permission "bag_read" ("Read").

Package: rightcheck
Code: RightRequired

PoolNotFoundError.description:
Error returned by `DELETE /pool/{poolID}` when no pool with the given
id exists.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `ResourceNotFound` — the pool id in the URL does not match any
    stored pool. `parameters.resource` carries a `Pool <id>`
    token.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `resource` is the `Pool <id>` token
identifying the missing pool.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `404`.

example.error:
Resource Pool 42 not found

Package: api
Code: ResourceNotFound

PoolPost202.description:
Confirm-and-resend payload (HTTP 202) returned by `POST`/`PUT /pool`.
Two cases are reachable:

  * A watermark or caption change requires originals to be
    re-rendered. The task carries a `confirm` field — resend with
    `confirm=resync` to schedule the re-sync or `confirm=skip` to
    save without re-rendering.
  * The post-save collection-ACL re-check exceeded its time limit
    (default 30s, overridable via `background_invalid_acl_timelimit`).
    The task's button carries `background_invalid_acl`=`true` — resend
    with that parameter to finish the check in the background.

See the `confirm`, `background_invalid_acl` and
`background_invalid_acl_timelimit` query parameters on the operation.

tasks.title: Update renditions
tasks.message: 2 files need to update renditions. This may be done manually in /inspect/files.

SchemaCommit202.description:
The `202` body of `POST /schema/commit` — a `response_202` confirm-and-resend
task. A change that needs a reindex shows a `reindex` options field (`skip` /
`reindex` / `reindex_block`); a change that alters constraints shows a plain
confirm button with no form. Resend the call with `confirm=yes` (and, for the
reindex case, the chosen `reindex` value) to run the commit. The example shows
the reindex case; the constraints case has the same shape with only the
`buttons` entry (`name: confirm`, `value: yes`) and no `form`.

tasks.title: Re-indexing
tasks.message: Your change makes a re-indexing necessary. This runs in the background and will briefly generate a higher load on the server. New records or records that have been changed in the meantime will only become visible after indexing.

options.text: Perform re-indexing and temporarily lock system

RightPresetPost202.description:
The `202` body of `POST /right/{context}/presets` — a `response_202`
confirm-and-resend task raised when, after saving the presets, re-validating
the affected collections' ACLs exceeds the synchronous time limit
(`30s` by default, overridable with `background_invalid_acl_timelimit`). The
button re-sends the same `POST` with `background_invalid_acl=true` (the
button's `name`=`value`) to run the validity check as a background task and
return immediately.

tasks.title: Collection
tasks.message: Checking the collection permissions took longer than 30s and was aborted. Do you want to start this task in the background?

RightPresetDelete202.description:
The `202` body of `DELETE /right/{context}/presets/{presetId}` — a
`response_202` confirm-and-resend task raised when the preset being deleted
is or was in use, i.e. `right` rows reference it on current or archived
objects. The single button re-sends the same `DELETE` with `confirm=delete`
(the button's `name`=`value`); only then is the preset actually deleted
together with its associated permission rows in current and archived records.

tasks.title: Delete preset
tasks.message: The presets to be deleted are or were in use. Deleting the presets deletes associated permission in current and archived records.

L10nVersionError.description:
Error returned by `GET /l10n/user/{version}` when the requested datamodel
version cannot be resolved — either the supplied `version` is not `HEAD`,
`CURRENT`, or a positive integer, or no committed datamodel exists for that
version number.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Always `DatamodelVersionNotFound`
for this response.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details of the failure.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
Data model with version "HENK" was not found

Package: datamodel
Code: DatamodelVersionNotFound

L10nPostError.description:
Error returned by `POST /l10n/user/HEAD` when the write cannot be processed.
The `code` field identifies the cause.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `ServerGeneric` — the submitted document could not be decoded
    (malformed JSON body).
  * `ReadOnlyMode` — the instance is in read-only mode and rejects
    writes.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details of the failure.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
Read-only mode is enabled.

Package: ferrors
Code: ReadOnlyMode

allOf.description:
A confirm-and-resend payload (HTTP 202): the request was understood but needs
the client to confirm one or more decisions first. For each task the client
derives the query parameter(s) it implies and re-sends the **same** request
with them appended. Those parameters come from the task's `form` (the chosen
option's `value` under the field `name`) and from the button used
(`name`=`value`); `hidden` buttons carry parameters that must be sent even
though they are not shown. The `name`/`value` properties below are what a
frontend turns into the confirmation URL.

tasks.description:
The decisions the request raised. Every task must be resolved — its
parameter(s) added to the re-sent request — for the request to complete.

title.description: Short, localized heading for the confirmation.

message.description:
Localized explanation of what is being confirmed (for example that
the save removes the user's own access, or that the records being
deleted are still linked from other records).

form.description:
Optional input fields the user chooses from. The selected option's
`value` is sent as the query parameter named by the field's `name`
when the request is re-sent — this is how `delete_policy` and
`reverse_pool_changed_mode` are chosen. Absent for a plain
button-only confirmation such as revoke.

label.description: Localized label for the field. May be empty.

type.description:
Input-control type of the field — `options` for a single
choice out of `options`.

name.description:
**Query-parameter name** under which the chosen option's
`value` is sent when re-sending the request (for example
`delete_policy`, `reverse_pool_changed_mode`). One of the two
values a frontend reads to build the confirmation request.

options.description: The selectable choices for this field.

text.description: Localized label shown for this choice.

value.description:
**Value to send** for the field's `name` parameter when
this choice is selected (for example `remove`,
`setnull`, `create_collection`). The other half — with
the field `name` — of the confirmation parameter.

disabled.description:
When true the choice is shown but not selectable (for
example a delete policy that other records prevent).

horizontal.description: Display hint — lay the options out in a row.

buttons.description:
The actions offered for the task. A button whose `name` is set adds
`name`=`value` to the re-sent request. `revoke`, `confirmTransition`
and `revoke:confirmTransition` are the parameters the server acts on;
a generic submit button may instead carry `name: confirm`, which the
server ignores — there the operative parameter comes from the task's
`form`.

text.description: Localized button label.

hidden.description:
When true the button is not shown, but its `name`=`value` is a
parameter the client must still include when re-sending — used
to carry an already-chosen parameter across a second
confirmation (for example a `delete_policy` preserved while a
workflow transition is confirmed).

name.description:
**Query-parameter name** to add to the re-sent request when
this button is used (Go `ConfirmKey`). Operative names are
`revoke`, `confirmTransition` and `revoke:confirmTransition`; a
generic submit button uses `confirm`, which the server ignores.

value.description:
**Value** for the `name` parameter (Go `ConfirmValue`) — for
example `yes` for `revoke`, or the confirmation token for
`confirmTransition`.

TagsPost202.description:
Confirm-and-resend payload returned by `POST /api/v1/tags` when one or more
tags that would be deleted are still referenced only in contexts **fylr** can
clean up automatically (`collection.create_object`, `objecttype_tag`,
`pool_tag`). Resend the identical request with `delete_policy=remove_tags` to
confirm.

tasks.title: Delete tags in use?
tasks.message:
The tags which are to be deleted are still in use:

- 1 Collection(s)

Should the tags automatically be removed and deleted anyways?

**Query-parameter name** under which the chosen option's
`value` is sent when re-sending the request (for example
`delete_policy`, `reverse_pool_changed_mode`). One of the two

`setnull`, `create_collection`). The other half — with
the field `name` — of the confirmation parameter.

disabled.description:
When true the choice is shown but not selectable (for

The actions offered for the task. A button whose `name` is set adds

XmlMappingDelete202.description:
Returned by `DELETE /xmlmapping/mapping/{mapping}` when the mapping is
still linked from one or more collections' upload settings
(`collection_create_object.mapping_id`). Re-send the same `DELETE` with
`?delete_policy=delete` to unlink it from every affected collection
(sets `mapping_id` to `NULL`) and then delete the mapping.

tasks.title: Confirmation
tasks.message:
The Metadata Mapping you are about to delete is linked in the upload settings of the following 2 collections:

- My Imports
- Archive

Should the mapping be removed from the collection settings and be deleted?

options.text: Unlink from collections and delete mapping

Error.description:
Standard error envelope. Every **fylr** API error is returned as a JSON object
in this shape, together with the matching HTTP status code.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code, for example `UnknownObjecttype` or
`VersionMismatch`. Use this to branch on errors in client code instead of
parsing the `error` message.

error.description:
Human-readable error message. Intended for developers and log output, not
for display to end users.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for the error, for **fylr** support and logs. It is
not part of the API contract and carries no meaning for clients — branch on
`code`, never on `package`.

parameters.description:
Additional, error-specific details. Which keys are present depends on the
`code`. An empty object when the error carries no extra information.

realm.description: Realm the error originates from. For API errors this is `api`.

description.description: Optional longer explanation of the error.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here for convenience.

UnauthorizedError.description: Error returned when the request has no authenticated user.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `UserRequired` — the request did not carry a valid access token.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable details of the error.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `401`.

example.error:
The API call requires an authenticated user

Package: ferrors
Code: UserRequired

ForbiddenError.description: Error returned when the authenticated user lacks a required system right.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `SystemRightRequired` — the user holds none of the system rights the
    operation requires.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. For `SystemRightRequired`, `rights` lists the
system rights that would have satisfied the check.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `403`.

example.error:
One of the system rights ["system.api.event[get], system.root"] is required.

Package: acl
Code: SystemRightRequired

SearchError.description:
Error returned by `/search` when the search cannot be processed. Several
distinct validations map to this `400`; the `code` field identifies which
one rejected the request.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `SearchFieldUnknown` — a `search` element references a field that
    does not exist.
  * `SearchFieldNotAllowed` — a `search` element uses a field that is
    not allowed in the current search context.
  * `NestedQueryIn` — an `in` query targets an entire nested object,
    which is not supported.
  * `WildcardOnlyNotAllowed` — a `text` search on this field consists
    only of wildcards, which is not allowed.
  * `WildcardExpandedToTooManyTerms` — a wildcard expanded to more
    terms than the configured limit; narrow the term.
  * `UnknownTimezone` — the request's `timezone` is not a known
    timezone identifier.
  * `DateWrongSyntax` — a date value in the query has the wrong
    syntax.
  * `UnsupportedInAggregation` — an aggregation uses an unsupported
    combination of options.
  * `ReindexInProgress` — a reindex is already running and the request
    conflicts with it.
  * `IndexErrorWaitForReindex` — language settings have changed; wait
    for the re-index to complete.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details of the failure.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
The search field "nonexistent_field" is unknown

Package: search
Code: SearchFieldUnknown

SearchParseError.description:
Error returned by `/search/parse` when the request cannot be processed.
Several distinct failures map to this `400`; the `code` field identifies
which one occurred.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. `QueryParse` is raised by the
parser when the query string is not syntactically valid. Every other
code is raised while the parsed query is compiled — the same pipeline
as `/search`, so the same codes can surface here:

  * `QueryParse` — the query string is not syntactically valid.
  * `SearchFieldUnknown` — the query refers to a field that does not
    exist.
  * `SearchFieldNotAllowed` — the query uses a field that is not
    allowed in the current search context.
  * `NestedQueryIn` — an `in` query targets an entire nested object,
    which is not supported.
  * `WildcardOnlyNotAllowed` — a text search on this field consists
    only of wildcards, which is not allowed.
  * `WildcardExpandedToTooManyTerms` — a wildcard expanded to more
    terms than the configured limit; narrow the term.
  * `UnknownTimezone` — the request's `timezone` is not a known
    timezone identifier.
  * `DateWrongSyntax` — a date value in the query has the wrong
    syntax.
  * `UnsupportedInAggregation` — an aggregation uses an unsupported
    combination of options.
  * `ReindexInProgress` — a reindex is already running and the request
    conflicts with it.
