  * `IndexErrorWaitForReindex` — language settings have changed; wait
    for the re-index to complete.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details of the failure.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
title ==: 1:9: error: expected one of "?", null, string, int, uint, or "["; got: end-of-file

Package: query
Code: QueryParse

parameters.msg: 1:9: error: expected one of "?", null, string, int, uint, or "["; got: end-of-file

DbObject.description:
A **fylr** object as exchanged with the `/db/{objecttype}` endpoint. Every
object carries the system fields below. The content fields live in a nested
object whose key is the object type name; which fields are present depends on
the object type and the `_mask`. The object-type-specific sections of this
documentation describe those fields concretely.

_objecttype.description: Name of the object type. Matches `{objecttype}` in the URL.

_mask.description:
Name of the mask that shapes the object. The mask selects which fields
are present and whether each is writable or read-only.

_system_object_id.description:
Instance-wide unique ID of the object. Settable while the object is
version 1 (new), immutable afterwards.

_global_object_id.description:
Globally unique object ID: the `_system_object_id` followed by
`@<database name>`.

_uuid.description: System-wide unique identifier for the object.

_owner.description: The owner of the object; may be a user or a group.

_created.description: The datetime the object was created.

{objecttype}.description:
Content of the object, in a property named after the object type. Holds
`_id`, `_version` and the fields defined by the object type and selected
by the `_mask`.

_id.description:
ID of the object, unique within the object type. Settable while the
object is version 1 (new), immutable afterwards.

_version.description:
Version of the object. Starts at 1 and is incremented on every
update; an update must send the current version.

DbPostError.description:
Error returned by `POST /db/{objecttype}` when the save cannot be processed.
The `code` field of the response identifies which validation rejected the
request — listed below are the most common stable codes; other validation
errors from the save pipeline may also surface here.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Common codes for this endpoint:

  * `UnknownObjecttype` — the object type in the URL is not defined in
    the datamodel.
  * `ReadOnlyMode` — the instance is in read-only mode and rejects
    writes.
  * `LookupFoundTooMany` — a lookup in the payload matched more than
    one object; lookups must resolve to exactly one.
  * `LookupNotFound` — a lookup in the payload matched no object.
  * `DatabaseUniqueKeyViolation` — a uniqueness constraint was
    violated (for example, a duplicate reference value).
  * `SchemaInconsistent` — the payload does not match the object
    type's schema (unexpected field, wrong type, …).

Other validation errors from the save pipeline may also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details of the failure.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
Unknown Objecttype pictures

Package: api
Code: UnknownObjecttype

DbDeleteError.description:
Error returned by `DELETE /db/{objecttype}` when the delete cannot be
processed. The `code` field identifies which validation rejected the
request — listed below are the most common stable codes; other
delete-validation errors may also surface here.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Common codes for this endpoint:

  * `UnknownObjecttype` — the object type in the URL is not defined in
    the datamodel.
  * `ReadOnlyMode` — the instance is in read-only mode and rejects
    writes.
  * `LookupFoundTooMany` — a lookup used to identify an object matched
    more than one.
  * `LookupNotFound` — a lookup used to identify an object matched
    nothing.

Other delete-validation errors may also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details of the failure.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
Read-only mode is enabled.

Package: ferrors
Code: ReadOnlyMode

DbReadError.description:
Error returned by the `GET /db/{objecttype}/{mask}/{objectId}` and
`GET /db/{objecttype}/{mask}/list` endpoints when the request cannot be
processed. The read path is small, so most stable codes here concern the
identity of the object type / mask; the remaining 400s come from generic
bad-request validation without a stable `code`.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Common codes for this endpoint:

  * `UnknownObjecttype` — the object type or mask in the URL does not
    exist.

Other 400s on this endpoint (missing object ID, unparseable query
parameter, …) come from generic bad-request validation without a
stable `code`.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details of the failure.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
Unknown Objecttype pictures

Package: api
Code: UnknownObjecttype

DbForbiddenError.description:
Error returned by the `/db` endpoints when the user is not allowed to
perform the operation. Two distinct codes map to this `403` depending on
whether the missing right is object-level or system-level.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `ObjectInsufficientRights` — the user lacks a required permission
    on a specific object (for example `read`, `write`, `delete`).
  * `SystemRightRequired` — the user lacks a required system right
    (for example `system.root`, needed for `skip_plugins` /
    `skip_events`).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `403`.

example.error:
The object #4711 [picture] needs right "Write" (write)

Package: ferrors
Code: ObjectInsufficientRights

DbLockedError.description:
Error returned by the `/db` write endpoints when an object affected by the
request is locked by a concurrent write. Retrying the request once the
lock is released succeeds.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `DatabaseLockError` — unable to acquire a database lock; another
    write is in progress on the same object.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable details (the resource that could not be locked).

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `423`.

example.error:
Unable to acquire a lock for resource "".

Package: ferrors
Code: DatabaseLockError

TaskError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/task/*` endpoints when the request cannot be
processed. The `code` field identifies which validation rejected the
request. `ModuleUnknown` is the only stable code raised from the task
package itself (it fires when saving a new task — `_version` 1 — whose
`task_module` is not registered). Most other task-validation failures —
a `_version` / `_id` mismatch on save, writing a read-only field, or
cancelling a task that is not in status `running` — surface as generic
`400` errors with a non-stable code and a descriptive `error` message.
Generic codes from the server pipeline (`ReadOnlyMode`,
`UnsupportedAccept`, parameter parsing) may also surface here.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Common codes for these endpoints:

  * `ModuleUnknown` — the `task_module` named in the payload is not
    registered on this fylr instance. `parameters.name` carries the
    offending module name.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable, code-specific details. For `ModuleUnknown`,
`parameters.name` is the unknown module name.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
The module name "indexing" is unknown.

Package: task
Code: ModuleUnknown

TaskForbiddenError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/task/{taskId}*` endpoints when the
authenticated user is not allowed to perform the operation. The `code`
field carries the specific reason: the user lacks the `system.task`
system right (`SystemRightRequired`), or the task belongs to another
user (`InsufficientRights`).

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `SystemRightRequired` — the authenticated user lacks the
    `system.task` system right (and is not `system.root`).
    `parameters.rights` lists the rights that would have satisfied
    the check. Raised by package `acl`.
  * `InsufficientRights` — the user is neither the task's creator nor
    a `system.root` user. Tasks are user-specific; only the user who
    created a task (or a `system.root` user) can read, log, cancel,
    update or delete it. `parameters.taskid` carries the task that was
    targeted. Raised by package `task`.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. `InsufficientRights` originates from package `task`,
`SystemRightRequired` from package `acl`. Branch on `code`, not
`package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable, code-specific details. For `InsufficientRights`,
`parameters.taskid` is the id of the task that was requested. For
`SystemRightRequired`, `parameters.rights` lists the system rights
that would have satisfied the check.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `403`.

example.error:
Insufficient rights to access task 42

Package: task
Code: InsufficientRights

TagsError.description:
Error returned by `POST /api/v1/tags` when the request could not be
processed. The `code` field identifies which validation rejected the
request.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Codes raised from the tag
package:

  * `TagsInUse` — one or more tags that would be deleted are still
    referenced elsewhere in the system and cannot be removed
    automatically. This endpoint has no DELETE method; any tag not
    present in the request is deleted, and the request fails if any
    of those deletions would orphan a reference. `parameters.usages`
    lists every place each tag is still in use so the caller can
    resolve the reference manually before retrying.

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline (request parsing,
`ReadOnlyMode`, …) may also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable, code-specific details. For `TagsInUse`,
`parameters.usages` is the array described below.

usages.description:
List of every (tag, context) pair that prevents the deletion.
Each entry must be resolved before the tag can be removed.

tag_id.description: ID of the tag that cannot be deleted.

context.description:
Location where the tag is referenced. Known values:

| Value | Description |
|---|---|
| `object` | Tag is attached to one or more objects (records) |
| `right_preset._acl` | Tag is used in the ACL of a rights preset |
| `pool._acl` | Tag is used in the ACL of a pool |
| `tag._acl` | Tag is used in the ACL of another tag |
| `objecttype._acl` | Tag is used in the ACL of an objecttype |
| `objecttype._columnfilters` | Tag is used in a column filter of an objecttype |
| `objecttype._maskfilters` | Tag is used in a mask filter of an objecttype |
| `transition.tagfilter:before` | Tag is used in a transition (workflow) in the "before" tag filter |
| `transition.tagfilter:after` | Tag is used in a transition (workflow) in the "after" tag filter |
| `collection.create_object` | Tag is part of a collection's `create_object` configuration |
| `baseconfig` | Tag is referenced in the base configuration |

ids.description:
Row IDs of the rows that reference the tag. The kind of ID
depends on `context`: system object ID for `object`, pool
ID for `pool._acl`, objecttype ID for `objecttype._acl` /
`objecttype._columnfilters` / `objecttype._maskfilters`,
the other tag's ID for `tag._acl`, right-preset ID for
`right_preset._acl`, transition ID for the two
`transition.tagfilter:*` contexts. Present for every
context **except** `baseconfig`, which uses `refs`
instead.

refs.description:
Path-like references for contexts where a numeric row ID
does not identify the location. Used only for
`baseconfig`: each entry is the JSON path of the
offending tagfilter field on the `/api/v1/config` tree,
e.g. `system.config.oai_pmh.tagfilter_deleted` for core
config, or `plugin.<plugin-name>.config.<field>` (with
`[<index>]` segments where the path crosses a table row)
for plugin config.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error: The tags can not be deleted, because they are used in 10 places where they have to be deleted manually

DbInfoError.description:
Error returned by `POST /api/v1/db_info/create` and `POST /api/v1/db_info/update`
when the permission check cannot be performed. The `code` field of the
response identifies the specific reason.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Code-specific reasons (branch on
these values):

  * `NoMasksForCreate` — the requested `objecttype` (and `pool_id`,
    if given) has no mask the user could create new objects against.
    `parameters.objecttype` and `parameters.pool` carry the input.
  * `ObjecttypeNotFound` — the `objecttype` in the request body is
    not defined in the current datamodel. `parameters.objecttype`
    carries the name.
  * `TagNotFound` — one of the `tag_ids` in the request body does
    not match any tag. `parameters.tagid` carries the offending id.
  * `TagNotEnabled` — one of the `tag_ids` exists but is not enabled
    for the requested objecttype/pool combination.
    `parameters.tagid` carries the offending id.

This enum is not exhaustive. Not every 400 carries one of the codes
above: input-validation failures such as an unknown `pool_id`, an
objecttype that has no pool or no tags, or a `db_info/update` call
missing `objecttype`/`object_ids`, are returned as generic 400
messages without a stable `code`. Other 400 codes from the server
pipeline (request parsing, `ReadOnlyMode`, …) may also surface here.
Always tolerate codes outside this enum.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable, code-specific details. See `code` for the keys
each variant carries.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
Objecttype pictures not found

Package: dbinfo
Code: ObjecttypeNotFound

NotFoundError.description:
Error returned when the resource addressed by the request URL does not
exist. Shared across endpoints that look up a record (collection, task,
message, …) by path parameter.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `ObjectNotFound` — the entity addressed by the URL was not
    found. `parameters.object` carries an `<area>:<id>` token so
    the caller can correlate (for example `collection:42`,
    `task:7`).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `object` is the `<area>:<id>` token
identifying the missing resource.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `404`.

example.error:
Object collection:42 was not found

Package: ferrors
Code: ObjectNotFound

CollectionError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/collection*` endpoints when the request could
not be processed. The `code` field identifies which validation rejected
the request.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. The code raised specifically by
the collection object operations:

  * `CollectionRemoveNotAllObjectsFound` — `POST /api/v1/collection/remove/{id}`
    referenced one or more objects that are not currently linked to
    the collection. The raise site sets no parameters, so
    `parameters` is empty for this code; the human-readable `error`
    is the constant "The collection does not contain all objects
    requested to remove".

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline (request parsing,
`ReadOnlyMode`, `LookupNotFound`, `LookupFoundTooMany`, …) may
also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable, code-specific details. See `code` for the keys
each variant carries.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
The collection does not contain all objects requested to remove

Package: objects
Code: CollectionRemoveNotAllObjectsFound

CollectionForbiddenError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/collection*` endpoints when the user is not
allowed to perform the operation. The `code` distinguishes a missing
collection/object permission (`RightRequired`), the owner lacking a
grantable right when sharing (`CollectionObjectInsufficientRights`), and a
missing system right (`SystemRightRequired`).

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `RightRequired` — the dominant 403: the user lacks a required
    permission on the collection or its objects. The collection
    endpoints check `BAG_READ` / `BAG_WRITE` / `BAG_DELETE` /
    `BAG_CREATE` / `BAG_ACL` on the collection, and `LINK` / `UNLINK`
    on the objects for the object operations
    (`push` / `splice` / `objects` / `remove`).
    `parameters.right` is the missing right's key (lowercase, e.g.
    `bag_write`, `link`, `unlink`) and `parameters.rightdisplayname`
    its localized name.
  * `CollectionObjectInsufficientRights` — only on writes that share
    rights: the collection's `_owner` lacks a grantable right for one
    of the linked objects, so the collection's ACL cannot be applied.
    `parameters` carries `systemobjectid`, `displayname`, `owner`,
    `right` and `rightdisplayname`.
  * `SystemRightRequired` — the user lacks a system right needed by
    the request (for example to bypass certain ACL checks).
    `parameters.rights` lists the rights that would have satisfied
    the check.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `403`.

example.error:
Current user is missing permission "bag_write" ("View & Edit Collection").

Package: rightcheck
Code: RightRequired

EmailSendError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/system/sendmail` and
`/api/v1/system/sendmail/test` when the email could not be handed off
to the MTA. The send-attempt also writes an `EMAIL_SENT_ERROR` event.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Codes raised from the email
package:

  * `EmailSent` — sending the message failed (template render
    failure, MTA rejection, recipient validation, attachment
    fetch). `parameters.msg` carries the underlying message.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. For `EmailSent`, `parameters.msg` is the
underlying transport / template error.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
Sending email failed: dial tcp smtp.example.com:25: connect: connection refused

Package: email
Code: EmailSent

parameters.msg: dial tcp smtp.example.com:25: connect: connection refused

ShareLinkError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/system/share_link*` endpoints when the
request could not be processed. The `code` field identifies the
specific reason. Note that the `realm` is `share_link`, not `api` —
share-link errors are tracked in their own realm.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Codes raised from the
share_link package:

  * `ShareLinkPathInvalid` — the `path` field in the create
    payload is empty, malformed, or does not start with `/`.
  * `ShareLinkAliasInvalid` — the optional `alias` field contains
    characters outside the RFC 3986 unreserved set, or starts /
    ends with a non-alphanumeric character.
  * `ShareLinkExpirationDateInPast` — `expires_at` is in the past
    (or otherwise rejected by the validator).
  * `ShareLinkAlreadyExists` — a share link with the same path
    (and alias, if given) is already on file.
  * `ShareLinkInvalid` — `GET /system/share_link/{id}` was called
    with an id that does not exist or that has expired.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable details.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `share_link`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
A shortened share link already exists for this configuration.

Package: share_link
Code: ShareLinkAlreadyExists

EasError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/eas*` endpoints when the request could not
be processed. The `code` field identifies which validation rejected
the request.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Codes raised from the eas
pipeline:

  * `FileLimitExceeded` — the upload exceeds `fylr.eas.upload.max_filesize`.
    `parameters.filesize` and `parameters.limit` carry the offending and
    allowed sizes.
  * `UploadError` — an error occurred while reading the upload body.
    `parameters.filename` carries the file name from the multipart
    part. Wrapped underlying error in `error`.
  * `ProduceOriginalOnly` — `POST /eas/produce` was called against a
    non-original version. Only originals can be rotated or cropped.
  * `ProduceExtensionNotSupported` — the source file extension is not
    one /eas/produce can read. `parameters.extension` and
    `parameters.supported_extensions` carry the input and the allow-list.
  * `ProduceFormatNotSupported` — the requested output format is not
    one /eas/produce can write. `parameters.format` and
    `parameters.supported_formats` carry the input and the allow-list.
  * `EasNoParentID` — `POST /eas/put` (when creating a new version)
    received a request without a `parent_id` reference.
  * `SkipHeadRequestError` — `POST /eas/rput` for a remote URL could
    not derive a `filename` from the upstream HEAD response.
    `parameters.url` carries the remote URL.
  * `EasEmptyFilename` — `POST /eas/rput` received an empty filename.
  * `EasInvalidFilename` — `POST /eas/rput` received a filename that
    fails validation. `parameters.filename` carries the offending input.
  * `EasRputError` — `POST /eas/rput` could not fetch the remote
    resource. `parameters.url` and `parameters.msg` carry the upstream
    target and underlying error.
  * `EasRputErrorContentLength` — `POST /eas/rput` got no usable
    `Content-Length` from the remote. `parameters.url` and
    `parameters.content_length` carry the upstream target and the
    (invalid) length.
  * `CustomMetadataRecipeNotFound` — a metadata extraction recipe
    referenced from the upload payload is not present in any active
    config (often a side-effect of an unsupported file extension).
    `parameters.recipe` carries the recipe name.

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline (request parsing, SSRF
block on the upstream URL for /eas/rput, `ReadOnlyMode`, ...) may
also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable, code-specific details. See `code` for the keys
each variant carries.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
Allowed filesize 100.0 MB exceeded

Package: fileupload
Code: FileLimitExceeded

EasNotFoundError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/eas/download/*`, `/eas/zoom/*` and
`/eas/iiif/*` when the addressed **file id** does not exist. A wrong
`hash`, wrong `version`, or an invalid / expired `x-fylr-signature`
is a `403` `ForbiddenError`, not this `404`.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `ResourceNotFound` — the addressed file id does not exist.
    `parameters.resource` carries an identifier for the missing
    target.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`. (This 404 is raised
from the `api` package for a missing file id, and from
`filedownload` when the stored object URL cannot be resolved.)

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `resource` is a token identifying the
missing file (`file <file_id>` for the file-id-not-found path).

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `404`.

example.error:
Resource file 42 not found

Package: api
Code: ResourceNotFound

GroupApi.description:
A user **group**. Bundles a set of users together so the ACL system
can address them collectively.

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as a group.

_id.description: Server-issued numeric id of the group. Required for updates.

_version.description: Version of the group; increment on each update.

reference.description:
Optional unique reference. Lets clients address the group by a
stable string identifier instead of `_id`.

name.description: Internal name of the group (unique).

frontend_prefs.description: Arbitrary frontend-only preferences stored with the group.

type.description:
Group type. One of `easydb` (a normal user group) or `system`
(an internal, immutable group). New groups default to `easydb`.
Creating a group with `type: system` is rejected with
`GroupTypeNotAllowed`, and an existing group's `type` cannot be
changed (`GroupTypeChangeNotAllowed`).

lookup:_id.description:
Optional lookup descriptor used to resolve `_id` from the
group's `reference` server-side.

_generated_rights.description:
Read-only per-session derivation of the rights the current user
holds on this group.

_system_rights.description: System rights granted to members of this group.

_pseudonymization.description: Pseudonymization configuration for members of this group.

_auth_method_group_maps.description:
Mappings between external auth providers (LDAP / SAML / OAuth)
and this group — controls which incoming auth subjects are
assigned to the group.

_ip_subnet_filter.description:
CIDR / subnet entries. If set, members of this group can only sign
in from a matching network — unless `_ip_subnet_filter_exclude` is
true, in which case the matching networks are denied.

_two_fa_required.description:
If true, members of this group must pass a second authentication factor
(email one-time code / TOTP) at login, when two-factor authentication is
enabled in the base config. Defaults to false.

_owner.description:
The group's owner. The grant `who._owner: true` resolves to
this principal.

_created_at.description: UTC time the group was created.

_updated_at.description: UTC time the group was last updated.

UserError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/user*` endpoints when the request could not
be processed. The `code` field identifies which validation rejected
the request.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. Codes raised by the user
management code in the `acl` package:

  * `UserNotFound` — no stored user matches the id in the URL of
    `GET /user/{id}` (returned as `400`, not `404`).
  * `DeleteSystemUser` — attempt to delete a system user
    (`system:root`, …). System users are immutable.
  * `UserDeleteSelf` — the requester is attempting to delete
    their own account.
  * `UserLimitNotAllowed` — the limit operation would lock the
    requester (or any other system user) out.
  * `UserDeletePolicy` — `delete_policy` was supplied but is not
    allowed for user deletion.
  * `UserArchived` — the user payload is `_archived` and cannot
    be saved (only un-archived users can be modified).
  * `UserPictureNoRights` — the user picture supplied was not
    uploaded by the current user.
  * `UserTypeNotAllowed` — the requested user `type` (e.g.
    `easydb`, `easydb_idm`) is not enabled on this instance.
  * `UnableToSetPasswordForUserType` — the user `type` does not
    allow setting a password (e.g. SSO-only types).
  * `ColumnNeedsSystemRoot` — a property in the payload may only
    be set by a user holding `system.root`.
  * `ColumnCanNotBeUpdated` — a property is set-once and cannot be
    changed after the user is created.
  * `NoPassword` — `/user/change_password` was called without
    providing the new password.
  * `WrongPassword` — `/user/change_password` was called with a
    wrong current password.
  * `PasswordChangedRequired` — the user must change their
    password before they can perform the requested operation.
  * `UserEmailMalformed` — the email field is not RFC-acceptable.

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline (request parsing,
`ReadOnlyMode`, …) may also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `400`.

example.error:
The email "not-an-email" is not acceptable.

Package: acl
Code: UserEmailMalformed

UserConflictError.description:
Error returned by `POST /api/v1/user` (create or update) when a user
with the same login or email already exists.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. One of:

  * `UserDuplicateEmail` — another user has the same email
    address. `parameters.email` carries the email,
    `parameters.name` the name of the existing user.
  * `UserDuplicateLogin` — another user has the same login.
    `parameters.login` and `parameters.name` carry the details.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description:
Machine-readable details. `email` / `login` carry the offending
value, `name` carries the name of the existing user.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: The HTTP status code, repeated here. Always `409`.

example.error:
Email "alice@example.com" already exists. User: alice

Package: acl
Code: UserDuplicateEmail

UserApi.description:
A **user** record. Used as the request and response body of the
`/api/v1/user*` endpoints.

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as a user.

_id.description: Server-issued numeric id. Required for updates.

_version.description: Version; increment on each update.

login.description: Login name (unique). Required for password-based user types.

reference.description:
Optional unique reference. Lets clients address the user by a
stable string identifier instead of `_id`.

type.description:
User type (e.g. `easydb`, `easydb_ldap`, `easydb_sso`,
`easydb_oauth`, `system`). Determines whether a password can
be set and which auth pipeline is consulted on login.

frontend_language.description: Preferred UI language as a BCP-47 tag (`de-DE`, `en-US`, ...).

database_languages.description: Languages the user is allowed to edit / see data values in.

frontend_prefs.description: Arbitrary frontend-only preferences stored on the user.

custom_data.description: Custom-data values (per the datamodel's user customisation).

displayname.description:
Rendered display name. Either set explicitly or derived from
`first_name` / `last_name` / `login`.

login_disabled.description: If true, the user cannot sign in.

login_valid_from.description:
Lower bound of the login window. Logins before this date are
rejected with `LoginValidFrom`.

login_valid_to.description:
Upper bound of the login window. Logins after this date are
rejected with `LoginValidTo`.

require_password_change.description:
If true, the next successful login must immediately change
the password before any other operation succeeds.

mail_schedule.description: When (and how often) fylr sends notification mails to this user.

email.description:
Primary email address. Must be RFC-acceptable; otherwise
`UserEmailMalformed` is raised on save.

confirm_email.description:
If true, the user has to confirm their email by clicking the
link sent on save before login is allowed.

confirm_email_sent_at.description: When the confirmation mail was last sent.

email_confirmed_at.description: When the user confirmed their email.

welcome_email.description: If true, a welcome mail is sent on user creation.

welcome_email_sent_at.description: When the welcome mail was sent.

lookup:_id.description:
Optional lookup descriptor used to resolve `_id` from the
user's `reference` server-side.

_generated_rights.description:
Read-only per-session derivation of the rights the requester
holds on this user.

_groups.description:
Groups this user belongs to. On write, the array may carry full
group payloads or just `{_id: N}` references.

_password.description:
Plaintext password (write-only). Set on user creation to seed a
password; otherwise use `POST /user/change_password`. Never echoed
back in responses.

_system_rights.description: System rights granted directly to the user.

_owner.description:
The user's owner. The grant `who._owner: true` resolves to
this principal.

_collection_pin_codes.description:
Per-collection PIN codes the user holds (for pin-protected
collections).

_password_hash.description:
The stored password hash. Only returned when `include_password=true`
is set and the requester holds `system.root` — used by backup
tooling.

_two_factor_totp_enrolled.description:
Read-only. True when the user has a confirmed authenticator-app (TOTP)
second factor enrolled (ticket #78550). Surfaced in the admin user editor.

_two_factor_passkey_enrolled.description:
Read-only. True when the user has a passkey (WebAuthn) second factor
enrolled (ticket #78550).

_two_factor_last_method.description:
Read-only. The two-factor method the user last completed; a returning
user is sent straight to it at login (switchable on the page).

_two_factor_reset_totp.description:
Write-only action. Set `true` to clear the user's authenticator (TOTP)
enrollment, forcing re-enrollment at next login. Available to admins via
the user editor and to the user themselves via the settings dialog
(self-service). The user's other two-factor state is preserved across
normal edits.

_two_factor_reset_passkey.description:
Write-only action like `_two_factor_reset_totp`, clearing the user's
passkey (WebAuthn) enrollment.

_created_at.description: UTC time the user was created.

_updated_at.description: UTC time the user was last updated.

_archived_at.description:
UTC time the user was archived. Archived users cannot sign in
(login fails with `LoginUserArchived`) and cannot be modified.

_last_seen_at.description: UTC time of the user's most recent activity.

SessionApi.description:
A **session** descriptor — returned by `GET /api/v1/user/session`.
Carries the access token, the authenticated user and their groups,
the system rights they hold, available authentication methods, and
instance-wide defaults the front end needs to render correctly.

access_token.description:
The session's access token. Use it as the `Bearer` token (or in
the `x-fylr-authorization` / `Authorization` header) for
subsequent requests.

groups.description: Groups the authenticated user belongs to.

config.description:
Per-session configuration block — feature flags, theme,
cache-id, and other values the front end consults at every page
load.

frontend_language.description:
The user's preferred UI language as a BCP-47 tag (`de-DE`,
`en-US`, ...).

database_locales.description:
Locales the user is allowed to edit data values in (a richer
view of `user.database_languages` with locale metadata).

system_rights.description:
Resolved system-right map. Keys are right names
(`system.root`, `system.export`, ...); values describe the
effective grant.

authentication_methods.description:
The authentication methods this instance exposes for the
session.

authenticated.description:
The authentication method the current session was established
with. Omitted for anonymous sessions.

frontend_locale.description:
Locale metadata (number format, calendar patterns) for the
user's `frontend_language`.

current_max_event_id.description:
The latest `_id` issued in the event log when the session was
built. Pass it as `fromEventId` to `/api/v1/event/poll/{fromEventId}`
to subscribe to subsequent events.

instance.description:
Instance-wide settings the front end needs on every page load
(display name, external URL, support address, version, ...).

PoolApi.description:
A storage **pool** — fylr's hierarchical container for objects, ACL
rules, tag policy and per-objecttype default masks.

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as a pool.

pool.description: The mutable pool data.

_id.description: Server-issued numeric id. Required for updates.

reference.description:
Optional unique reference. Lets clients address the pool by a
stable string identifier instead of `_id`.

_id_parent.description:
Parent pool id. `null`/absent for root pools. Creating or
re-parenting a pool under a parent requires the `bag_create`
right on that parent; other changes may require additional
BAG rights (`bag_write`, `bag_acl`). A non-root pool saved
without a parent is rejected with code `PoolNeedsParent`.

lookup:_id_parent.description:
Optional lookup descriptor for `_id_parent` — resolve the
parent pool by `reference` server-side.

description.description: Localized long description of the pool.

contact.description: The pool's contact principal (user or group).

is_system_pool.description:
Read-only marker for built-in system pools. It cannot be set
or changed through the API — sending a value that creates or
changes it is rejected with code `UnableToSetIsSystemPool`.

watermark.description:
Watermark configuration applied to image variants produced
from objects in this pool. Changing it on save can trigger a
202 re-sync prompt (see the `confirm` parameter on POST/PUT).

caption.description:
Per-field caption configuration for objects in this pool.
Changing it on save can trigger a 202 re-sync prompt.

janitor_policy.description:
Janitor (retention/clean-up) policy applied to objects in
this pool.

custom_data.description:
Custom-data values attached to the pool (per the datamodel's
pool customisation).

mapping_dc_export.description:
Dublin-Core export mapping for the pool — a mapping
`reference` (string) or its numeric id.

mapping_image_export.description:
Image-export mapping for the pool — a mapping `reference`
(string) or its numeric id.

mapping_image_import.description:
Image-import mapping for the pool — a mapping `reference`
(string) or its numeric id.

_has_children.description:
Read-only flag indicating that at least one sub-pool exists.
Useful for tree-view rendering without an extra query.

_private_acl.description:
If true, this pool's ACL is private — it does not inherit ACL
items from its parent.

_generated_rights.description:
Read-only per-session derivation of the rights the requester
holds on this pool.

_private_transitions.description:
If true, this pool's transitions are private — sub-pools do not
inherit them.

_transitions.description: Workflow transitions exposed on this pool.

_private_tags.description:
If true, this pool's tags are private — sub-pools do not inherit
them.

_tags.description: Per-pool tag definitions (override the global tag set).

_level.description: 0 for root pools, +1 for every descent into the tree.

_path.description:
Array of ancestor pools from the root to the immediate parent,
each rendered in the `standard` format. Useful for breadcrumbs.
The recursion is broken at render time via PoolApi's
`x-recursion: true` marker.

_owner.description:
The pool's owner. The grant `who._owner: true` resolves to
this principal.

_compiled_tags.description:
Effective tag set — the merge of this pool's tags with the
inherited tags from ancestors. Each entry is rendered in the
standard tag format.

_standard_masks.description:
Per-objecttype default mask preference. Map of objecttype id to
an ordered array of mask ids. The first entry is the preferred
mask for that objecttype within this pool.

EventApi.description:
An **event** record. fylr writes events for almost every server-side
mutation (object insert/update, collection changes, ACL changes,
email sends, login, ...).

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as an event.

_id.description:
Server-issued, monotonically-increasing event id. Used as the
cursor by `/api/v1/event/poll/{fromEventId}`.

type.description:
Event type, e.g. `COLLECTION_INSERT`, `OBJECT_UPDATE`,
`EMAIL_SENT`, `USER_LOGIN`. The full set is defined by
fylr and grows over time — clients should treat unknown
values as opaque.

object_id.description:
Object id (per-objecttype) the event relates to. Omitted for
system-level events.

object_version.description: Object version at the time the event was written.

schema.description:
Datamodel schema the event was recorded under (`user`,
`system`, ...).

basetype.description:
The basetype of the affected entity (`object`, `collection`,
`user`, `group`, ...).

objecttype.description: Objecttype name when `basetype: object`.

timestamp.description: UTC time the event was written.

pollable.description:
Whether this event is surfaced through the long-poll / stream
endpoints (vs. only being available via `/event/list`).

info.description:
Event-specific structured payload. The keys depend on `type`
— for example object events carry `field`, `old_value` and
`new_value`; email events carry `from`, `to` and `subject`.

remote_addr.description:
IP address of the request that produced the event, when
available.

user_generated_displayname.description:
Rendered display name of the user that produced the event
(snapshot at event time — does not update when the user's
display name later changes).

user.description:
Snapshot of the user (or group) that produced the event.
Omitted for system-generated events.

MessageApi.description:
An internal **message** addressed to one or more user groups. Used by
fylr to display admin notifications, maintenance announcements and
release notes inside the front end.

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as a message.

_groups.description:
Groups the message is addressed to. Only users in at least one
of these groups see the message.

_id.description: Server-issued numeric id. Required for updates.

_version.description:
Object version. Send `1` to create a new message. To update,
send the existing version + 1 together with `_id`; a mismatch
returns `VersionMismatch` (400).

webfrontend_props.description:
Free-form key/value pairs for the front end to consume
(display location, theme, dismiss button text override, ...).

confirm_every_version.description:
If true, a confirmation only counts for the exact `_version`
it was made on, so each new version must be confirmed again.
If false, any prior confirmation counts.

show_always.description:
If true, the message is shown on every page load until
confirmed (or until the `end_time` passes). For `show_always`
messages, a confirmation older than 60s is ignored so the
message reappears.

reference.description:
Optional unique reference. Useful when seeding messages from
tooling.

client_ids.description:
Restrict the message to specific front-end client ids
(e.g. mobile app only). Empty means show in every client.

start_time.description:
When the message becomes visible, wrapped as
`{ "value": <date-time> }`. Null/omitted means visible
immediately. The value is truncated to whole seconds (UTC).

end_time.description:
When the message stops being visible, wrapped as
`{ "value": <date-time> }`. Null/omitted means visible until
confirmed. Truncated to whole seconds (UTC). Must not be
before `start_time` (else `StartTimeAfterEndTime`, 400).

PublishApi.description:
A **publish record** — fylr's note that an object was published in an
external system. fylr does not produce or host the publication itself;
this entry links the object to the **collector** it was published
through and to the external URL where the publication lives.

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as a publish.

_timestamp_created.description: UTC time the publish entry was created.

version.description:
Optional. When set (> 0) the publication is pinned to that
exact object version; when omitted it tracks the object's
current version. Echoed in responses only when an exact
version was pinned. **fylr** performs no range validation on
this value.

system_object_id.description: The `_system_object_id` of the object being published.

collector.description:
Name of the publish collector — must match a collector
configured in the base config (`internalname`), otherwise the
request is rejected with `PublishUnknownCollector`. Example:
`datacite`.

publish_uri.description:
The URL clients can use to reach the published artifact.
Stored verbatim (only surrounding whitespace is trimmed); fylr
does not validate it as a URI. Must be non-empty, else the
request is rejected with `PublishInvalidData`.

easydb_uri.description:
The URL clients can use to reach the source object inside
fylr (deep-link back into the editor). Stored verbatim (only
surrounding whitespace is trimmed); fylr does not validate it
as a URI. Must be non-empty, else the request is rejected with
`PublishInvalidData`.

ExportApi.description:
An **export** — a long-running job that bundles a set of objects
(and optionally their files) into a downloadable archive (TAR.GZ or
ZIP).

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as an export.

type.description:
Export kind. `export` produces a fresh archive every run.
`export_incremental` only includes objects that changed since
the last run. `download` is a single-shot package.

name.description: Human-readable name.

search.description:
The search descriptor that selects which objects to include
(same shape as `POST /api/v1/search`).

fields.description:
Per-field export selection. Each KEY is a search/column
api-name (e.g. `objecttype.field`); unknown keys are rejected,
and the field's required merge depth must not exceed
`merge_max_depth`. Each VALUE is an object whose only
meaningful property is `display_name`.

display_name.description: Optional display name for the exported field.

eas_fields.description:
Per-asset-field export options, keyed by an asset field's
api-name (`objecttype.field`). The key must resolve to a
`files`-type column within `merge_max_depth`.

classes.description:
Per-file-class rendition defaults. Keyed by file class name
(e.g. `image`, `audio`, `video`); each value is the ordered
list of renditions emitted for every file of that class,
unless overridden per file via `assets`.

assets.description:
Per-file override, keyed by file id. Each value is `true`
(use the class defaults), `false` (skip the file), or an
object selecting an explicit version list.

csv.description:
CSV output configuration. Either the literal boolean `false`
(no CSV output) or a configuration object. Absent CSV is
echoed back as `false`.

localized_column_names.description:
If true, column headers use localized display names
instead of api-names.

hierarchy.description:
How object hierarchy/path is rendered in a column.
Empty string is treated as unset; any other value is
rejected.

include_changelog.description:
Either a boolean (legacy: true enables the changelog
with user, false clears it) or an object.

with_user.description: If true, include the changelog user column.

use_bom.description: If true, prepend a UTF-8 BOM to the CSV file.

xml.description: Whether to include an XML representation.

xml_one_file_per_object.description:
If true, every object becomes its own XML file; otherwise a
single XML containing all objects.

mapping.description:
XML mapping selection. `null` defaults to `easydb`. Accepts
the string aliases `easydb` / `easydb_flat`, or a numeric
mapping-profile id (a positive integer). Other values are
rejected.

xslt_xml_post_processing.description:
XSLT post-processing applied to the rendered XML. References a
stylesheet file by id; that file must have a `.xsl` or `.xslt`
extension.

_id.description: File id of the XSLT stylesheet to apply.

xlsx.description:
XLSX (Excel) output configuration. Either the literal boolean
`false` (no XLSX output) or a configuration object. Absent
XLSX is echoed back as `false`.

localized_column_names.description:
If true, column headers use localized display names
instead of api-names.

hierarchy.description:
How object hierarchy/path is rendered in a column.
Empty string is treated as unset; other values are
rejected.

include_changelog.description:
Either a boolean (legacy) or an object enabling the
changelog.

with_user.description: If true, include the changelog user column.

include_thumbnail.description: If true, embed object thumbnails in the XLSX output.

json.description: Whether to include a JSON representation.

json_one_file_per_object.description:
If true, every object becomes its own JSON file; otherwise a
single JSON containing all objects.

merge_linked_objects.description:
Controls whether (and how) objects linked from the main
search result are pulled into the export.

merge_max_depth.description: Maximum recursion depth when following linked objects.

include_parents.description:
If true, include parent objects when an included object
references a parent.

file_url_expire_days.description:
Number of days the file URLs embedded in the export remain
valid. Defaults to a server-side setting.

produce_options.description:
Options for the export *producer*. Only the reserved key
`plugin` is interpreted by fylr; every other key is passed
through verbatim to the selected producer. `plugin` selects
the producer: a value containing a colon is resolved as
`<plugin-name>:<callback-name>` against the loaded plugins
(callback type `export`); a value without a colon is an
internal producer name (`json`, `xml`, `csv`, `file`, `iiif`,
`html`, `xlsx`). A non-string `plugin` is rejected.

plugin.description:
Producer selector. `<plugin>:<callback>` resolves a plugin
export callback; a colon-less value is an internal
producer name.

all_languages.description:
If true, export all configured languages for localized fields
instead of only the standard/requested languages.

file_metadata.description: If true, include file (asset) metadata in the export output.

no_inheritance.description:
If true, do not resolve inherited field values; exported
objects carry only their own field values.

batch_size.description:
Number of objects processed per batch. `null` (or omitted)
means no explicit batch size. When set it must be between 0
and 10000 inclusive, otherwise the save is rejected.

filename_original.description:
If true, exported files keep their original filename. Mutually
exclusive with `filename_template`.

filename_template.description:
Template used to build exported filenames. Mutually exclusive
with `filename_original`.

_date_created.description: UTC time the export was created.

_last_updated_at.description: UTC time the export configuration was last updated.

_last_run_at.description: UTC time the most recent production run started.

_last_relevant_object_count.description:
Number of objects included in the most recent run that had at
least one change since the previous run.

_last_relevant_run_at.description:
UTC time of the most recent run that actually produced output
(vs. a run that found nothing new).

_next_run_at.description:
UTC time the next scheduled run is expected. Set by the
`_schedules` entries.

_state.description:
Current export run state. `new` after create/update; `pending`
once a run is scheduled; `processing` while a run is active;
`done` / `done_with_warnings` on success; `failed` on error or
after a stop.

_plugin_log.description:
Lines of plugin-emitted log output captured during the most
recent run. Cleared at the start of each run.

_files.description:
Produced artifact files of the most recent run. Hidden files are
omitted from regular (non-plugin) API output.

path.description:
URL-path-escaped relative path of the produced file inside
the export package.

size.description:
File size in bytes. Omitted when the size is unknown (e.g.
files produced on the fly); a missing `size` therefore means
"unknown", distinct from a real 0.

_log.description:
Per-run event log — start/stop, validation failures, transport
results.

_transports.description:
Configured delivery transports and their per-transport result.
Round-trips between request and response.

plugin.description:
Transport selector. Empty string for an internal/download
transport, otherwise `<plugin>:<callback>`.

options.description:
Free-form transport options whose accepted keys depend on
the selected transport plugin. Secret values are hidden on
output; an absent options object is rendered as `{}`.

email.description:
Email-delivery settings. Present only when a message or
recipients exist.

external_url.description: External download URL for the transport (set server-side).

_schedules.description:
Schedules controlling automatic re-runs of the export. Same
shape as `user.mail_schedule`.

PresetApi.description:
A **right preset** — a reusable bundle of ACL grants (and an optional
tag filter) that can be applied to objects or collections. Presets
live per context (`object` or `collection`).

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as a right preset.

_id.description: Server-issued numeric id. Required for updates.

_position.description:
Display position. Lower positions render before higher ones
in the preset picker.

preset.description: The actual permission bundle this preset represents.

default_expiration_hours.description:
How many hours after the preset is applied the granted
ACL items expire. If null, grants are permanent until
revoked.

rights.description:
The right items the preset grants. Keys are right names
(`read`, `write`, `delete`, `acl`, ...); each value is
a RightItemApi (same per-right configuration shape used
inside RightApi.rights).

tagfilter.description:
Optional tag-filter that restricts which objects this
preset can apply to (e.g. only objects tagged
`confidential`).

BackupApi.description:
A **backup** descriptor — a record of a single backup run produced by
`PUT /api/v1/system/backup/new`.

created_at.description: UTC time the backup was created.

finished_at.description:
UTC time the backup completed. Null while the backup is still
running.

create_user.description:
The user that triggered the backup. Omitted for backups
triggered by the scheduler.

stats.description:
Stats captured at the end of the run — file size, MIME type
and file name of the produced artifact.

opts.description:
The options the backup was started with — format and whether
the event log was included.

log.description: Per-run log lines emitted by the backup pipeline.

LocationApi.description:
A **storage location** descriptor — an external bucket where fylr
reads / writes files. Used by `/api/v1/system/location*` and
surfaced read-only on every file download via the
`X-Fylr-Storage-Location` response header.

id.description:
Server-issued numeric id. On create, must be set to the same
value that appears in the URL `/system/location/{id}` (or
`0` for `POST /system/location/new`).

created_at.description: UTC time the location was created.

name.description: Human-readable label.

kind.description:
Storage driver — `file` (local disk), `s3` (AWS / S3-compatible),
or `azure` (Azure Blob Storage). Selects which `config` shape
(`file` / `s3` / `azure`) is expected.

prefix.description:
Optional bucket prefix — every key written to the location is
prepended with it. Usually ends in `/`.

config.description:
Driver-specific configuration. Secrets (access keys, account
passwords, ...) are removed on read; they must be supplied
again on update.

allow_purge.description:
Whether `POST /api/v1/system/purgeall` may delete files from
this location. Disable to keep an archival location intact.

allow_redirect.description:
Whether file downloads from this location may use HTTP
`Location:` redirects (for remote buckets with their own URL
signers).

read_only.description:
Whether this location accepts writes. If true, new files are
sent to the next writable location and only reads come from
here.

status_msg.description:
Read-only diagnostic message attached to a non-`connected`
`status` — usually the underlying error from the driver.

TransitionApi.description:
A **workflow transition** descriptor — one rule in the
admin-managed transition set. `GET` / `POST /api/v1/transitions`
read and replace this set; the rules themselves fire implicitly
through the object pipeline (insert / update / delete / tag), not
via a separate call.

type.description:
Transition kind. One of `resolve`, `reject`, `exit_resolve`,
`exit_reject`, `process`. Any other value is rejected with a
`ServerGeneric` error (HTTP 400).

operations.description:
Object-pipeline operations the transition runs on. Each item is
one of `INSERT`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE` (uppercase). An empty array
is allowed and disables the transition. Any other value is
rejected with a `ServerGeneric` error (HTTP 400).

Differs from easydb 5: easydb 5 requires a non-empty array;
**fylr** accepts an empty array (which disables the transition).

comment.description:
Admin-facing comment / note (not user-visible).

Differs from easydb 5: this field does not exist in easydb 5; it
is a **fylr** addition.

confirm.description:
Confirmation prompt shown to the user before the transition
executes. Optional — empty for transitions that do not require
explicit confirmation.

sticky.description:
Boolean flag stored on the transition and round-tripped
verbatim by this endpoint.

who_not.description:
If true, the `who` list is interpreted as a deny-list instead
of an allow-list — every user EXCEPT those in `who` can
invoke the transition.

Differs from easydb 5: this field does not exist in easydb 5; it
is a **fylr** addition.

objecttype_ids.description:
Object types the transition applies to. Empty means it
applies to every object type.

tagfilter:before.description:
Tagfilter the target object must satisfy BEFORE the transition
runs. The optional `changed` slot — set only on transitions —
identifies the tags whose addition / removal should *trigger*
the transition.

tagfilter:after.description:
Tagfilter the target object will satisfy AFTER the transition
runs (used by the engine to enforce the post-condition).

type.description:
Action discriminator. Built-in values: `set_tags`,
`change_owner`, `email`, `webhook`. Any other string is
routed to a plugin action runner (e.g.
`fylr_example:set_comment`).

Differs from easydb 5: `change_owner` is a **fylr**
addition (easydb 5's built-in actions are only `set_tags`,
`email`, and `webhook`, plus plugin actions).

actions.description:
Ordered list of actions the transition performs. Each action is
`{type, info}`; the parameters live under `info`, not at the top
level.

who.description:
Users / groups that may invoke this transition (or, if
`who_not` is true, the deny-list). Same shape as the `who`
slot on RightApi grants.

SchemaApi.description:
A fylr **datamodel** snapshot at a specific version — the JSON-Schema
view that `/api/db` and `/api/search` validate against.

This is the shape `GET /api/v1/schema/user/{version}` returns when
the `format` query parameter is unset (or any value other than `svg`
/ `png`).

version.description:
The datamodel version this snapshot represents. Every datamodel
record carries a version — HEAD's version equals the version a
future commit would produce. To distinguish HEAD from a committed
snapshot, check `committed_at` (null on HEAD).

based_on_version.description:
The committed datamodel version this snapshot derives from. For
a committed version, equals `version - 1`. For `HEAD`, the last
committed version this working copy was forked off.

based_on_base_version.description:
The base-version (pre-customisation) the snapshot derives from.
Used by upgrades to track the upstream baseline separately from
per-instance edits.

max_table_id.description:
Highest `table_id` used in this snapshot. New tables added in a
subsequent edit start at `max_table_id + 1`.

max_column_id.description:
Highest column id used in this snapshot. New columns added in a
subsequent edit start at `max_column_id + 1`.

type.description:
Always `user` — the user-defined datamodel. (fylr also has a
`system` datamodel; that one is not exposed through /schema.)

tables.description:
The objecttypes (tables) in this datamodel. Each entry is a
`SchemaTableApi`. Which entries are present is
**instance-dependent** — it reflects the customer's actual
objecttypes (`picture`, `event`, `person`, ...).

SchemaTableApi.description:
A single **objecttype** (table) within a datamodel snapshot. Each
entry in `SchemaApi.tables` is one of these.

name.description:
Stable, machine-readable name of the objecttype (used as the
`{objecttype}` path parameter on `/api/v1/db/{objecttype}` and
as the key on `/api/v1/objecttype/{id}`).

table_id.description:
Server-issued numeric id, unique within the datamodel. Survives
renames of the objecttype.

pool_link.description:
If true, objects of this type live inside a pool — their `_pool`
property identifies the containing pool. If false, objects are
not pool-scoped (instance-global).

owned_by.description:
When present, this table is a nested table — records ship
embedded inside top-level objects of the objecttype the
descriptor's `other_table_id` points at, and have no
independent ACL or owner. Absent on regular top-level
objecttypes.

acl_table.description:
If true, objects of this type carry their own `_acl` and
respect per-object ACL grants. If false, ACL inheritance from
the parent pool / collection applies.

has_tags.description:
If true, objects of this type accept `_tags` (tag references).
The available tags come from `/api/v1/tags`.

is_hierarchical.description:
If true, this objecttype forms a **single-parent** tree —
each object can have at most one parent via `_id_parent`.
Mutually exclusive with `polyhierarchical`.

polyhierarchical.description:
If true, this objecttype forms a **DAG** — each object can
have any number of parents, exposed through the
`_object_parents` system column. Mutually exclusive with
`is_hierarchical`; also incompatible with `acl_table: true`
(the datamodel validator rejects both combinations).

in_main_search.description:
If true, objects of this type are indexed into the main search
index and surface in `/api/v1/search` results by default.

columns.description:
Ordered list of columns. The order matters for default rendering
in masks that don't explicitly reorder.

foreign_keys.description:
Foreign-key declarations on this table — references to other
objecttypes' records used to look up linked data.

unique_keys.description:
Unique-key constraints. Violations surface as
`DatabaseUniqueKeyViolation` on `POST /api/v1/db/{objecttype}`.

bidirectional.description:
Bidirectional-link declarations — pairs of columns on different
objecttypes that mirror each other (writing the link on one side
automatically materialises the reverse link on the other).

ObjecttypeApi.description:
An **objecttype** descriptor — fylr's per-record-type configuration
layered on top of the datamodel definition: per-objecttype default
masks, exposure flags (frontend menus, collections, facets), ACL
rules, tags, transitions, mask / column filters, asset filename
policy, watermark and janitor (cleanup) policy.

Returned by `/api/v1/objecttype` and `/api/v1/objecttype/{id}`. The
same shape is accepted as the request body of `POST /api/v1/objecttype`
(only the fields the requester is allowed to mutate are honoured).

_basetype.description: Fixed marker identifying this object as an objecttype.

objecttype.description: The mutable objecttype data.

_id.description:
Server-issued numeric id of the objecttype. Required for
updates. Stable across renames of `name`.

_version.description: Version; increment on each update.

name.description:
Stable, machine-readable name (used as the `{objecttype}`
path parameter on `/api/v1/db/{objecttype}`).

contact.description:
Vestigial field. Not backed by any objecttype storage: it is
always returned as `null` and ignored on `POST`. (Unlike the
pool's `contact`, this is never resolved to a user / group.)

mapping_image_export.description:
Reference to the image-export mapping: either the integer id
of a mapping, or the string `"none"`. Returned as `"none"`
when no mapping is set; any other value is rejected.

mapping_image_import.description:
Reference to the image-import mapping: either the integer id
of a mapping, or the string `"none"`. Returned as `"none"`
when no mapping is set; any other value is rejected.

mapping_image_import_recipe_configs.description:
Per-recipe configuration for the image-import mapping. Only
accepted when `mapping_image_import` names a mapping (not
`"none"`).

mapping_dc_export.description:
Reference to the Dublin Core export mapping: either the
integer id of a mapping, or the string `"none"`. Returned as
`"none"` when no mapping is set; any other value is rejected.

show_in_collections.description: Whether objects of this type can be linked into collections.

show_in_facet_grouping.description:
Whether the type appears as a top-level facet in the search
UI's grouping.

show_in_main_menu_with_icon.description:
Icon name to show next to the objecttype entry in the
front-end main menu. Empty / null suppresses the entry.

empty_placeholder_icon.description:
Icon shown in the detail view when an object of this type
has no file attached.

standard_numbering.description:
Legacy field. Present in the response (always empty) and
accepted in a `POST` body, but the value is ignored — it is
never stored or applied.

custom_data.description:
Custom-data values attached to the objecttype itself (per
the datamodel's objecttype customisation).

watermark.description:
Watermark configuration applied to image variants produced
from objects of this type.

caption.description:
Per-field caption configuration burned into image variants
produced for objects of this type. Keys are field names on
the objecttype.

janitor_policy.description:
Per-objecttype retention / purge policy enforced by the
janitor. Same shape as the pool-level janitor policy.

_standard_masks.description:
Ordered list of mask ids to prefer when rendering objects of
this type. The first entry is the default mask. `null` (rather
than an empty array) signals "no preference defined" — the
datamodel's standard mask is then used.

_export_asset_filenames.description:
Per-field filename templates used when exporting assets. Each key
is the full api name of a field on this objecttype that is either
a files column or a link to an objecttype that has files; an
unknown field is rejected with `UnknownField`, a field of any
other type with `UnsupportedFieldType`. The value is the
localised filename template (placeholders come from
`_filename_replacements`). Empty templates are dropped on save.

_acl.description:
ACL grants on this objecttype — typically the
`objecttype.read` / `objecttype.write` / `objecttype.delete`
rights.

_private_transitions.description:
If true, transitions configured directly on this objecttype do
not inherit transitions from the parent pool / global.

_transitions.description: Workflow transitions exposed on this objecttype.

_private_tags.description:
If true, the tag set configured on this objecttype shadows the
inherited tags (does not merge with them).

_compiled_tags.description:
Effective tag set — the merge of this objecttype's tags with
the inherited tags from pool / global.

_maskfilters.description:
Per-mask tag-filter overrides, keyed by mask id. A mask's
`tagfilter` restricts which objects the mask is offered for.

_columnfilters.description:
Per-column visibility filters — used to hide individual fields
from users that lack a matching tag / right.

_filename_replacements.description:
Read-only helper, computed server-side. Keys are the full api
names of the objecttype's files fields; each value is the list of
placeholder tokens (e.g. `%_asset.original_filename%`,
`%<field>%`) available for that field's `_export_asset_filenames`
template. Output only — ignored if sent in a `POST` body.

XmlMappingProfile.description:
A **profile** for XML / Exiftool / Dublin Core import or export — the
template from which concrete `XmlMappingApi` mappings are derived.
Profiles are loaded from `xmlmapping/<profile>.yml` files in the
base config (and from plugins that contribute their own).

Returned by `GET /api/v1/xmlmapping/profile/{profile}` and embedded
in each entry of `GET /api/v1/xmlmapping/list` (with its concrete
mappings appended under `mappings`).

profile.description:
Stable profile name as stored — this is the file's `profile:`
value and includes the source file extension, e.g. `dc.yml`.
This exact string is the `{profile}` path parameter of
`GET /xmlmapping/profile/{profile}` and the `XmlMappingApi.profile`
value of mappings built from it.

profile_type.description:
Capability flags. Determine which targets this profile can be
bound to when creating a concrete mapping.

xml_export.description: Profile can drive an XML export.

exiftool_export.description: Profile can drive an Exiftool-based metadata export.

exiftool_export_omit_empty.description:
When set, exiftool export skips mapping fields with no
value and no source — instead of emitting an empty
`-Tag=` wipe. Inherited from the profile to new mappings.

exiftool_import.description: Profile can drive an Exiftool-based metadata import.

dc_export.description: Profile can drive a Dublin Core export.

plugins_import.description:
When set, plugin-contributed import mapping definitions are
automatically merged into the profile on read.

xml_base.description:
Base XML namespace URL that the resulting XML documents anchor
themselves to. Mostly relevant for XML export.

tabs.description:
The profile's field layout, grouped into tabs (each tab is one
page in the front-end editor).

custom.description:
If true, the front end lets the user add custom fields
inside this tab.

_compiled_recipes.description:
Per-recipe compiled state — the recipes this profile uses at
runtime, keyed by recipe name. Read-only; managed by the
mapping pipeline.

_recipes_required.description:
Recipe names the mapping needs at runtime. The instance
must have all of them registered (via base config or a
plugin) for the mapping to be usable.

mappings.description:
Concrete mappings built from this profile. Always present
(possibly an empty array) on both `GET /xmlmapping/list` and
`GET /xmlmapping/profile/{profile}` — the list handler sets a
non-nil `mappings` slice for every profile and the per-profile
handler returns that same profile object unchanged.

XmlMappingProfileField.description:
A single field inside an XML-mapping profile tab. Describes the
XML / Exiftool / JSON source / target plus the fylr-side target
fields and the import strategy.

_id.description:
Stable field id (a slug derived from the profile file). Used
to correlate `XmlMappingApi.fields` entries with their profile
definition.

name.description: Human-facing field name (the front-end label).

strategy_import.description:
How the field combines incoming values on import — e.g.
`replace`, `append`, `merge`, `skip_if_present`.

type.description:
`Output` — exported; `Input` — imported only; `Splitter` — drives
sub-field decomposition; `Hidden` — internal helper, not exposed
in the editor.

text.description:
Display text for the editor. Accepts either a plain string or a
`LocaValue` (`{<lang>: <text>}`) for localised labels.

xml.description:
Free-form XML hint shown next to the field in the editor (for
reviewers who want to see the target XPath / element).

xml_export.description:
Per-source XML-export rules — XPath / attribute / value
formatters applied when this field's value is written out.

xml_import.description:
Per-source XML-import rules — the XPath / attribute / value
readers applied when an incoming document is parsed.

json_import.description:
JSON pointer used to read the field's value during a JSON
import.

json_import_type.description:
Column type the value should be coerced into when imported
(e.g. `text`, `number`, `date`).

easydb.description:
Fylr-side target fields the value flows into. Each entry
identifies a column path and any per-target transformation.

custom.description:
Marker set on fields the user added (not declared in the
profile YAML). Custom fields can be edited and deleted from
the front end.

x.description:
Optional grid column position used by the front-end layout
editor.

y.description:
Optional grid row position used by the front-end layout
editor.

recipe_name.description:
Recipe this field's compilation depends on. Set by the loader
when the profile references a recipe.

XmlMappingApi.description:
A concrete **XML mapping** — pairs a profile with a target
objecttype, the field bindings and any recipe configuration the
mapping needs at runtime.

Used as the request body of `PUT /api/v1/xmlmapping/mapping` and
`POST /api/v1/xmlmapping/mapping/{mapping}`, and as the response
body of `GET /api/v1/xmlmapping/mapping/{mapping}`.

id.description:
Server-issued numeric id. Empty / 0 on create; required on
update.

idTable.description:
Objecttype id this mapping targets. Required for every mapping
except global / cross-objecttype ones.

profile.description:
Profile name (`xmlmapping.Profile.profile`) the mapping is
built from. Must match an existing profile registered with the
instance.

profile_type.description:
Snapshot of the profile's capability flags at the time the
mapping was created. Used to surface stale-mapping warnings
when the underlying profile is updated.

xml_export.description: Read and persisted from the request body.

exiftool_export.description: Read and persisted from the request body.

exiftool_export_omit_empty.description: Read and persisted from the request body.

exiftool_import.description: Read and persisted from the request body.

dc_export.description:
Read-only on a mapping: returned in responses (it reflects the
mapping's stored flag) but ignored in `PUT`/`POST` request
bodies — the request decoder does not copy it.

plugins_import.description:
Profile-level flag only (see `XmlMappingProfile.profile_type`).
It is not persisted on a mapping — neither read from the
request body nor written to the response — and is listed here
only because the request/response object shares the
`profile_type` shape.

xml_base.description:
Override of the profile's `xml_base` namespace anchor for this
mapping. Optional.

fields.description:
Per-field bindings — one entry per profile field this mapping
configures. Order is meaningful for export.

limited_extensions.description:
Optional whitelist of file extensions (e.g. `tif`, `jpg`)
this mapping should be offered for. Empty means no restriction.

recipe_configs.description:
Per-recipe configuration applied when this mapping runs.
Keys are recipe names; values are the recipe's configuration
object.

custom.description:
Read-only marker set when the mapping was created by a user
(vs. loaded from a config file). Custom mappings are editable
and deletable through the API; config-loaded ones aren't.

MaskApi.description:
A **mask** descriptor — projects an objecttype to a subset of fields,
with per-field rendering / editability metadata. Masks are the unit
through which the front-end renders forms and detail views.

Returned inside the `masks` array of the `MaskSchemaApi` wrapper by
`GET /api/v1/mask/{version}` and `GET /api/v1/mask/{version}/{mask}`.
