Masks can recursively contain sub-masks via `fields[].mask`; the
`x-recursion: true` marker tells the renderer to stop at one level
of nesting.

name.description:
Stable mask name. The special name `_all_fields` returns the
objecttype's complete field set (available to any authenticated
user).

mask_id.description: Server-issued numeric id; stable across renames.

table_id.description:
Numeric id of the objecttype this mask belongs to (matches
`SchemaTableApi.table_id`).

table_name_hint.description:
Convenience copy of the objecttype name — saves clients an
objecttype lookup when rendering breadcrumbs.

is_preferred.description:
Whether this mask is one of the pool / objecttype's preferred
masks. Omitted on sub-mask entries (nested in another mask).

hide_in_detail.description: Suppress this mask from the front-end detail-view picker.

hide_in_editor.description: Suppress this mask from the front-end editor picker.

hide_in_print_dialog.description: Suppress this mask from the print / export dialog.

standard_numbering.description:
Numbering scheme applied to objects produced through this mask
(overrides the objecttype-level setting when non-empty).

require_comment.description:
Optional rule that forces the user to add a comment when saving
through this mask. Empty / omitted means no requirement.
Recognised values include `always`, `on_change`, ...

comment.description:
Admin-facing free-text comment on the mask itself. Not shown
to end users.

system_fields.description:
Per-mask system-field overrides (visibility, label, default).
Only present on top-level masks; omitted on sub-masks.

fields.description:
The ordered list of fields included in this mask. Each entry
identifies the underlying column plus per-field mask
configuration (required, read-only, default, embedded sub-mask).

custom_settings.description:
Free-form key/value pairs for plugin / theme consumers. Keys
and values are strings; semantics are owned by the consumer.

MaskSchemaApi.description:
The full **mask schema** document — the wrapper returned by
`GET /mask/{version}` and `GET /mask/{version}/{mask}` and the body
that `POST /mask/HEAD` expects and returns: schema-level metadata
(`type`, `version`, `based_on_schema_version`, `max_mask_id`) plus
the array of masks. The single-mask GET returns the same wrapper
with `masks` filtered to the one requested mask.

This is what the working datamodel writes to and reads from; once
the masks are saved into HEAD, they only become visible to other
readers after a separate `POST /api/v1/schema/commit`.

type.description:
Always `"user"` — distinguishes mask-schema documents from
datamodel-schema documents on the same code path.

version.description:
Schema-format marker, always `1` on output. Not consumed on
input to `POST /mask/HEAD`.

based_on_schema_version.description:
On output, the datamodel version this mask set was read from
(the server sets it from the loaded datamodel). On input to
`POST /mask/HEAD` it is decoded but not consumed: the write
always applies to the current HEAD.

max_mask_id.description:
Highest `mask_id` server has issued so far. New masks the
client adds without `mask_id` are assigned ids starting at
`max_mask_id + 1`.

masks.description:
The full set of masks the datamodel HEAD should hold after
the write. Sending fewer masks than HEAD currently has
deletes the missing ones.

FieldApi.description:
One **field** entry inside a mask — the per-mask configuration for
one column (or for an embedded sub-mask). Carries the underlying
column's id and name hint, link / reverse-link metadata, the mask's
per-field edit / output / search behaviour, plus any nested sort
and inheritance settings.

kind.description:
Field kind — currently `column` (a regular field referencing one
column) or `link` (a navigation field that exposes a linked
objecttype's record through this mask). Empty / omitted when
the entry represents an inline placeholder.

type.description:
The underlying column type (`text`, `select`, `link`, `nested`,
`reverse`, ...). Copy of the column's `Type` for client
convenience.

options.description:
Free-form options string (typically per-column type-specific
flags). Empty / omitted for fields without options.

column_id.description:
Numeric id of the column this field points at. Stable across
renames.

column_name_hint.description:
Current column name — provided as a convenience so clients don't
need a separate column lookup when rendering. May be stale if
the column was just renamed.

is_hierarchical.description: Whether the underlying column is a hierarchical link (`_id_parent`-style).

other_table_id.description:
For link / nested / reverse columns — the linked objecttype's
`table_id`.

other_column_id.description:
For reverse-link columns — the linked-back column's id on the
other table.

hierarchical_mask_hint.description:
Whether the linked record should be rendered through its
hierarchical mask (vs. the standard mask).

inline.description:
Inline rendering directive (front-end-defined). Controls whether
the linked record is shown inline next to the field or in a
separate panel.

mask_id.description:
The mask the linked record should be rendered through. Either an
integer mask id, the string `"preferred"`, or `"standard"`.

edit.description: Per-mask edit-mode settings for this field.

append_only.description:
For collection-valued fields — entries can be added but
existing entries cannot be removed through this mask.

remove_only.description:
For collection-valued fields — existing entries can be
removed but no new entries may be added through this mask.

output.description: Where and how this field is rendered in output contexts.

text.description: Include in plain-text rendering (search snippets, exports).

standard.description:
Field's contribution to the object's `_standard` rendering —
the short label fylr produces for an object in pickers and
breadcrumbs.

standard_eas.description: Field's `_standard` ordering for the EAS-rendered string.

standard_geo.description: Field's `_standard` ordering for the geo-rendered string. Geometries copied into `_standard` are simplified (Ramer-Douglas-Peucker) to at most `fylr.geo.standardMaxVertices` vertices per shape; the field value itself keeps full precision.

nested.description:
Nested search — only meaningful on fields whose underlying
column is `nested` / `reverse`.

custom_settings.description:
Plugin / theme-consumed key/value pairs. Keys and values are
strings; semantics are owned by the consumer.

nested_sort.description:
For nested-link fields — sort directives applied to the linked
records when this field is displayed.

field.description: Column api-name to sort by.

nested_standard_first_only.description:
For nested-link fields — when true, only the first nested record
contributes to the object's `_standard` rendering.

inheritance.description:
For fields backed by inheritable columns — controls whether the
field is inherited from parent records.

mask.description:
Embedded sub-mask used to render the linked record (for `link`
/ `nested` / `reverse` fields). Recursion is broken at render
time via MaskApi's `x-recursion: true` marker (when set on the
schema).

column.description:
The underlying column descriptor, surfaced for clients that
don't have the full datamodel in hand.

full_name.description:
Fully-qualified field path — `<table>.<column>` for top-level
fields, deeper for nested sub-mask fields.

ColumnApi.description:
A **column** — one field definition on an objecttype. Carries the
column's identity (`name`, `column_id`, `type`), nullability,
validation rules, and link metadata (for `link` / `nested` /
`reverse` columns).

kind.description:
Column kind — typically empty for user columns, `system` for
fylr-managed columns (`_id`, `_pool`, `_owner`, ...).

name.description:
Stable, machine-readable column name. Used as the JSON key on
objects of this type.

type.description:
Column data type. Recognised types include `text`, `text-l10n`,
`select`, `link`, `nested`, `reverse`, `reverse_hierarchical`,
`date`, `daterange`, `number`, `boolean`, `tags`, `eas`, plus
`custom:*` types contributed by plugins. Enabled plugins
surface their custom types in the rights catalog
(`GET /api/v1/right`); the datamodel rejects unknown types
on save with `code: UnknownColumnType`.

not_null.description: Whether the column requires a non-null value on every write.

column_id.description: Server-issued numeric id; stable across renames.

reverse_edit.description:
For `reverse` columns — whether the column can be edited from
this side (vs. read-only mirroring of the link).

is_hierarchical.description:
For self-referential `link` columns — true if the column forms
a hierarchy (`_id_parent`).

check.description:
Stored CHECK constraint expression (SQL fragment). Empty when
no constraint is set.

check_regexp.description: Regex the value must match (for `text` columns).

check_range_lower.description: Lower bound of an allowed value range.

check_range_upper.description: Upper bound of an allowed value range.

check_range_lower_open.description:
When set, the lower bound is exclusive (`>`). Otherwise inclusive
(`>=`).

check_range_upper_open.description:
When set, the upper bound is exclusive (`<`). Otherwise inclusive
(`<=`).

check_range_not_empty.description:
For `daterange` / numeric range columns — whether an empty range
is rejected.

other_table_id.description:
For link / nested / reverse columns — the linked objecttype's
`table_id`.

other_table_name_hint.description: Convenience copy of the linked objecttype's name.

other_column_id.description:
For `reverse` columns — the linked-back column's id on the
other table.

other_column_name_hint.description: Convenience copy of the linked-back column's name.

bidirectional_reverse.description:
For columns participating in a bidirectional link — true if
this column is the reverse half of the pair (writes to it
mirror through the forward half).

custom_settings.description:
Plugin / theme-consumed key/value pairs on the column. Keys and
values are strings; semantics are owned by the consumer.

name_localized.description:
Localised display name for the column. Shown wherever the
front-end surfaces the column label.

OwnedByApi.description:
Marks the table as a **nested table** of the objecttype identified
by `other_table_id`. Nested rows have no independent identity:
they ship as embedded values inside the top-level objects of the
objecttype `other_table_id` points at, have no `_acl` of their
own and don't carry an owner. On the wire they surface under
`_nested:<table-name>` (or `_reverse_nested:<table-name>`) on the
top-level object.

The name `owned_by` is historical. fylr is an easydb 6 Go
rewrite that targets the easydb 5 API contract; in easydb 5 every
nested table was a real DB table with an owning-table foreign
key. fylr keeps the nested values inside the top-level object's
JSON instead, but the descriptor's name (and its `other_table_id`
slot) stuck.

Constraint: a nested table cannot declare `reverse_link` columns
— the datamodel validator rejects that on save.

other_table_id.description:
`table_id` of the objecttype this table is a nested table of.
Must point at a table that exists in the same datamodel
snapshot.

other_table_name_hint.description:
Convenience copy of that objecttype's name — saves clients a
separate lookup when rendering. May be stale if the
objecttype was just renamed.

ForeignKeyApi.description:
A **foreign-key** declaration on a table. Documents the DB-level
reference between this table's `columns` and the `referenced_table`
primary key, plus the cascade behaviour fylr applies on delete /
update.

name.description:
Database-level constraint name. Server-issued; clients should
treat it as opaque.

on_delete.description:
Cascade action when a referenced row is deleted. Standard SQL
values: `CASCADE`, `RESTRICT`, `SET NULL`, `SET DEFAULT`,
`NO ACTION`.

on_update.description:
Cascade action when a referenced row's primary key is updated.
Same values as `on_delete`.

columns.description:
The column(s) on **this** table that participate in the foreign
key. Multi-column keys reference a composite key on the other
side, in the same order.

column_id.description: Numeric id of the column on this table.

column_name_hint.description: Convenience copy of the column's current name.

referenced_table.description: The table this key points at and the columns on its side.

name_hint.description: Convenience copy of the referenced table's name.

columns.description:
The referenced table's columns this FK targets — paired
positionally with this table's `columns` array.

auto_column_primary_key.description:
True when the referenced column is the referenced
table's auto-managed primary key (`_id`).

UniqueKeyApi.description:
A **unique-key** constraint on a table. Violations surface as
`DatabaseUniqueKeyViolation` on `POST /api/v1/db/{objecttype}`.

group.description:
Constraint-group label (`a` / `b` / `c`). Lets multiple unique
constraints be authored side-by-side on the same table — same
group label means they are alternatives, not all required.

columns.description:
The column(s) that together must be unique. A multi-column key
requires the combination to be unique, not each column
individually.

auto_column_parent.description:
True when the column is the auto-managed `_id_parent`
(hierarchical link).

auto_column_uplink.description:
True when the column is the auto-managed uplink column
for a hierarchical parent-child relationship.

column_name_hint.description: Convenience copy of the column's current name.

BidirectionalApi.description:
A **bidirectional link** declaration — a pair of columns on two
different objecttypes that mirror each other. Writing the link on
one side automatically materialises the reverse link on the other;
reading either side returns the same connection.

columns.description:
The two columns participating in the bidirectional link — the
forward column on this table and the reverse column on the
other (`bidirectional_reverse: true` on the other side's
ColumnApi).

auto_column_uplink.description: True when the column is the auto-managed uplink column.

column_name_hint.description: Convenience copy of the column's current name.

LookupByReference.description:
Reference-based lookup descriptor — used on `lookup:_id` (or
`lookup:_id_parent`) slots throughout the API. Lets clients
address an existing user / group / pool by its stable
`reference` string instead of by numeric `_id`. The lookup must
resolve to exactly one record; zero or multiple matches cause
the enclosing write to fail with `LookupNotFound` / `LookupNotUnique`.

reference.description:
The target record's `reference` string. Must match a stored
reference on the addressed basetype exactly.

GeneratedRightsApi.description:
Read-only **generated rights** — the effective per-right
configuration the requesting session holds on the resource that
carries the field. Returned by fylr inside many top-level
responses (objects, pools, groups, users, ...) so the front-end
doesn't have to recompute the ACL.

Keyed by right name (one of the values from the rights catalog
at `GET /api/v1/right`). Each value mirrors the same per-right
configuration shape used inside `RightApi.rights` —
`RightItemApi`.

SystemRightsApi.description:
**System rights** granted to a principal (a group, a user, …) —
the instance-wide permissions the principal holds. The map is
keyed by right name (matches the entries in the `system` context
of `GET /api/v1/right`); each value is a map of right-parameter
name to that parameter's value.

Parameter value types are right-specific — the inner map carries
whatever shape the right's parameter declares (`bool`, `string`,
`string-list`, ...). For a fully-typed view of which parameters
each right accepts, consult the rights catalog.

additionalProperties.description:
Per-right parameter assignments. Keys are parameter names; value
type matches the parameter's declared type in the rights catalog.

AutomaticAuthApi.description:
**Automatic-auth** descriptor — when set on a group, marks the
group as one whose members can be granted a short-lived session
through fylr's automatic-auth flow (action-code / email-link).
Carries the auth method that issued the binding and a timestamp.

type.description:
Auth method that granted the automatic-auth binding. One of
`easydb`, `anonymous`, `collection`, `email`, `action_code`,
`auto` (and any auth method a plugin contributes).

timestamp.description: When the binding was issued. Set server-side.

AuthMethodGroupMapsApi.description:
**Auth-method group mappings** — rules that map external auth
providers (SSO, LDAP) and fylr's built-in system groups onto this
group. Used so signed-in users from those identity sources are
automatically assigned to the group.

Each entry is an array of rules. A rule succeeds when its `method`
/ `value` test against the incoming auth subject holds. The
`system` array only supports `method: "eq"` (other methods are
rejected on save).

system.description:
Rules matched against fylr's built-in system groups. Only
`method: "eq"` is accepted; `value` must be the reference of
an existing system group.

AuthGroupRuleApi.description:
One rule inside an `_auth_method_group_maps.<sso|ldap|system>`
array. The rule's `method` says how to compare the incoming
subject against `value`; if it matches, the user is assigned to
the group.

method.description:
Comparison method. `eq` is supported everywhere; LDAP and SSO
contexts may also accept other comparison methods (regex /
glob, depending on configuration). The `system` array only
accepts `eq`.

value.description:
The string the rule matches against. For `system` rules, the
reference of an existing system group; otherwise the value the
external provider supplies (LDAP group DN, SSO claim value, ...).

GroupPseudoApi.description:
**Pseudonymization** template for a group. When a user account
belonging to this group is deleted, fylr replaces the user's
identifying fields with the strings configured here so that
event log entries and ACL grants remain attributable to "some
user from this group" without leaking the original identity.

user.description:
Pseudonymised replacements for the user's identifying fields.
Empty / unset uses the per-instance defaults.

login.description: Pseudonymised login name.

_emails.description:
Pseudonymised email — note the leading underscore in the
JSON key.

ScheduleApi.description:
A **schedule** — selector of when a recurring action should run.
Used for the user's mail-notification schedule (`mail_schedule`)
and elsewhere fylr lets users pick a recurrence in calendar terms
rather than as a cron expression.

All fields are arrays; emptier means "match any value" for that
field. The schedule fires whenever the wall clock in `timezone`
satisfies every non-empty constraint.

days_of_month.description: Days of the month the schedule may fire on.

weekdays.description:
Weekdays the schedule may fire on. Lowercase three-letter
abbreviations.

timezone.description:
IANA timezone identifier (`Europe/Berlin`, `UTC`, ...). When
omitted, the instance default timezone is used.

collection_id.description:
`_id` of the collection the pin applies to. Must be a
pin-protected collection (`pin_required: true`).

pin_code.description:
The pin code as the user entered it. Stored hashed
server-side; the cleartext value is only present here on the
write path. Reads return the same shape with the cleartext
omitted when the requester is not the user themselves.

LocaleApi.description:
Locale metadata — number formatting and calendar text the front
end uses to render localised values. Derived from CLDR for the
user's `frontend_language` (and per `database_locales` entry, for
each language the user is allowed to edit data in).

name.description:
Display name of the locale in its own language (`Deutsch`,
`English (United States)`, ...).

bc.description: Era marker before common era (e.g. `v. Chr.`).

year.description: Format pattern for a bare year.

year_month.description: Format pattern for a year-month value (`MMMM y`).

SessionAuthenticationMethodApi.description:
One authentication method the instance exposes (or the method the
current session was established with).

method.description:
The auth method identifier. Recognised values include `easydb`,
`email`, `collection`, `action_code`, `auto`, `anonymous`, plus
any method a plugin contributes.

timestamp.description:
When the session was authenticated through this method (only
set on `authenticated`, not in the `authentication_methods`
catalog).

client_id.description:
Identifier of the client that drove the authentication (e.g.
the browser UUID). Empty for server-driven sessions.

WatermarkApi.description:
**Watermark** configuration applied to image variants produced by
fylr's asset pipeline for objects in a given scope (a pool or an
objecttype). The watermark image is fetched through `image` (a
full file descriptor); the placement / appearance parameters
follow the ImageMagick conventions.

image.description:
File descriptor of the watermark image. Setting `image: null`
(or omitting it) clears the watermark for this scope.

gravity.description:
Anchor point on the produced image where the watermark is
placed, given as a short compass code. On save the API accepts
exactly these values; any other value is rejected with
`watermark.gravity "<value>" not supported`. fylr maps them
internally to the corresponding ImageMagick gravity:

  * `nw` → NorthWest, `n` → North, `ne` → NorthEast
  * `w` → West, `c` → Center, `e` → East
  * `sw` → SouthWest, `s` → South, `se` → SouthEast
  * `""` (empty) → no gravity (placement defaults / tiling)

dissolve.description:
Opacity of the watermark, in percent (`0` = invisible,
`100` = fully opaque). The 0–100 range is enforced when the
rendition is produced, not validated on save.

size.description:
Target size of the watermark on the produced image. When the
rendition is produced fylr accepts a positive pixel size
(`200`), a percentage of the canvas's largest side (`30%`), a
fixed `<width>x<height>` (`200x100`), or `<width>%x<height>%`.
The value is not validated on save.

tile.description:
When true, the watermark image tiles across the whole canvas
instead of being placed once at `gravity`.

BackupStatsApi.description:
Summary stats captured for one backup run — filesize and the
content-type / filename the produced backup artifact carries.

filesize.description: Size of the produced backup file, in bytes.

mimetype.description:
MIME type of the produced backup file. Depends on the chosen
`opts.format` — typically `application/vnd.sqlite3; charset=binary`
for SQLite snapshots, `application/x-tar` for pg_dump archives.

filename.description:
File name fylr assigns to the artifact —
`fylr-backup-<format>-<instance>-<YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM>.<ext>`.

BackupOptsApi.description:
Options the backup was started with. Echoed back on read so
clients can tell which format the produced artifact is in.

format.description:
Backup format. The recognised values are configured per
instance and surface in `GET /api/v1/system/backup/list`;
typical values include `sqlite3_db` (single-file SQLite
snapshot) and `pg_dump` (PostgreSQL dump archive).

include_events.description:
When true, the event log is included in the backup. When
false, only the datamodel and user data are dumped — useful
for migrating to a new instance without carrying the event
history along.

CaptionApi.description:
Per-field **caption** configuration — used by the asset-rendering
pipeline to burn a text caption into image variants produced for
an objecttype's records.

text.description:
Caption text template. Supports the same field-reference syntax
as other fylr text templates (`<no value>` etc.).

size.description:
Caption font size. Either a positive pixel integer (`24`) or a
percentage of the canvas height (`1`–`50` followed by `%`).

padding.description:
Padding around the caption, in pixels. `0` or higher; no
decimals, no leading zeros.

multiline.description: Allow the caption to wrap across multiple lines.

overlay.description:
When true, the caption is drawn on top of the image; when
false, it's appended below.

CaptionByFieldApi.description:
Per-field caption configuration map — keyed by the field name on
the objecttype whose record's caption should be rendered for the
asset. Each entry is a CaptionApi.

JanitorPolicyApi.description:
**Janitor policy** — controls automatic cleanup of historic file
versions and soft-deleted objects under this scope (a pool or an
objecttype). Both blocks are independently optional; `null` means
the corresponding cleanup is disabled.

file_link_delete.description:
When set, the janitor removes historic file versions linked
from this scope's objects according to either time-based or
count-based retention. Set both fields to `0` to keep nothing
and clean up immediately.

keep_days_history_versions.description:
Keep historic versions newer than this many days. `0`
means "do not keep by time at all".

keep_count_history_versions.description:
Keep at most this many historic versions per file. `0`
means "do not keep any historic versions".

object_purge.description:
When set, the janitor permanently purges soft-deleted objects
this scope owns once they have been deleted for at least
`min_deleted_days`.

min_deleted_days.description:
Number of days an object must have been soft-deleted
before the janitor purges it. `0` means "purge
immediately on soft-delete".

XmlMappingFieldExport.description:
One **XML-export rule** for a profile field. Targets an XML
location (XPath / element / attribute) and the value-formatting
options the writer applies.

`list` and `l10n` control how multiple values and languages are
written. With neither flag a single value is written (the preferred
language of a multilingual source, joined with `; ` for a repeatable
field). Set together they produce a **localized list** — an
`rdf:Bag` whose entries are `rdf:Alt` language alternatives — for
custom tags and for tags whose schema defines that shape (e.g.
`XMP-plus:Custom1`). A built-in tag can only take the shapes its
schema allows; the mapping editor offers the valid options per tag
(see the `list`/`l10n` capability flags on `GET /xmlmapping/tags`).

path.description:
XPath / element path the field's value is written to. May
include namespace prefixes declared on the parent profile.

list.description:
When true, the field's values are written as an unordered
list — an XMP `rdf:Bag` with one `rdf:li` element per value.
For a multilingual (l10n) source field the bag collects the
values of **all** languages, not only the preferred one, and a
nested or repeatable field contributes every entry. Use this
when the target tag holds plain values without language
information, for example `dc:subject`.

l10n.description:
When true, the field's value is written localized per language
as an XMP language alternative — an `rdf:Alt` carrying one
`rdf:li` with an `xml:lang` attribute per language plus an
`x-default`. Internally the writer emits one element per
language using the Exiftool-style `<path>-<lang>` suffix
convention, which Exiftool combines into the lang-alt. Use this
for a multilingual target tag such as `dc:title`.

attributes.description:
Static XML attributes to add to the output element. Keys are
attribute names; values are the literal attribute strings.

elements_js.description:
Optional JavaScript snippet used to post-process the output
element. Set by the loader; not serialised on profile YAML.

XmlMappingFieldImport.description:
One **XML-import rule** for a profile field. Tells the reader how
to find the field's value in an incoming XML document.

search.description:
XPath / element search expression the reader runs against the
incoming document to extract this field's value.

l10n_prefix.description:
Compatibility shim for easydb 5 — the language prefix used by
the legacy importer. Not consumed by fylr; kept on the wire
for round-trips with easydb-5 profile files.

XmlMappingTag.description:
One entry of the metadata **tag catalog** (`GET /xmlmapping/tags`):
a tag exiftool understands, with the capability flags the mapping
editor uses to offer only the value-shapes the tag supports.

path.description:
Tag path as used in a mapping's `xml_export`/`xml_import` rules,
in the form `<group>:<tag>` (e.g. `XMP-dc:Subject`).

group.description:
Tag group (exiftool family-1 group, e.g. `XMP-dc`, `IPTC`,
`ExifIFD`). Also the key into the response's `xmlns` map.

description.description:
Localized display name of the tag, keyed by frontend language
(e.g. `de-DE`). May be empty for tags exiftool ships without
translations.

writable.description:
When true, exiftool can write the tag — only writable tags are
offered for export mappings.

type.description:
The exiftool value type, e.g. `string`, `integer`, `lang-alt`,
or `?` for tags without a declared type.

l10n.description:
When true, the tag is a localized **lang-alt** (`rdf:Alt` with
one value per language). Derived from `type` being `lang-alt`.
Omitted when false.

list.description:
When true, the tag is a repeatable list (an XMP `rdf:Bag` or
`rdf:Seq`). Together with `l10n` this classifies the tag's
container: scalar (neither), list, lang-alt, or list-of-lang-alt
(both — e.g. `XMP-plus:Custom1`, a localized list). Omitted when
false.

list_type.description: The concrete list container. Only present when `list` is true.

XmlMappingFieldEasydb.description:
One **fylr-side target** for an XML-mapping field. Identifies the
column the value should land in and the display text the editor
shows next to it.

text.description: Display text shown for this target in the editor.

type.description:
Column type hint. Currently unused by the server but mapped
through for client convenience.

field.description:
The fylr column path the value flows into. Same syntax as
datamodel column references — `<objecttype>.<column>` for
regular fields, dotted for nested links.

RecipeConfigs.description:
Per-recipe configuration. This object is a map whose keys are **recipe
names** and whose values configure that recipe for the current operation
(e.g. producing a version or running a metadata mapping).

Recipe name format:
  * Built-in / config cookbooks: `COOKBOOK-NAME:RECIPE-NAME`
    (for example `imageconverter:browserthumbs`).
  * Plugin-provided recipes: `PLUGIN-NAME:COOKBOOK-NAME:RECIPE-NAME`.

The `params` of each recipe are **recipe-specific**: the set of valid
parameter keys, their value types, defaults, ranges and option lists are
defined by each recipe individually, not by this API schema. That is why
`params` is documented as an open object (`additionalProperties: true`)
rather than a fixed schema — a closed schema cannot describe every recipe,
and the available recipes additionally depend on which plugins are
installed.

To discover the parameters a recipe accepts on a running instance, use the
recipe listing endpoint:

    GET /inspect/apidocs/recipes/

It lists every cookbook and recipe currently loaded (including
plugin-provided ones) together with each parameter's key, description and
type. Each value you send is validated against that recipe's parameter
definition: unknown keys are rejected; a `string` param may be regex-
constrained; an `int` param is bounded by its `min`/`max`; a `select`
param must be one of the recipe's options; a `bool` param accepts a
boolean or a string (`true`/`1`/`yes` mean true). Omitting a key (or
sending `null`) applies the recipe's
default; a parameter marked mandatory by the recipe must be supplied if it
has no default.

additionalProperties.description:
Configuration for a single recipe, keyed in the parent map by the recipe
name (see the parent description for the name format).

params.description:
Recipe-specific parameters as a map of parameter name to value.

The accepted keys and value types are defined by the recipe itself
and are listed, per recipe, at `GET /inspect/apidocs/recipes/`.
Validation rules applied per parameter, depending on the recipe's
declared parameter type:
  * string  — value must be a string; may be constrained by a regex.
  * int     — value must be an integer (a numeric string is also
              accepted); bounded by the parameter's `min`/`max`.
  * select  — value must be a string equal to one of the parameter's
              allowed options.
  * bool    — value must be a boolean (a string is also accepted:
              `true`/`1`/`yes` mean true, anything else false).
An absent key, or a `null` value, applies the recipe's default. A
parameter the recipe marks as mandatory must be provided unless it has
a default. Keys not defined by the recipe are rejected.

FileMetadata.description:
Free-form **file metadata** document — a map of metadata groups
to per-group key/value bags.

The well-known group `_technical_metadata` carries the file's
intrinsic properties (`width`, `height`, `dpi`, ...); other groups
carry whatever the configured metadata recipes produce (EXIF, IPTC,
XMP, custom extractors). Group names beginning with `_` are
reserved for fylr-managed groups; user-defined groups should use
unprefixed names.

Each group's value is a flat key/value bag whose values can be
strings, numbers, booleans, nested objects, or arrays — the type
set per key is fixed by the producing recipe, not by fylr.

additionalProperties.description: A metadata group's key/value bag. Value types are recipe-defined.

IiifImageInfo.description:
IIIF Image API 2.0 **image information** document — the standard
reply to a `/info.json` request, defined by the [IIIF Image API
2.0 specification](https://iiif.io/api/image/2.0/#image-information).

fylr serves this document so any IIIF-aware client (Mirador,
Universal Viewer, OpenSeadragon, ...) can discover the image's
dimensions, supported tile sizes and feature profile.

@context.description: JSON-LD context — always the IIIF Image API 2 URL.

@id.description:
The image's IIIF service URL — `/info.json` without the
`/info.json` suffix.

width.description: The image's native width, in pixels.

height.description: The image's native height, in pixels.

profile.description:
Two-element array — the first item is a compliance-level URI
(`http://iiif.io/api/image/2/level1.json`, `level2.json`, ...);
the second (optional) is a free-form object describing the
additional features the service supports beyond the level.

tiles.description:
The tile geometries the service is willing to serve directly.
Clients use these to assemble deep-zoom mosaics without
downloading the full image.

width.description: Tile width, in pixels (square unless `height` is given).

height.description: Tile height, in pixels. Defaults to `width`.

scaleFactors.description:
The downscaling factors the service can produce for these
tiles (powers of two — `[1, 2, 4, 8, 16]`).

sizes.description:
Pre-rendered fixed sizes the service can serve at full quality.
Clients should request from this list when they only need a
whole-image thumbnail.

service.description:
Optional related services (auth, search, ...) advertised by
the image. Shape is service-dependent.

ObjectsError.description:
Error returned by `GET`/`HEAD /api/v1/objects/*` when the deep-link
request cannot be served.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `DeepLinkAccessDisabled` — deep-link access is turned off in
    the base config (`system.deep_link_access.enabled = false`).
    Returned before any path parsing.
  * `DeepLinkAccessByIdDisabled` — an `id/<system-object-id>`
    selector was used while `system.deep_link_access.allow_access_by_id`
    is off.
  * `DeepLinkAccessByColumnDisabled` — a `column/...` selector was
    used while `system.deep_link_access.allow_access_by_column` is
    off.
  * `ApiObjectPath` — the selector path is malformed or the
    addressed object/version/column cannot be resolved (e.g. no
    object found, a column value matching multiple objects, an
    unknown format or path element). The message embeds the
    offending path.
  * `FileNotFound` — a `file/...` selector addressed a file that
    does not exist (`statuscode: 404`).
  * `FileVersionNotAvailable` — the requested
    `file_version/name/<name>` is not among the file's available
    versions.
  * `FileGroupNotAvailable` — the requested
    `file_version/group/<group>` has no matching version for the
    file.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`. `DeepLinkAccessDisabled`
originates in `api`; the remaining codes originate in `export`.

example.error:
The deep link access needs to be enabled in the base config.

Package: api
Code: DeepLinkAccessDisabled

OaiError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/oai` when the OAI-PMH endpoint is
disabled at the instance level (HTTP 400). Protocol-level errors
(bad `verb`, unknown identifier, ...) are encoded inside the XML
body per the OAI-PMH 2.0 specification and carry the matching
HTTP status (400 / 404 / 500) — they do **not** use this schema.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `OAIpmhNotEnabled` — the instance has the OAI-PMH
    endpoint turned off in its base config
    (`fylr.oai_pmh.enabled = false`).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

example.error:
The OAI/PMH endpoint is not enabled.

Package: api
Code: OAIpmhNotEnabled

WebdavError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/WebDAV/*` when the WebDAV endpoint is
disabled at the instance level. Per-resource failures inside the
WebDAV protocol (404, 423 lock-conflict, 207 multi-status with
per-property errors) follow RFC 4918 and do **not** use this
schema.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `WebdavNotEnabled` — the instance has the WebDAV
    endpoint turned off in its base config
    (`webdav.read_write_access = false`).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

example.error:
WebDAV access is not enabled

Package: api
Code: WebdavNotEnabled

RightPresetError.description:
Error returned by `POST /api/v1/right/{context}/presets` when a
preset cannot be saved. The codes here come from the right-check
pipeline that validates each preset against the rights catalog
for the addressed context before storage.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code raised by the rightcheck
pipeline:

  * `UnknownRight` — the preset references a right name that
    does not exist in the addressed context. `parameters.right`
    carries the offending name, `parameters.context` the
    context.
  * `UnknownRightParam` — the preset sets a parameter that
    the addressed right does not declare. `parameters.param`
    carries the offending name, `parameters.right` /
    `parameters.right_displayname` identify the right.
  * `UnknownRightValue` — a parameter's value is not in the
    right's `choices` (or fails the right's value validation).
    `parameters.value` carries what was sent.
  * `ChoiceNotUnique` — the preset enables more than one
    option of a `choice` right; only one may be set.
    `parameters.choice` identifies the choice group.

Generic 400 codes from the server pipeline (request parsing,
`ReadOnlyMode`, ...) may also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`. Right-validation codes
carry `rightcheck`; generic server errors (e.g. an unknown context)
carry `ferrors`.

example.error:
The right "supervise" is not known in context "object"

Package: rightcheck
Code: UnknownRight

WhoApi.description:
**Who** — references a principal an ACL grant addresses (or names
the owner of an object). Exactly one of `_owner`, `_pool_contact`,
`user`, `group` resolves; the rest are absent.

_basetype.description:
The principal kind. Required for `user` / `group` references;
absent for `_owner` / `_pool_contact` synthetic principals.

_owner.description:
When `true`, the grant addresses *whoever currently owns* the
resource (a synthetic principal that resolves per-object).

_pool_contact.description:
When `true`, the grant addresses the pool's configured contact
user. Only valid for grants on objects living in a pool.

user.description:
A specific user reference. Must carry either `_id` or
`lookup:_id`. Other fields (`login`, `type`,
`frontend_language`, `_generated_displayname`, ...) mirror the
embedded user record; `additionalProperties: true` reflects
that on read fylr returns a UserDataApi snapshot which carries
many optional fields beyond `_id`.

lookup:_id.description: Reference-based lookup for the user. Resolved server-side.

_generated_displayname.description:
Read-only compiled displayname for the user (login,
first/last name fallback chain).

group.description:
A specific group reference. Must carry either `_id` or
`lookup:_id`.

lookup:_id.description: Reference-based lookup for the group. Resolved server-side.

_generated_displayname.description: Read-only compiled displayname for the group.

RightApi.description:
An **ACL grant** — one entry of an `_acl` array. Combines a
principal (`who`), a set of rights with per-right configuration
(`rights`), an optional time window (`when`) and an optional tag
filter (`tagfilter`).

_id.description:
Server-issued numeric id of the grant. Stable across updates;
absent when the grant is being created.

sticky.description:
Sticky grants cannot be removed by lower-privileged updates —
a write that omits a sticky grant from the new ACL keeps it
in place. Only `system.root` (or another grant marked sticky
with `_grantable`) can clear a sticky grant.

active.description:
Whether the grant is currently effective. Defaults to `true`
when omitted. Setting `false` suspends the grant without
deleting it. The capability map on the rights catalog says
which contexts respect `active`.

_right_preset.description:
Id of the preset this grant was instantiated from (zero /
omitted for hand-crafted grants).

when.description:
Optional time window outside of which the grant is treated
as inactive. Both bounds are ISO 8601 timestamps; either or
both may be omitted.

from.description: Grant takes effect at or after this timestamp.

rights.description:
Map of right name → per-right configuration. Keys are the
right names from the rights catalog
(`/api/v1/right`); each value is a `RightItemApi`.

send_email_notification.description:
Write-only — when set on creation, fylr sends a notification
email to the affected participants. Not part of the read
shape.

text.description: Plain-text body of the notification email.

RightItemApi.description:
One **right item** inside an ACL grant — the per-right
configuration that scopes the right (which masks, objecttypes,
pools, file classes / extensions / versions it applies to) and
whether the grant is grantable downward.

A `RightApi.rights` value is a map of right name → `RightItemApi`.
Unset fields mean "no constraint" (apply to all); set to an empty
array means "no value matches" (effectively disabled). The
per-field constraint semantics depend on the right — see the
rights catalog (`GET /api/v1/right`).

_grantable.description: If true, the grantee may grant this right onward to others.

allow_update_and_remove.description:
For `update` / `remove` style rights, allow the grantee to
both modify and remove the resource (or only modify when
false).

classes.description:
Restrict to specific EAS classes (`image`, `document`, ...).
`null` / absent means any class.

extensions.description:
Restrict to specific file extensions. `null` / absent means
any extension.

versions.description:
Restrict to specific version names (`original`, `preview`,
`thumbnail`, ...). `null` / absent means any version.

limit.description:
Upload size cap, in bytes (parsed from human-readable strings
like `"10MB"` on input). `0` means no cap.

mask_ids.description:
Per-objecttype mask restriction. Map of objecttype api id to
an array of mask api ids (or the string `"standard"` for the
objecttype's standard mask).

column_ids.description:
Restrict to specific column api ids. Empty array matches all
columns; absent means no constraint.

objecttype_ids.description: Restrict to specific objecttype api ids.

pool_ids.description:
Restrict to specific pool ids. Empty array matches all pools;
absent means no constraint.

TagfilterApi.description:
**Tag filter** — a three-way set predicate over tag ids. Used by
ACL grants, right presets and transitions to limit applicability
to objects carrying (or not carrying) specific tags.

A match succeeds when **all** non-empty conditions hold: every id
in `all` is present on the object, *at least one* id in `any` is
present, and *no* id in `not` is present.

all.description: Every listed tag must be present on the object.

any.description:
At least one listed tag must be present. Empty / absent means
"no any-constraint".

not.description: None of the listed tags may be present.

changed.description:
Only used by transitions — the tags whose addition / removal
should *trigger* the transition. Omitted everywhere else.

SystemFieldApi.description:
Per-mask configuration for the **system fields** — the fields fylr
attaches to every object regardless of the user datamodel
(`_id_parent`, `_id`, `_tags`, `_acl`, `_owner`, `_pool`,
`_collections`, `_publish`). Each present key carries the visibility
/ inline / edit settings the mask applies to that system field.
Only present on top-level masks; sub-masks inherit.

object_id.description: Settings for the `_id` (system object id) field.

tags.description: Settings for the `_tags` field.

pool.description: Settings for the `_pool` field.

SystemFieldDefApi.description:
Per-system-field rendering / editing options on a mask. Each key
controls one aspect of how the corresponding system field is
presented in the front-end. All sub-fields are optional; omitted
entries mean "use the default behaviour".

inline.description:
Inline rendering directive — when set, the front-end embeds
the system field's value alongside other fields rather than
in the standard sidebar. Recognised values are
front-end-defined.

mask_id.description:
Mask the system field should render through (when the field
links to objects of another type, like `_owner`). Either an
integer mask id or the special string `"standard"`.

output.description: Output (detail / table / text) display mode for the field.

SystemFieldDefModeApi.description:
Per-mode display setting for a system field (output or edit). Used
inside `SystemFieldDefApi.output` / `.edit`.

mode.description:
The mode the system field should use in this context.
Recognised values include `show`, `edit`, `off`,
`hide`, `inline`. The set is front-end-defined; unknown
values are silently ignored by the editor.

SuggestRequest.description:
Auto-suggest request — accepted as the JSON request body of
`POST /api/v1/suggest`, and as the value of the `BODY` query
parameter on `GET /api/v1/suggest`. Drives token and / or field
completion through the search index.

Each result kind is opted into independently: token suggestions
(`tokens`, on by default unless `tokens` is `false`), per-field
value matches (`fields_suggest: true`, which also needs a non-empty
`fields`), and linked-object descriptors (a non-null
`linked_objecttypes`).

query.description:
The user's current input. Optional; defaults to the empty
string. Tokenised (whitespace-split) unless
`tokens_dont_split_query` is set. The last token is treated as
the partial term to complete.

tokens_dont_split_query.description:
If true, the query is sent verbatim — the engine does not split
it on whitespace. Useful when the input is a multi-word phrase
that should match as a single token.

tokens.description:
If true, null, or omitted, return token completions in
`suggestions.tokens`. Set to false to suppress them. Independent
of `fields_suggest`.

fields.description:
Field paths whose values should be considered as completion
candidates. Each entry is a fully-qualified field name
(e.g. `picture.title`, `_tags.tag.name`). Must be non-empty to
get any per-field suggestions: combining `fields_suggest: true`
with an empty `fields` yields no `suggestions.fields` (it is a
silent no-op, not an error).

fields_suggest.description:
If true, also return per-field completions in
`suggestions.fields` — one entry per matching value, with the
list of system-object ids that hold it.

objecttypes.description:
Restrict suggestions to objects of these objecttypes. Empty /
omitted means no restriction.

pool_ids.description:
Restrict suggestions to objects in exactly these pools — there
is no automatic sub-pool inclusion. Empty / omitted means no
restriction.

languages.description:
Restrict token / field matching to these language-tagged
sub-fields (e.g. `de-DE`, `en-US`). Empty means consider all
languages enabled on the instance.

linked_objecttypes.description:
If set, the engine also returns `linked_objects` — full
`ObjectApi` entries of the named objecttypes that match the
query. By default every match is returned, referenced or not;
set `linked_objects_referenced_by_objecttypes` to keep only the
ones actually in use. Useful for autocomplete pickers that need
to show the target object inline.

linked_objects_referenced_by_objecttypes.description:
Opt-in filter for `linked_objects`. When set, a linked-object
suggestion is kept only if at least one record of these
objecttypes links to it (directly, nested or reverse — the
`_linked.<ot>._id` closure the "in" filter queries when a
suggestion is clicked), so selecting the suggestion as a search
filter over these objecttypes always yields results. Absent /
empty returns every match. Pass the objecttypes the caller will
actually filter on click — kept separate from `objecttypes` on
purpose, since `objecttypes` may be widened with the linked
objecttypes so token matches inside linked objects are found.

analyze_wildcards.description:
If true, the query string is interpreted as a wildcard pattern
(`*` and `?`) rather than a prefix. Applies to token
suggestions only; the field and linked-object searches ignore
it.

tokens_mode.description:
Token-matching mode. One of:

  * `` (empty) / `ngram` — substring match (default).
  * `edgengram` — prefix match.
  * `exact` — exact-term match.

Any other value is rejected with a 400.

timeout.description:
Per-request timeout in milliseconds passed through to the
search index. Zero / omitted uses the indexer's default.

limit.description:
Maximum number of suggestions to return (per kind). Defaults
to 10 when unset or zero.

SuggestResponse.description:
Auto-suggest response — returned by `GET /api/v1/suggest` and
`POST /api/v1/suggest`. Carries diagnostic timing info plus a
`suggestions` envelope with the three optional result sets the
request opted into.

es_time.description:
Time the search index spent on the request, in milliseconds.
Excludes fylr-side serialisation overhead.

timed_out.description:
True if the request reached the per-request `timeout`. Partial
results may still be present in `suggestions`.

suggestions.description:
Result envelope. Each sub-field is present only when its
matching request flag opted in: `tokens` is present unless
`tokens` was `false`; `fields` is present when `fields_suggest`
is true; `linked_objects` is present whenever
`linked_objecttypes` is non-null (an empty `linked_objecttypes`
array yields an empty `linked_objects` list).

suggest.description: The rendered suggestion with the matched prefix wrapped in `...` tags — ready to drop into the front-end picker.

tokens.description:
The individual tokens this suggestion is composed of
(front-end uses this for keyboard navigation).

count.description: Number of indexed documents the term occurs in.

field.description:
The complete matched field value, in plain text
(HTML-unescaped). This is the value itself, not the
field path the request asked for.

suggest.description: The same value, HTML-escaped, with the matched part wrapped in `...` tags — ready to drop into the front-end picker.

count.description:
Number of indexed documents that hold the value at
that field.

_system_object_id.description:
System-object ids of the matching documents — capped
by the request's `limit`. Lets the front end jump
straight to the underlying object.

linked_objects.description:
Full object descriptors of the linked objects whose
objecttypes are in `linked_objecttypes`. Each entry is an
`ObjectApi` rendered in the search "standard" format — a
`_standard` map keyed by mask id, mirroring the search
output. Only objects that a record of the searched
`objecttypes` actually links to are returned, so selecting
one as a search filter yields results. Useful for picker
UIs that need to render a label alongside the suggestion.
Present whenever `linked_objecttypes` is non-null; an empty
`linked_objecttypes` array yields an empty list.

GroupError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/group*` endpoints when the request cannot
be processed.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `DeleteSystemGroup` — attempt to delete a group whose `type` is
    `system`. System groups cannot be deleted.
  * `GroupTypeNotAllowed` — the requested group `type` is not allowed
    on create. In practice this is raised for `type: system`;
    `parameters.type` carries the offending value.
  * `GroupTypeChangeNotAllowed` — the group `type` cannot be
    changed after the group exists.

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline may also surface here, for
example `ReadOnlyMode` (the instance is in read-only mode) and
`VersionMismatch` (on update, the supplied `group._version` does not
equal the stored version + 1).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

parameters.description: Machine-readable, code-specific details.

realm.description: The realm the error originates from. Always `api`.

statuscode.description: Always `400`.

example.error:
The group type "system" is not allowed

Package: acl
Code: GroupTypeNotAllowed

PoolError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/pool*` endpoints when the request cannot
be processed.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. The pool endpoints raise:

  * `IllegalPathForPool` — the requested parent placement would
    make a pool its own parent/descendant. Raised on POST/PUT.
    `parameters.id` is the offending pool id.
  * `UnableToSetIsSystemPool` — `is_system_pool` cannot be set or
    changed through the API; system pools are seeded at install
    time only. Raised on POST/PUT.
  * `PoolNotEmpty` — DELETE was attempted on a pool that still
    contains objects (directly or via sub-pools).
    `parameters.stats` lists the non-empty objecttypes.
  * `PoolNeedsParent` — a non-root pool was saved without a
    parent; pools cannot be saved at the top-level root.

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline may also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`. Different pool errors
carry different origins (e.g. `pool`, `api`, `rightcheck`).

example.error:
The pool #42 contains itself as parent and cannot be saved

Package: pool
Code: IllegalPathForPool

ObjecttypeError.description:
Error returned by `POST /api/v1/objecttype` when the submitted
objecttype cannot be saved.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code raised by the objecttype
package. Both codes come from resolving an
`_export_asset_filenames` template:

  * `UnknownField` — the template references a field name that
    does not exist on the objecttype. `parameters.field` carries
    the offending api field name. Message: `Unknown field
    <field>`.
  * `UnsupportedFieldType` — the template references a field that
    is neither a files column nor a link to an objecttype that has
    a files column. `parameters.field` and `parameters.type` carry
    the field name and its column type. Message: `Unsupported type
    <type> for field <field>`.

These are common values, not an exhaustive list: other 400 codes
from the save pipeline and the datamodel validator may also
surface here (see the operation's 400 description).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

example.error:
Unknown field subtitel

Package: objecttype
Code: UnknownField

DatamodelError.description:
Error returned by the datamodel endpoints
(`POST /api/v1/mask/HEAD`, `POST /api/v1/schema/user/HEAD`,
`POST /api/v1/schema/commit`, and `GET /api/v1/schema/user/{version}`)
when the submitted datamodel cannot be saved or committed, or the
requested version does not exist.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code raised by the datamodel
validator. The list grows with each fylr release; treat unknown
values as opaque.

  * `InvalidIdentifier` — an objecttype / mask / column name
    does not match the identifier regex
    (`^[a-z][a-z0-9\-_]{0,128}[a-z0-9]$`).
  * `SchemaDuplicateObjecttypeName` — two objecttypes share a
    name.
  * `SchemaDuplicateMaskName` — two masks on the same
    objecttype share a name.
  * `ObjecttypeMustHaveExtactlyOnePreferredMask` — exactly one
    mask per objecttype must have `is_preferred: true`. (The
    wire spelling has the historical `Extactly` typo.)
  * `LinkMaskNotFound` — a link column references a mask that
    does not exist on the linked objecttype.
  * `UnknownColumnType` — a column requests a type unknown to
    this fylr release (or to the loaded plugins).
  * `TypeChangeUnsupported` — an existing column's type
    cannot be changed to the requested one — the conversion
    path isn't implemented.
  * `InvalidMaskMode` — a mask declares an unknown `mode`
    value.
  * `InheritanceNotSupported` — a mask attempts to inherit
    from another mask in a way the datamodel does not allow
    (cross-objecttype inheritance, cyclic inheritance, ...).
  * `SchemaLoopingReverse` — a reverse-link column closes a
    loop the indexer cannot resolve.
  * `DatamodelVersionNotFound` — the requested datamodel
    version does not exist (for example
    `GET /schema/user/{version}` with an unknown or
    non-positive version).
  * `ObjecttypeNotEmpty` — a commit / HEAD write would delete
    an objecttype that still holds objects; `parameters` names
    the affected objecttypes.

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline (request parsing,
`ReadOnlyMode`, ...) may also surface here, and `package` is not
always `datamodel` (for example `ObjecttypeNotEmpty` is package
`api`, `ReadOnlyMode` is `ferrors`).

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`. (Not always `datamodel`
— pipeline errors carry their own origin, e.g. `api` or `ferrors`.)

example.error:
Objecttype "picture" is declared twice

Package: datamodel
Code: SchemaDuplicateObjecttypeName

MessageError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/message*` endpoints when the request
cannot be processed.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `StartTimeAfterEndTime` — the message's `start_time` is
    after its `end_time`; the validity window is empty.
  * `VersionMismatch` — on update, the supplied `_version` was
    not the stored version + 1.

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline (request parsing, ...)
may also surface here.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

example.error:
The given start time 2000-01-01 00:00:00 +0000 UTC is after the given end time 1999-01-01 00:00:00 +0000 UTC

Package: message
Code: StartTimeAfterEndTime

EventError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/event*` write endpoints when the request
cannot be processed.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code. The event-specific code is:

  * `EventTypeNotAllowed` — the `type` in the payload is not one of
    the user-event types that may be written through the API (i.e. it
    is not a FRONTEND/PLUGIN type and `skip_constraints` was not set
    by a root caller). `parameters.type` carries the offending value.

This is not a closed set: other `400` codes from the server pipeline
(e.g. JSON-decode or validation failures, the non-root
`skip_constraints` error) and the shared `401`/`403` codes can also
surface on these endpoints.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

example.error:
The event type OBJECT_INSERT is not allowed

Package: userevent
Code: EventTypeNotAllowed

ExportError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/export*` endpoints when the request
cannot be processed.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code raised by the export
package:

  * `ExportDuplicateName` — another export owned by the same
    user already uses the requested `name` (statuscode `409`).
    `parameters.name` carries the conflicting value.
  * `FileNotFound` — a download path addressed a file that
    does not exist in the export (statuscode `404`).
    `parameters.file` carries the requested path.
  * `FileNotDone` — the download was attempted while the
    file being exported is not yet in status `done`.
    `parameters.file` and `parameters.status` describe it.
  * `FileVersionNotAvailable` — a request pointed at a
    historic export run that has been cleaned up.
  * `FileGroupNotAvailable` — the export run's grouping
    structure no longer matches the requested path inside
    the archive.
  * `DeepLinkAccessByIdDisabled` — the export references
    objects by id and the instance has deep-link-by-id
    access disabled.
  * `DeepLinkAccessByColumnDisabled` — same but for
    deep-link-by-column access.
  * `WarningExportFile` — a non-fatal warning surfaced
    during a download — the response carries the message
    so the front end can show it next to the file.
  * `ApiObjectPath` — an invalid api-object path was
    requested inside an export archive.

Other 400 codes from the server pipeline may also surface here.
Most of these codes are `400`; `ExportDuplicateName` is `409`
and `FileNotFound` is `404` — see `statuscode`.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

example.error:
Export name "weekly-press-kit" already exists

Package: export
Code: ExportDuplicateName

PublishError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/publish*` write endpoints when a
publication cannot be created or updated.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code raised by the object /
publish pipeline:

  * `PublishUnknownCollector` — the `collector` named in the
    payload is not configured in this instance (checked first).
    The base config registers the collectors that are valid
    here. `parameters.collector` carries the offending value.
    Message: `The collector "<name>" unknown and cannot be used.
    It must be configured in the base config.`
  * `PublishInvalidData` — `publish_uri` or `easydb_uri` is
    missing or blank (the value is whitespace-trimmed and must
    be non-empty). `parameters.field` is `publish_uri` or
    `easydb_uri`; `parameters.value` is the empty string.
    Message: `The value "" of field "<field>" is invalid.`
  * `PublishDeletedObject` — the targeted object has been
    (soft-)deleted and cannot receive a new publication.
    `parameters.object` is the `#<system_object_id>` token.
    Message: `Unable to publish deleted object "<object>".`

A read-only instance is rejected before any of these checks with
`ReadOnlyMode` (`package: ferrors`), returned as the standard
`Error` envelope, not as a `PublishError`.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

example.error:
The collector "datacite" unknown and cannot be used. It must be configured in the base config.

Package: object
Code: PublishUnknownCollector

SuggestError.description:
Error returned by `/api/v1/suggest` when the request cannot be
processed.

code.description:
Stable, machine-readable error code:

  * `ServerGeneric` — a plain server-side error wrapped into the
    API envelope. `error` carries the underlying message. The
    suggest endpoint produces this for a malformed request:
    invalid JSON (in the request body, or in the `BODY` query
    parameter on the GET form), an objecttype in `objecttypes`
    or `linked_objecttypes` that does not exist (`Objecttype %q
    not found`), an unknown `tokens_mode` (`Unknown tokens_mode
    %q`), or an entry in `fields` that cannot be resolved.

error.description: Human-readable message, ending with a `Package` / `Code` footer.

package.description:
Internal origin marker for **fylr** support; not part of the API
contract. Branch on `code`, not `package`.

example.error:
Server error occurred: Unknown tokens_mode "fuzzy"

Package: ferrors
Code: ServerGeneric

LocaApi.description:
Datamodel translation document — a flat map of dotted **loca keys**
to per-language values (`LocaValue`).

The key set is generated from the datamodel — every objecttype,
mask, column, link and splitter contributes its own keys.
Reading clients should treat any key as opaque and look it up
by exact match. **`POST /l10n/user/HEAD` is a full replace**: any
generated key omitted from the body is cleared, so always send the
complete document (typically the body returned by
`GET /l10n/user/HEAD`). Unknown keys are ignored (not rejected);
language entries whose tag starts with `_` and empty-string values
are dropped on write.

Key patterns currently in use:

  * `schema.<objecttype>.name` — objecttype label.
  * `schema.<objecttype>.column.<column>` — column label.
  * `schema.<objecttype>.column.<other_objecttype>` — link-column label
    (`nested`).
  * `schema.<objecttype>.column.reverse:<other_objecttype>.<other_column_api_id>`
    — reverse-link column label.
  * `schema.<objecttype>.column.reverse:<objecttype>` — hierarchical
    reverse-link column label.
  * `mask.<objecttype_api_id>.<mask>.name` — mask label.
  * `mask.<objecttype_api_id>.<mask>.<other_objecttype_api_id>.field.<column_api_id>.{edit_user_hint|output_user_hint|search_user_hint}`
    — user-hint texts for a regular field.
  * `mask.<objecttype_api_id>.<mask>.<other_objecttype_api_id>.link.<column_api_id>.{edit_user_hint|output_user_hint|search_user_hint}`
    — user-hint texts for a link field.
  * `mask.<objecttype_api_id>.<mask>.<other_objecttype_api_id>.linked-table.<column_api_id>.{edit_user_hint|output_user_hint|search_user_hint}`
    — user-hint texts for a nested link.
  * `mask.<objecttype_api_id>.<mask>.<other_objecttype_api_id>.reverse-linked-table.<column_api_id>.{edit_user_hint|output_user_hint|search_user_hint}`
    — user-hint texts for a reverse link.
  * `mask.<objecttype_api_id>.<mask>.splitter.<index>` — splitter label.

RightsCatalog.description:
The **rights catalog** — the full inventory of right definitions,
capabilities and saved presets known to this fylr instance,
partitioned by context.

This is the response of `GET /right`. Clients use it to render
permission UIs (preset editors, ACL bag editors) and to discover
which rights are available in each context.

Plugins extend the catalog by contributing additional rights to
the `system` context (named `plugin.<plugin_name>.<right>`).

pool.description: Rights that apply to objects via pool ACLs.

object.description: Rights that apply to individual objects.

objecttype-without-pool.description:
Rights that apply to objecttypes that don't participate in pool
ACLs (objecttypes whose `acl: false`).

RightsContextInfo.description:
Per-context entry of the rights catalog — capabilities, the
right definitions available in this context, and (where the
context supports them) the saved presets.

rights.description:
The right definitions available in this context. Each entry
is either a regular right or a `choice` group whose `rights`
array carries the mutually-exclusive options.

presets.description:
Saved presets for this context, in display order. Only
populated for contexts that support presets (`object`,
`collection`).

RightsCapabilities.description:
Per-right capability flags for a context — which rights can be
marked `active`, which are eligible for a preset, which accept a
tag filter, which are considered structurally valid for the
context, and which are sticky (cannot be removed once granted).

Each map is keyed by right name; the value is currently a free-form
string describing the capability flavour (commonly `"true"` or a
policy keyword).

active.description: Rights that may be enabled (`active: true`) in this context.

preset.description: Rights that may appear in a preset for this context.

tagfilter.description: Rights that accept a tag filter (`_tagfilter`) in this context.

valid.description:
Rights that are structurally valid for this context. A grant
for a right not listed here is silently dropped by the ACL
pipeline.

sticky.description:
Sticky rights — once granted, the ACL cannot have them removed
by lower-privileged updates.

RightDef.description:
A **right definition** — one entry in the rights catalog. A
definition is either a regular right (`type` empty or `"right"`)
or a `choice` group whose `rights` array carries the
mutually-exclusive options the user picks one of.

type.description:
`""` and `"right"` mean a regular right. `"choice"` means a
group of mutually-exclusive sub-rights — the `rights` array
then carries the options.

name.description:
Stable, machine-readable right name. For choice groups, the
name is shared by the picker; for regular rights, it's the
identifier used in ACL grants.

group.description:
Logical grouping the UI uses to lay the right out next to
related rights.

has_grantable.description:
Whether this right may be marked grantable in an ACL grant
(the grantee can grant the same right onward).

parameters.description:
Parameters this right accepts in an ACL grant. Defaults are
filled in by the server; choice lists may be dynamic (e.g. the
list of EAS classes the instance has configured).

rights.description:
Sub-rights of a `choice` group — the mutually-exclusive
options the user picks one of. Only set when `type: choice`.

RightDefParam.description:
A parameter that an ACL grant may carry alongside a right —
typically a per-grant constraint like the list of mask names
the grant applies to, or a per-preset display setting.

name.description: The parameter's identifier.

type.description:
The parameter's data type. Common values:

  * `bool` — boolean.
  * `string` — single string.
  * `string-list` — list of strings.
  * `preview-versions` — list of `<class>.<version>` keys, with
    `choices` populated from the instance's EAS configuration.
  * `eas-class-list` — list of EAS class names, with `choices`
    populated from the instance's EAS configuration.
  * `mask-list` — list of mask names.

choices.description:
The closed list of allowed values (when applicable). For
dynamic types (`preview-versions`, `eas-class-list`), the
server fills this in from the instance configuration.

merge_policy.description:
How conflicting values from multiple grants should be combined
into one effective value. Currently `less` is supported for
`bool` parameters — the effective value is the AND of all
grants. Empty string means default merge.

FileStatBucketItem.description:
One leaf entry inside a `FileStatBucket.by_extension` /
`by_class` map — the per-extension or per-class slice of the
bucket's totals.

size.description: Sum of file sizes, in bytes.

FileStatBucket.description:
One status bucket of file statistics — the totals for one
lifecycle state (latest / history / deleted) of one storage
category (originals / versions). The bucket carries the
rolled-up `size` / `count` for the slice plus per-extension
and per-class breakdowns.

size.description: Sum of file sizes in this bucket, in bytes.

count.description: Number of files in this bucket.

by_extension.description:
Per-file-extension breakdown of the bucket. Key is the
lowercase extension without leading dot (e.g. `jpg`, `pdf`).
Omitted when the bucket is empty.

by_class.description:
Per-EAS-class breakdown of the bucket. Key is the EAS class
name (e.g. `image`, `document`, `audio`) as configured in
the base config. Omitted when the bucket is empty.

FileStatBuckets.description:
The three lifecycle buckets file statistics are sliced into —
files that are still the current version of their object, files
superseded by a newer version (history), and files belonging to
soft-deleted objects.

latest.description: Files attached to the current version of live objects.

history.description: Files attached to historic (non-current) versions of live objects.

deleted.description:
Files attached to soft-deleted objects (the object has a
non-NULL `deleted_at`).

FileStats.description:
Aggregated file statistics — the file counts and storage usage
rolled up across all objects in some scope (an objecttype, a pool,
a sub-tree, ...). The totals are sliced first by storage category
(uploaded originals vs. derived versions) and then by lifecycle
(latest / history / deleted).

count.description: Total file count across both originals and versions.

size.description: Total file size across both originals and versions, in bytes.

originals.description:
Statistics for **original** files — the file the user
uploaded.

versions.description:
Statistics for **derived** versions — previews, thumbnails,
converted formats produced from the originals.

ObjecttypeStats.description:
Asset statistics for one objecttype — the rolled-up file counts
and storage usage across every object of the type.

PoolStats.description:
Asset statistics for one pool — file counts and storage usage
for the objects in this pool (and, when the request set
`include_subpools=true`, the transitive sub-tree).

pool_id.description: The pool this stats document is for.

created_at.description:
RFC 3339 timestamp of when these stats were collected (for example
`2024-01-01T00:00:00Z`). The pool stats query is expensive, so the
result may be served from a cache window.

PluginApiGET.description:
Response of `GET /plugin` — the inventory of enabled plugins plus
the URLs of their concatenated front-end bundles.

bundle.description:
URLs of the concatenated JS / CSS / HTML / l10n bundles, in
load order. Omitted when bundling is disabled in the instance
configuration.

plugins.description:
One descriptor per enabled plugin, ordered as loaded. Disabled
plugins are not included.

PluginBundle.description:
URLs of the concatenated plugin front-end bundles. The endpoints
these point at (`/plugin/bundle.js`, `/plugin/bundle.css`,
`/plugin/bundle.html`, `/plugin/bundle/l10n/{lang}.json`) merge
the assets of every enabled plugin in load order.

javascript.description: URL of the merged JavaScript bundle.

css.description: URL of the merged CSS bundle.

html.description: URL of the merged HTML template bundle.

l10n.description:
Prefix URL of the merged l10n bundle, e.g.
`https://<host>/api/v1/plugin/bundle/l10n`. Clients append
`/<lang>.json` (for example `/de-DE.json`) to fetch
translations for a specific language.

PluginBase.description:
Descriptor of one plugin as exposed by `GET /plugin` — the
publicly-visible parts of the plugin's manifest plus the URLs
the front-end uses to fetch its assets.

name.description:
The plugin's unique identifier. Matches the directory name
in the plugins folder and the `name:` from the manifest.

version.description: Plugin version, as declared in the manifest.

l10n.description:
Path (relative to the plugin's static URL) of the plugin's
l10n CSV file.

require.description:
The minimum fylr server API version this plugin
supports.

url.description:
Optional homepage URL for the plugin (linked from the
admin UI).

info.description:
Localised long description. Defaults to `"-"` in the
instance default language when the manifest does not
provide one.

webfrontend.description:
Free-form metadata the plugin contributes to the front-end
(e.g. which routes / panels it registers). Shape is
plugin-specific.

custom.description:
Free-form `custom:` section from the manifest — published as-is
so the plugin's front-end code can read its own configuration.

base_url.description:
URL the plugin's static assets are served from (set during
the API request).

plugin_url.description:
URL of the plugin's own backend mount point (set during the
API request).

collection_upload.description:
Map of collection-upload integrations the plugin contributes
(key = integration name). Value is the plugin's configuration
definition for that integration.

PluginBuildInfo.description:
Build-time provenance of a plugin — repository, revision,
release tag, and dates. Populated from the plugin's
`build_info.json` (or equivalent) and included only when those
values are non-empty.

repository.description: Repository URL the plugin was built from.

rev.description: Git revision the build was made from.

release.description: Release tag of the build, if any.

lastchanged.description:
Last-changed timestamp of the build source (free-form text;
no fixed format).

builddate.description: Build timestamp (free-form text; no fixed format).

SystemStats.description:
System statistics — per-objecttype and per-basetype object counts,
index-queue depth, file processing queues, and per-index storage
stats. Returned by `GET /system/status`.

Build / version / capability information is **not** in this body
— it travels in the `x-fylr-status` response header, in the form
`<config>-<instance>-<version> <build_commit>` plus per-component
flags (use the inspect UI's status page for the rendered view).

indices_objects.description:
Per-index object counts, broken down by basetype/objecttype.
Outer key is the index name (`BaseRead`, `BaseWrite`,
`ObjectRead`, `ObjectWrite`); inner key is the term value
(`_basetype` / `_objecttype` / `_masks`).

total_not_indexed.description:
Sum of objects still waiting in the index queue across all
types.

pending_files.description:
Number of files in the "pending" pipeline state (waiting to
be processed by the execserver).

queued_files.description:
Number of files queued for processing — superset of
`pending_files` that includes retries.

file_stats.description: Counts of files grouped by processing status.

SystemTypeStats.description:
Aggregated counts for one objecttype or basetype — total stored
objects, index status counts, and per-file timing / size averages.
