total_count.description: Number of stored objects of this type.

indexed_count.description: Subset of `total_count` that is currently in the search index.

not_indexed_count.description: Subset of `total_count` still waiting in the index queue.

error_count.description: Objects of this type whose last indexing attempt errored.

avg_time.description: Average indexing time per object, in seconds.

avg_size.description: Average serialised size per object, in bytes.

SystemFileStat.description:
Count of files in one processing status. Used inside
`SystemStats.file_stats`.

status.description:
File processing status — one of the values fylr's file
pipeline assigns (e.g. `pending`, `queued`, `done`, `error`).

count.description: Number of files in this status.

SystemIndicesStats.description:
Storage size of one search index. Used inside
`SystemStats.indices_stats`.

name.description:
Logical index role (`BaseRead`, `BaseWrite`, `ObjectRead`,
`ObjectWrite`).

index.description: The physical index name this role currently resolves to.

size.description:
Storage size of the index, as reported by Elasticsearch
(human-readable string such as `42mb`).

type.description: The search domain, echoed from the request.

objecttypes.description: The object types that were searched.

count.description: Total number of objects matching the search.

offset.description: Offset of the returned page within the full result.

limit.description: Maximum number of objects in the returned page.

format.description: Render format of the objects in the response.

merge_linked_objects.description: The `merge_linked_objects` mode applied, echoed from the request.

merge_max_depth.description: The `merge_max_depth` applied, echoed from the request.

objects.description:
The page of matching objects. The shape of each item depends on the
search `type`: rendered objects for an object search, otherwise pools,
collections, events, messages or users/groups. Each object search hit
carries its relevance in `_score` (number): sort by `_score` to order
by relevance, raise it for selected search elements with their `boost`
parameter.

aggregations.description: The computed aggregations, keyed by the names used in the request.

point_in_time.description: The point-in-time context used or opened for this search.

SearchRequestSort.description: One sort criterion of a search.

field.description: Field to sort by. Use `_score` to sort by relevance.

language.description: For localized fields, the language to sort by.

with_path.description: Sort hierarchical fields by their full path.

mode.description: How to pick a sort value when the field has several values.

numeric.description: Sort the field numerically rather than as text.

number_groups.description: Number group boundaries used for numeric grouping.

width.description: For date fields, the bucket width to sort by.

SearchAggregation.description:
One aggregation computed over the search result. Aggregations are keyed by a
client-chosen name in the request and the response.

type.description:
Aggregation kind, for example a term aggregation, a date range
aggregation or a geo tile grid.

field.description: Field to aggregate over.

value_field.description: Field whose value is reported for each bucket.

objecttype.description: Restrict the aggregation to this object type.

limit.description: Maximum number of buckets to return.

format.description: Render format for objects reported in buckets.

include.description: Regular expression limiting which bucket keys are returned.

language.description: For localized fields, the language to aggregate.

filter_parent.description: Restrict buckets to children of this parent object.

ranges.description: Explicit ranges, for a date range aggregation.

bounds.description: Bounding box that limits a geo aggregation.

SearchRequestField.description:
Selects a field whose values are collected for each hit when the search
request uses `fields`.

key.description: Name this field appears under in the response.

field.description: The field to collect.

with_path.description: Collect the full path for hierarchical fields.

mode.description: How to pick a value when the field has several values.

language.description: For localized fields, the language to collect.

SearchParseOptions.description: Options for parsing a query string at `/search/parse`.

objecttypes.description: Restrict the search to these object types.

search.description:
If `true`, run the parsed search and return its result instead of the
parsed `SearchRequest`.

limit.description: Maximum number of objects to return.

offset.description: Number of objects to skip before the first returned object.

format.description: Render format for the objects in the result.

timezone.description: Timezone used when interpreting dates in the query string.

XmlMappingTagCatalog.description:
The metadata **tag catalog** returned by `GET /xmlmapping/tags`
with the default `exif_list` recipe — the JSON conversion of
`exiftool -f -listx`, produced by `fylr metadata list`.

tags.description: One entry per known tag, deduplicated by `<group>:<tag>` path.

xmlns.description:
XML namespace URI by tag group, following exiftool's
`http://ns.exiftool.ca/<family0>/<family1>/1.0/` convention.

exiftool_version.description: The version of the exiftool binary that produced the catalog.

SearchRequest.description:
Describes a search to run against **fylr**. The `search` property holds
the query; the remaining properties control paging, sorting, the response
format and aggregations.

type.description:
Domain to search. Omit (or use an empty string) to search indexed
objects. Each other domain has its own object shape in the response.

objecttypes.description:
Restrict the search to these object types. When empty, all object
types are searched.

search.description:
The query: a list of search elements combined into a single boolean
query.

offset.description: Number of hits to skip before the first returned object.

limit.description: Maximum number of objects to return.

format.description:
Render format for the objects in the response, for example `standard`
or `long`.

merge_linked_objects.description: Controls whether linked objects are embedded into the hits.

merge_max_depth.description:
Maximum depth up to which linked objects are embedded when
`merge_linked_objects` is used.

languages.description: Languages to load for localized fields.

sort.description: Sort order of the result. Criteria are applied in order.

include_deleted.description: If `true`, objects in the trash are included in the result.

aggregations.description:
Aggregations to compute over the result, keyed by a name chosen by the
client. The same names appear in the response.

fields.description:
Collect the values of specific fields for each hit, instead of (or in
addition to) returning whole objects.

field.description: Field name used when this request is a sub search.

include_fields.description: If set, only these fields are included in the returned objects.

exclude_fields.description: Fields to omit from the returned objects.

point_in_time.description:
Run the search against a point-in-time snapshot of the index, for
stable paging across requests. See `DELETE /search/point_in_time`.

keep_alive.description: How long the context is kept alive, for example `1m`.

search_after.description:
Cursor for deep pagination: the `sort` values of the last hit of the
previous page. Use instead of a large `offset`.

file_url_expire.description: Number of days the signed file URLs in the response stay valid.

timezone.description: Timezone used when interpreting and bucketing dates.

generate_rights.description: If `true`, the effective rights are generated for each hit.

best_mask_filter.description: If `true`, each object is reduced to the best mask the user may see.

SearchObject.description:
One element of a search query. The `type` selects how the element matches;
which of the other properties apply depends on that `type`.

type.description:
Kind of match:
  * `text`: full-text match of `string` / `query` against `fields`.
  * `match`: like `text`, kept for compatibility.
  * `in`: match a field against a set of values, an object type or a
    `subsearch`.
  * `range`: match a field against a `from` / `to` range.
  * `changelog_range`: match objects by their changelog entries.
  * `complex`: group nested elements in `search` as a boolean query.
  * `nested`: like `complex`, but matches within the nested `path`.
  * `geo_bounding_box`: match a geo field inside a bounding box.
  * `geo_shape`: match a geo field against a shape.

bool.description:
How this element combines with the other elements of the same `search`
list. Defaults to `must`.

boost.description:
Relative weight of this element: a higher boost results in a higher
`_score` for objects matching it (useful with `bool: should` and
sorting by `_score`). Applies to every `type`, must not be negative,
has no effect on `must_not` elements. Defaults to `1`.

mode.description: Match mode for `text`, for example how tokens are combined.

languages.description: Restrict matching of localized fields to these languages.

in.description: For `in`, the set of values to match against.

objecttype.description:
For `in`: match linked objects of this object type. Use `_pool` to
match against pools.

include_path.description:
For `in` with `objecttype`: also match objects located on the path of
a hierarchical object type.

field.description: Single field, for `range` / `changelog_range`.

from_equals.description: Whether the `from` bound is inclusive. Defaults to `true`.

to_equals.description: Whether the `to` bound is inclusive. Defaults to `true`.

path.description: For `nested`, the nested field path the sub query runs in.

operation.description: For `changelog_range`, the changelog operation to match.

comment.description: For `changelog_range`, the changelog comment to match.

user.description: For `changelog_range`, ID of the user who made the change.

geo_bounding_box.description:
Bounding box for `type: geo_bounding_box`. Only this property and the
companion `field` are used by that type; `field` is REQUIRED and must
resolve to a geo field (for example `_standard.geo.1`). The box is
forwarded to an OpenSearch `geo_bounding_box` query.

Provide the two corners in exactly ONE of two mutually exclusive forms:
  * point form: `top_left` / `bottom_right` as `{ lat, lon }` objects.
  * geohash form: `top_left` / `bottom_right` as geohash strings.
When decoding, the geohash (string) form is attempted first; if that
fails the point (object) form is used. Both corners must use the same
form.

Requires a license with geo support; otherwise the search is rejected.

top_left.description:
Top-left corner of the box: either a `{ lat, lon }` point or a
geohash string. Must use the same form as `bottom_right`.

bottom_right.description:
Bottom-right corner of the box: either a `{ lat, lon }` point or a
geohash string. Must use the same form as `top_left`.

geo_shape.description:
Polygon for `type: geo_shape`. Only this property and the companion
`field` are used by that type; `field` is REQUIRED and must resolve to
a geo field (for example `_standard.geo.1`).

The only input is `points`, the polygon vertices. fylr builds the
GeoJSON polygon itself and queries OpenSearch with a FIXED spatial
relation of `within` (the relation is not configurable, and a raw
GeoJSON `{ type, coordinates }` body is NOT accepted here). The ring is
closed automatically by repeating the first vertex, so the first and
last vertex must not be duplicated in the request.

Requires a license with geo support; otherwise the search is rejected.

points.description:
Vertices of the polygon, each a `{ lat, lon }` point. At least 3
vertices are required (fewer is rejected). Do not repeat the first
vertex; the polygon is closed automatically.

ExportApiList.description:
A page of the authenticated user's **exports**, as returned by
`GET /api/v1/export`. `count` is the total number of exports
matching the filter, independent of `limit` / `offset`.

limit.description: The `limit` that was applied to this page.

offset.description: The `offset` that was applied to this page.

count.description:
Total number of exports matching the filter, ignoring
`limit` / `offset`.

objects.description: The exports on this page, newest first.

EventBackgroundOrApi.description:
Response of `POST /event`. For a synchronous write this is the stored
event (`EventApi`). With `background=true` the body is the acknowledgement
object `{"background": "ok"}` and the event is saved asynchronously.

EventListBackgroundOrApi.description:
Response of `POST /event/list`. For a synchronous write this is the array
of stored events (`EventApi`). With `background=true` the body is the
acknowledgement object `{"background": "ok"}` and the events are saved
asynchronously.

position.description: Position of the parameter in the output.

type.description: type of the parameter

name.description: name of the parameter as used in the API

ConfigParamEmail.description: **email** is an email address consisting of loca part and domain name.

ConfigParamFile.description: **file** is an eas file managed by fylr.

_id.description: An eas file id from fylr.

ConfigParamIntList.description: **int-list** is a plain list of integers, e.g. system object ids.

fields.description: Items of this array are of schema ConfigParam

ConfigParamForm.description: **form** is one row of nested parameters.

ConfigParamJson.description: The value of this type is and abritrary JSON object.

plugin_type.description: For some JSON, we support predefined schema.

ConfigParamLocationSelect.description: This presents a location select to the user.

default.description: Location is a name of a configured location

ConfigParamSecret.description: **secret** is string which will be stored encrypted in the database, if `fylr.encryptionKey` is set.

ConfigParamSelect.description: **select** is a choice select for one out of a list of options

options.description: The list of options available for choice.

ConfigParamStringList.description: **string-list** is a list of choices presented to the user.

choices.description: The list of options available for choice.

ConfigParamStringListSort.description: **string-list-sort** is a list of sortable choices presented to the user.

choices.description: The list of options available for choice.

ConfigParamTagSelect.description: **tag-select** is a list of tag ids, presented as a tag select to the user.

ConfigParamTagfilterSelect.description: This presents a tagfilter select to the user.

default.description: Tagfilter is a map of sets of ids, as used in the fylr API.

ConfigParamTextMultiline.description: **text-multiline** is like `text` but displayed in a mutliline form.

ConfigParamTextLocaMultiline.description: **text-l10n-multinline** is like `text-l10n` but displayed in multiline form.
